# Wednesday, June 4, 2025
---|---
**Good Morning!** On this day in 1989, the Chinese government violently suppressed pro-democracy protests in [Beijing's Tiananmen Square](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL2O4yAURp8Gd0TXQLBTUGzjbrbd0mLgMrk7_ERwYytvv8q-wvSfPp1z_nzsH54y1a_fvqDj1PaK58jIjH0fpX1juLdvHIXiiYMfyKEN3hWoqwQrwezRU37tZoouISrUE7p5MTfQs9F6urtkLdjlppSeA1pYVmuiXtYUlbmB1ziRe7-BBTMD6Ot8WdfoNVzTnAKsWithACNx65e_jSrfMeVXO7BfQitTdnfmxxD6l1CbUNt5npeIR6QR2rMPfI-E2nxnChmF2h4tE1MYQm3aGKtglm9HHLIlyeSrrwWr9JI7fT4ZJTf52f2B_SV9oThYDqpN-oKdgq-SsQ5qdUzdxcdJOdOwizDwVTzl_4z8o7CHU_8CAAD__3j2jwk), resulting in the deaths of hundreds, possibly thousands, of unarmed civilians. The event remains a highly sensitive and censored topic in China. Yesterday’s question about your earliest memory sparked a flood of responses—thank you to everyone who wrote in. One reader says they remember their own birth (yes, including the birth canal). Another shared a cinematic tale involving a crazed horse, a baby carriage, and a narrow escape through a paint store in 1940s Brooklyn. We love having these conversations with you. Thanks again to the hundreds who responded. **Starting tomorrow:** We’re adding a new section to the newsletter—**Podcast Extra** —spotlighting important stories not covered in our news publication. Additional headlines, politics, business, sports—all will be available exclusively on _The Flyover Podcast_. To enjoy these stories, tune in to tomorrow's episode. And if you aren't a podcast listener yet, [**CLICK HERE**](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkD1u6zAQhE9DdRKWu9RfweI16vzalAJNrizGlCmIGxu-feAggA-QdmbwDfB9nOaTiyneLv_dxlaWPN_4URKL8DGXLV_Zr_nKZYvhwUV2Fp-LzAjY1tDVYObgYnrOpgp2YUamiq3uzQikDVG1WjyP4Qx967FnbHsm3SGbzrWjHzyd-yraFw06MBqAWt0MQ3AE7aIXDwMRKgMcouSj-czxJisv6ZnvfDQ-b1Wyq8heFP1TOCmc9hy8K1Iat--JXxOF01d5NwonWbn-ZdTvNAY9aEDCsdPVYcP-iCnF0vXKwGVzMf38yZ8k3S1-BwAA__8tJXqP) and check out today's episode. ## Midwest, Southeast Choke on Smoke and Dust
Hundreds of thousands were told to [stay indoors Tuesday as smoke](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkbGO2zAQRL-G6miQXFqiChUGDkp1qQKkFHja5YmxRCrk2oL_PpB_IeW8wQwwmN-f06ePa0zfP_1GA4c8JTrqSsxUprrlO81LvlPdIh5UeSeec-XJKHOVqpXKTujj-ppsg0MgMgQNDbqzvQJtAZplCApQW--0bk0_e4LOhF6rPjjz5XRrmzicbapVVisFV31xDj2oa9BhVg7ACKsII-dy-ZNj4oXC-spPKpc5b806LMx7FXATZhRmPI7jstV0esKMlOSjnpQ8L1SEGTnvlXOJdOLlkbAQVpmD5CU_qk9YJecVJWdZ2b9kTJhzqTImCSfhd9AXebvpH-qv-SVgzHNEAR_LvtUkTDs_37JXHQYPGhT1tnXYB4dOO61tj18WQZiWooCPzjVlwP2I6xpr2wmrvjcf1_c6_q9LnoP5FwAA__9S651g) from over 200 Canadian wildfires pushed air quality to “very unhealthy” levels across Minnesota, Iowa, and Wisconsin. Additionally, a massive [Saharan dust plume](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkDuO6zAMRVdjdzJoffwpVLwmXV47paFYlKVEtgKLjia7H3iyhOlIgrg493xdp6sJMWzLf7OiJpemDUuOSIT7lNf0wNmnB-Y12IKZnkhzyjRx4IpBx0BO1oT4nmRttUPkKGrUbS9HEK0UovZ6hNHeFPBZjb26GSl60Xfc2b5Tyg6IddBnGnQgWwCh2mYYrBGgXOtmGITglQS0gdLe3FPYyKOL7_TCvZnTWkftiZ65Ev8qfqn4pZTSuPRd0JD_vJzHz8bObhW_ZOPNbjZmj0wsp4M8mkxsOaJjczrH-7EhO7EqLpaUlojTKyzbqaXetX2WEGPIXV9JWFYT4i8K_cnfS_OfAAAA___IMoak) is expected to reach Florida and the Gulf Coast by Thursday, bringing hazy skies, reduced visibility, and possible “dirty rain” through the weekend. The dust, driven by dry winds from Africa, can suppress tropical storms but may coat surfaces when mixed with rainfall. Over 17,000 people have been [evacuated in Canada](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGOnDAQhp_GdF6ZMexyBcVGEd2lSRGlQrP2cEzOYOIZFu3bR-QVrv1-6den79f7-I6ceP34gQv1OuVxpUMSqVIZZcmfFOb8SbJwPEh0Iw1ZdAQHrXVX65oxIqfX2FSxn4iAfEV9fWvenK8b76u5n94e4K9120KMj1uArouBHN5cB56Auor7881dXVM759v60nURvWunegqu8x5M4yiy5nL5k3nVmab0yk8ql5CXKvWz6ibG3w0MBobjOC6LrOdmYKDV7nJSQp2pGBg0b6K5MJ14t2KRi_27Y2J92ZnVolhwjT04xYkLiX3sZbUYShaxAVeMaGDAYu_3evgdFY0fcuBo_PdvZ0c65CeVrSp93A5OieV6M437WJDTf2P9UuZnD_8CAAD__0-1krU), where U.S. firefighters have been deployed to assist.
## Ukraine Hits Crimea Bridge
Ukraine damaged the [Kerch Bridge linking Russia to Crimea](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0LFuwyAQxvGnwVus84EdMzB08ZauHS0CR0yDQwSnoPTpK_cVOn_ST_r-X5f1YmOKj9un3clwyOuDWk3ETGWte76T2_Kd6h59o8pPYpcrrwg4nmA6gVq9jem9qs6bQIQkOzLDWWmQg5Ky24zVapaWMNBkndMolVNuVDqgP2uvQxfNocEEagCQ49DPs7cSxjAEB7OUKBSQj5xL_53jgzcK6Z1fVHqX9y6ZjflZhfwQuAhcWmv99eqOTeBynBG4pPgigYsr40z6XZB-uCvGP1tMKdbpLBTcdhvTn8j_yvAy-BsAAP__CWVxMw) with underwater explosives early Tuesday, temporarily halting traffic on the key military supply route. The Security Service of Ukraine said 2,400 pounds of TNT were used in the months-long operation targeting the bridge’s foundations. [Watch a video here](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0L1uwyAUxfGnwVusCxd_ZGCo1GZLl1aqOlnEXGIabCJzE-S3r9xX6HqO9Bv-X-fhbEMMy_XdzmTYp2GhkiMx0zrkOd1onNKN8hxcocx34jFlHhSo5gDtAfTgbIjboCtnPJEirMjITh8BpUasJnN0srfoqZMoj0pTZxuPF6V76hy0rqmC2TVoQUsAbGTd984iNF76EXpEJTSQC5zW-ieFhSfycUtPWusxzVU0E_M9C3wR6iTUqZRSb-nBjwvt_75YHieBp6fA1_ntkpbrh_rcvqvVuHsJMYbcdkLDdbYh_pH8rw5Po34DAAD__yN0cVA). Satellite imagery confirmed that a weekend drone strike destroyed or damaged [several long-range Russian bombers](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkMGumzAQRb_G7BwZ2wm8BYtUFZVapZsuukQDHsI0xkaeyUP8fUV_oet7daRzfj-GB1Ck9PwJK3Yy5yHhzhFFsAy85hdOS34hrxR2ZNlQpswyWGOv2ty08UMAisfgq9DNiBZdhV3d-A_jau9ctXStHRtTm_Gj9u00A7bu2gSHN-ebdnQ4VdSdNHMzvjbGXetL2wZw5jrX82Ra56zyBgNJLpc_mZIsOMcjf2K5THmtYreIbKzcXdle2X7f98vK6dyU7THpNyvbn1LnmEsMyvaQIB5MrBkEYyRBTSs8sRyal7yzfr8KUEINIjC9dECWkg8MGlLQAc5v0OXNTJD0mNcRy8mHou_3-pv58fiuXJ8nCsp9_XLWxZ1_Ydmq0oVtpxiJb43y5rkCxX8e8l_xPzv7NwAA__8X7JvP) used in missile attacks on Ukraine, in what some have called a “Russian Pearl Harbor.” Meanwhile, Russian [officials launched a manhunt](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkD2u2zAQhE9DdTT4J1kuWKRR99KmFNbi8mkjihTItQXfPlCKXCDNFDPAh5n59TV_ASXK3z9hR8-xzBnPlpAZ69z2suGylg3bTuHExgfyUhrPRpleqkEqNweg9JldF3xENGg79PruHspqZ223-j7en-oRjHrYIYxOW9Cht314LkYPvYkd-YumBuW0UrbXt3EMYFUfdVzUaK0RTmEgLvX2u1DmFWP6lDfW21L2LvmV-WjC_hBmEmbKn6M0viJhpgsszKSGS6ww01lqCvKaKMz02ipQJshNQmXcJdNeIuJbQg4SN2CslOGfDTJR3jBILrK-WiPI8kCoSa5Qn6UKM3XVh-OklKgNd-HU9w6U_vbk_zr37c2fAAAA___Klo8Q) for Artem Timofeev, a former Ukrainian DJ who runs a trucking company, and his wife, Ekaterina Timofeeva, an erotic novelist, accusing them of helping organize the drone attacks.
## Pet Owners Feel the Bite of Rising Costs
The [cost of owning a dog in the U.S.](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0L1urDAUBOCnMR3Ifxhu4eI2dJs2JTL2YTlZg5F9shZvH5FXSD-a0Tefj_nhMOLx_HA7WFrTfEAtEYggz2VPL_BbekHZMVQodAL5VGiWXPYtNy3Xc3AYr1k3wa4AElQDVgz6H1dCK9VsViwwjNoM2rhlXHwvvJIDyH5YpBmVMw3au40brgXnqhfdOAaneL-K1fNRKck0h4CUcveV8KAN1nilN-TOp72JdiM6C1P_mZyYnGqt3RsIMh4uX2d2ntDDjbrjTE6pHpBL-30EyFAId0fQepehvWVMTk224awYIxYzMM2fu8P4u0V_Ouht5U8AAAD__4rtfCY) now ranges from $22,125 to $60,602 over 15 years, according to a new study, while cat care runs $20,073 to $47,106. Veterinary costs are up 42% since 2019, with over half of pet owners reporting they’ve skipped or declined care due to cost—14% said their pet’s condition worsened or died as a result. Here’s a [breakdown of overall costs](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkD1uwzAUg09jbzaefuJYg4Yu3tK1o6FIT_ZLZMmwlAi5fZG2N-hIEiDB7-syXwwFisun2VAXn-aINQcsBY85b-mOdk13zBu5irnsWGzKZebATx0MHcjZGQqvWbZOe0SOokXNzlKBYFKIdtV-FAwZU2YA5a9K2evZGev5qEANSqmW9LsNBpAMQJxYP47OCDh55i2MQvBGAjoq6ehviWJZ0YdXeuLR27S1Qa-l7LkRHw2fGj7VWvuab--s4dP18crksOHTjkdO0YTOUzTR_lqlo5gfx59-P-uS71KNFJfOpaU9tNsrhUB5ODcSls1Q-Jkt_2L11Pw7AAD__4SJfzY). Only 23% recall being offered payment plans, and 73% say vets didn’t present cheaper alternatives. Smaller pets remain far less costly: a hamster’s lifetime care totals under $1,000, and even rabbits typically cost less than $6,000 over 10 years. See the [full study in PDF here](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0LFuwjAUheGnSTaQY18TM3ioVLHRtWPkOsfkFhtH8RWIt6_gFTqfo2_4v8_TOXDm2-UrFHhJdbrh0TJEsE2t1CviUq9ohecHmqyQWJtMWmm7U4edomkOnJ8T9bNPgIbp4YeRjsoMZEy_eAdLQSdEstGNZOM4kv0hB33UdoTr2b80dVA0KGXssHduDkbZNKSonDG6I4WZpW7738o3WZDys96x7WMtffaLyNo689HpU6dPKyRzgnBBTTFseOGv53s2a7igM59Dv_l5fXDO3A5jR-pSAuc3KP-qcPf6LwAA___Vh2-P).

On this day in 1989, the Chinese government violently suppressed pro-democracy protests in , resulting in the deaths of hundreds, possibly thousands, of unarmed civilians. The event remains a highly sensitive and censored topic in China. Yesterday’s question about your earliest memory sparked a flood of responses—thank you to everyone who wrote in. Thanks again to the hundreds who responded. Starting tomorrow: We’re adding a new section to the newsletter—Podcast Extra —spotlighting important stories not covered in our news publication. Additionally, a massive is expected to reach Florida and the Gulf Coast by Thursday, bringing hazy skies, reduced visibility, and possible “dirty rain” through the weekend. The dust, driven by dry winds from Africa, can suppress tropical storms but may coat surfaces when mixed with rainfall. Over 17,000 people have been , where U.S. firefighters have been deployed to assist. Ukraine Hits Crimea Bridge Ukraine damaged the with underwater explosives early Tuesday, temporarily halting traffic on the key military supply route. The Security Service of Ukraine said 2,400 pounds of TNT were used in the months-long operation targeting the bridge’s foundations. . Satellite imagery confirmed that a weekend drone strike destroyed or damaged used in missile attacks on Ukraine, in what some have called a “Russian Pearl Harbor.” Meanwhile, Russian for Artem Timofeev, a former Ukrainian DJ who runs a trucking company, and his wife, Ekaterina Timofeeva, an erotic novelist, accusing them of helping organize the drone attacks. Pet Owners Feel the Bite of Rising Costs The now ranges from $22,125 to $60,602 over 15 years, according to a new study, while cat care runs $20,073 to $47,106. Veterinary costs are up 42% since 2019, with over half of pet owners reporting they’ve skipped or declined care due to cost—14% said their pet’s condition worsened or died as a result. Here’s a . Only 23% recall being offered payment plans, and 73% say vets didn’t present cheaper alternatives. Smaller pets remain far less costly: a hamster’s lifetime care totals under $1,000, and even rabbits typically cost less than $6,000 over 10 years. See the . | | "Tax cuts for the rich" is a favorite argument of those who oppose lower tax rates. But does imposing higher tax rates on wealthy Americans actually increase the amount of revenue coming into the federal government? Hoover Institution Senior Fellow **Thomas Sowell takes an empirical approach** in his commentary, [**_"_****_Trickle Down " Theory and "Tax Cuts for the Rich._****_"_**](https://post.spmailtechnol.com/f/a/N9BFHUbuPy66fR2oSrtg8A~~/AARclBA~/bDYwl2fHRsQojWGYnuTbE0zMgKusEz6tQhhn4V1RNJp2v2Bv4zfyRQAGYXRb3gtAI7uCtD2uzQcRmmCQDWr4CNDS07rS77T3m_ETHymsHMPQdQpNtm66X4HClFMozaPR0QnUqWsErqV8HUS4fPjbNj594_RrXagXuIajYjrFnrDxS-4CH9IZe0nQklMWDvwTsoMCiMzEc1FOloB3hj_YR01KCwLXqs-Vv42vMx_DV4cOGdKt7hKSUtWVlkEKrFjJV6L2D264rmqXc4u3Mt74hw~~) Sowell separates fact from fiction when it comes to tax rates and uses real life data and examples to prove that lower tax rates for all actually lead to greater revenue for the government. As a friend and supporter of the Hoover Institution, we are proud to offer you this valuable, thought-provoking resource free of charge. [**Click here to get your free copy of**** _ "Trickle Down" Theory and "Tax Cuts for the Rich" _****now.**](https://post.spmailtechnol.com/f/a/N9BFHUbuPy66fR2oSrtg8A~~/AARclBA~/bDYwl2fHRsQojWGYnuTbE0zMgKusEz6tQhhn4V1RNJp2v2Bv4zfyRQAGYXRb3gtAI7uCtD2uzQcRmmCQDWr4CNDS07rS77T3m_ETHymsHMPQdQpNtm66X4HClFMozaPR0QnUqWsErqV8HUS4fPjbNj594_RrXagXuIajYjrFnrDxS-4CH9IZe0nQklMWDvwTsoMCiMzEc1FOloB3hj_YR01KCwLXqs-Vv42vMx_DV4cOGdKt7hKSUtWVlkEKrFjJV6L2D264rmqXc4u3Mt74hw~~) Thanks, Greg Greg Stamps | _Online Development_ Hoover Institution | Stanford University

Will you add your name telling the Senate to BLOCK the "Big Beautiful Bill" that would gut healthcare and life-saving services for millions of Americans? We need your signature by midnight tonight!!](https://default.salsalabs.org/T4ef2f29b-2312-43aa-9058-9885baa1663b/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)

| Trump and his MAGA cronies are THRILLED that they are one step closer to ending health care coverage and food assistance programs to MILLIONS of Americans. But let's call this "Big Beautiful Bill" what it really is, Peter: A tax break for Trump's billionaire buddies. That's right, you read that correctly – Trump and his MAGA cronies are willing to put millions of elderly, low income parents, disabled folks, and more at risk of losing life saving services so a couple of their rich friends can get a tax cut. Just take a look: [](https://default.salsalabs.org/T5caf8e28-aa7c-4132-8c2b-0ff8d80eb32b/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) If we don't act NOW, the Senate could pass this DANGEROUS bill and leave millions without the life-saving services they so desperately need. We cannot let MAGA Republicans hurt working families by passing the "Big Beautiful Bill" so their billionaire pals can get a tax break. And the Senate is our last hope to STOP this bill. [Please we need 10,000 signatures from 22122 to tell the Senate to vote "NO" on this bill and protect Medicaid and food assistance programs! Will you sign on by midnight tonight?](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tb8e38cdb-2e12-4e5a-b199-e54089d0f0c4/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)

| | | About Blue Amp Action: We cannot sugarcoat the current situation. Right-wing authoritarianism, defeated by a worldwide coalition during WWII, was only in hibernation. Over the past decade it has infected democratic societies--including ours. It is as simple as it is terrifying: What we do now will define the future of our country and world. We at Blue Amp Action are dedicated to liberal, open societies, where reason and science are applied to solve our problems; where civil rights--including voting rights!--are protected for all. We fight for these values every day. Speaking up on our platforms, raising the voices and the political prospects of liberal pols and pundits. **And savvy grassroots activists like you know, just like we do, that we can’t stop watching the people we help elect:_ after the elections, we have to keep organizing support for the policies we like and pushing back against the ones we don’t._** **__When the public tunes out between elections and the only people our elected officials hear from are the paid lobbyists of wealthy corporations, our government stops being accountable to the needs of ordinary families.__** **Democracy never sleeps, and neither can we.** So we ask that you support our work, so we can continue to engage in this fight every moment of every day. So our kids grow up in democracy, not dystopia. Thank you for your support for all we do! — _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. subject: Flipping and Tripping, the House Votes by a Margin of One to Legalize Magic Mushrooms
body:
Here's the latest breaking news at The Maine Wire

** Flipping and Tripping, the House Votes by a Margin of One to Legalize Magic Mushrooms (https://mainepolicy.us9.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a975bf67c40a8d8be91ad606d&id=37a7f07030&e=f093fa355c)
------------------------------------------------------------
By John Andrews on Jun 03, 2025 03:50 pm
Maine’s House of Representatives narrowly voted to decriminalize the possession of one ounce or less of psilocybin for persons 21 years of age or older on Monday evening. Better known as the active agent in psychedelic mushrooms, psilocybin has been argued in recent years to have therapeutic applications in mental health treatment. The fate of [...]

Early frontrunners in the 2026 Maine gubernatorial race suggest the field is still just beginning to develop on both the Republican and Democrat sides as a recent poll showed the two leading candidates for the GOP nomination haven’t even declared their candidacy while the one pegged at the lead of the Democrat pack may have [...]

------------------------------------------------------------
By Seamus Othot on Jun 03, 2025 01:08 pm
It may be a sign of the times that both chambers of the Maine Legislature passed a bill on Monday allowing a limited amount of home distilling of spirits for personal consumption, legalizing it at the state level while it remains illegal federally. By John Andrews on Jun 03, 2025 12:05 pm
Tensions flared in the Maine House chamber Monday night as Republican lawmakers staged a walkout in protest over extended legislative work past 9 p.m. The dispute, centered around procedural disagreements and concerns over long work hours, led to a rare show of defiance from GOP members, who accused Democratic leaders of broken agreements and partisan [...]

Flight Deck Brewing in Brunswick is under investigation by the U.S. Department of Labor’s (DOL) Wage and Hour Division for potential violations of the Fair Labor Standards Act (FLSA), the federal government agency confirmed. A DOL spokesperson confirmed that the investigation into the local brewery is open but did not elaborate on the details of [...]

body:
This year’s Women’s College World Series final features an [all-Texas showdown](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL2u4yAQhZ_G7sYCjP8KF9uku9tuaREYwmjHYAEKN2-_Sop9gduc6uj8fH--ji9DTPHx25y412-fjoitMNaK-TDMFb9NabaViO00VzHRaT0eSqgJxAxCH84Qvw7du_0u3CZ73OWiNzHqTcg-7GrbZlycX1avptnOSuFq9bJu26rcau897e8wMQstpZDjMvht8evdSTNOcvPad1qgo5ryUAN-9nh-pSfmwaaz5z3UepVu_NWpW6du5Uq5luFlQkpvQ6duJleyjJ26tXRiLGATMz4QWsrsoGAmLOApGoYr45OwQUgNPl3_1QawJkKjCDUgZfCUS4WSfL0bZrDBnBelWAJdUAMVaIh_Qc6TnGY5zkOoJ_d5d1cjZirz0mnxOA3x50f9Gf3nrv4FAAD__7uGnQc) between No. 6 Texas and No. 12 Texas Tech. Both teams are vying for their first NCAA softball championship in a best-of-three series starting tonight.

## Missing World War II Pilot Identified
The U.S. Department of Defense announced Monday that the remains of First Lieutenant Charles W. McCook, a 23-year-old Georgetown native who was killed during World War II, had been [identified earlier this year](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0Luu4yAUheGnwR0WN9vHBUWkkaUpTpopprR2YJOg4WIBCcnbj5JXOO0qlj79f7_3b_DBp-sZIur2dHlP2GvA1rDsEELDJ9Ruek3YIxwVklVK7oKJibKZMrVb8OG1q8HqC7MrH1DzRa1MqpXx4aYtuEkyp-zXJKdF2sXARRrJ2TpPzK1y8Pp9xmamOGdcLqNbF_d1sRzkxFenHFEMrW-5jO2GH48Lr_zAMpoch6BvrR2VyBMRGxFb7320B8AYfSBiO2Ov9E_LxWMlYvv9i55SyvdkMGJq7-lUmjcBidiUYFJxRcR2-JAbBWPyPTW01OVCXcmR9lyCpR0K9Z5GY3L-Rw0R21C0PboPwdd5IYpdI_jw8bWfVX1o8T8AAP__B7SLUQ). McCook, a pilot in the Army Air Forces, died when his B-25 crashed during a [low-altitude bombing raid in Burma](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL2y4yAUg5_G7vAcbPxDQbFNumy7pecEDjazGDxAQvL2d5xXuLU00if9u693dN6F7S8epMrbxjVQzZ5KobSi94XemKuuOVA98MwYjBDD2kM_MpgYiNWg859VtEY9wEjekuKzkDAICbzd1TJKmICQDKehXyRIjstgZ3hMfBk1tU5dYTCB4Bz4MHdWznZ5GI7DyKUVthFAxpWYurLTl8f6T3xR6nQ8Wq_2Us7cDH-a_tb0t1prZ-N7xmcuLlyWpr9dmy4tJm9YxcScY6fzsTC9Y_KUWWWH1jH-Z6h1fIZChtmY2EYxbVRiDezb3CZlzuq8d3maGwHbgc5_Ocrv3nup_icAAP__ybyGAQ). Two of the six crew members were captured by Japanese forces, while the remaining four perished and were declared missing in action. Their remains were [recovered in 1947](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkD2S3CAQhU-DMqYA9egnIHCibJ06VPVAs-oyginAy87tXZorbNJR1_e-9_587B_IkdPnbzzJtu-Q90S9RmqNyo4xNvrG2l2vifqJz4rJA4y7UeYu1SQV7B45vnYYvH0ov-qBrJ5hVSOsSg-H1euyzOaxLA5AwV0HCOo-a_QLjJNxNLC9YGpSoLXS43wL6xyWh9c43vUaIAhQ5LnlcmsHvX1CfOUvKjeXzyHao7VnFeMvYTZhtt77zUV2f82R_9WW0_UlzHbVEma7soTZ1HQdI8xW6EROVeYg32zZc4lediySWSKXE5P05CIW8vJklBgaFalXGOUzYiLpCtZDsqfUODB5YbahWP_sHCPXaRagPk_k-PZtP1v5y5r_AQAA___ET4_l) in a common grave near a Burmese village. They were unidentified at the time and were interred as "unknowns" in the National Memorial Cemetery of the Pacific in Honolulu. McCook’s remains were exhumed in 2022 and identified using dental, anthropological, isotope, and mitochondrial DNA analysis. He will be buried in Georgetown in August, and a [rosette will mark his name](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL2uozAQRp8GOkfjP4gLF9vQ3W23RIM9TkZrMAJvfPP2q_AKtzoaHWl09P35mr-QM2-P37iSr9-pzBu1M1OtdMyYc6VvPFto50Ztxf3ELRqjZwXKChgEmDki5_ds-ugXiE725OVoHGjjQPZPj5ZSlFEGHJY4yEGjsZjU6ACCGezQs_88gwGMlCD1eEtuTPclStRWumRSZ4Ai13Lc6pOunpTf5UXHLZS1z_5Z6352-lenpk5NrbXb30BbfX10pyY8KodMnZoypw92Kvt1t8Ysds6ligeV40G1tE1wpK1yYopi-Xd88CzrpTo1WQCBJO8uGiu0XZwwEkDcSVoRNFqtLY7hLvvDx71xznwOY2fgsSLnq7f-bOWXV_8DAAD__wqnj1s) on the Walls of the Missing at the Manila American Cemetery to indicate he has been accounted for.
## Legislature Restricts Use of Cellphones, Food Stamps
Bills passed during this year’s legislative session would add Texas to the growing number of states that have banned student cellphones from public schools and prohibited the purchase of junk food using food stamps. [House Bill 1481](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkDuy2zAMRVdDdfDwZyksVKRR99Km1PADiZwHkxqRNp93n5G3kAYVDnDu_fu1ftlEKe9_7APn9rOVNWOvhK3huVqihj-2dt9rxv6wR7U5aK1WyeUd-Ahcr8Emeq96CLPjwYgBZzFpw5U2XAxx1sYpPimxSbMpJ9AgN2qcvLxPdvR2HNJ8HeMj10JwoabbZqbtlwvCqrswm96Y5hhSK-etRfz4bPQuLzxvvjwGmmNrR2XqN5MLk0vv_eYp-W8Zy7O2kq8tJpcrFpPLh2dyuX4yufBrKMHk4hIRtGgb9PKkAM5m8Eh0xJKxQsrwQeF4Okoeqo-lUIWINlRoBfbygv3EHaxzpbUKAes3k8twzuHoiSjVcWKa7w-b6GPe_q_v1yz_BQAA__97FJTL) prohibits students from using their phones during the school day and requires school districts to determine how to implement the restriction. Several Texas school districts have already banned student cellphones, and at least nine other states have similar policies. [Senate Bill 379](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL2u4yAUhJ8Gd7EOmIApKHYLumyxW2xp8XMcI2FjmaM4efur61e4_Whmvu__Y3r4XPL2_ONXtPSe67Th2QoS4TH5Ugjfvp3xbBueq9-b35KUwyRA3G-gbiCn5HP5TLJLNkAyvEPLtTQwSAO8W2zQQaOKSSHcNQYTpBij9ykOCoUaY5ftdxkokJwDH3Q_Gz2PIXE_3LmZ5cwkYMpUj54WvP7M5VNfePSxrl2xC9He2PCLCceEi37PVEt_BftnfTHhqNRUY2PCjeYvEy7kC4yYcAuthQn37zfAoI3rF1q7w6b9zKXkpjST8Fx9LtcW_czQy4qvAAAA__9nyXmj) would prevent the purchase of sodas and candy using federal Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program benefits. “ _Taxpayer-funded soda and junk food turn into taxpayer-funded healthcare_ ,” [said bill author Mayes Middleton](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0LFuwyAUheGnwVuiC1yMGRi6eEvXjojAJUECOzI0jt--Sl6h6xk-Hf0_F3fxueTl9u0r2f5Kq1tob4V6p835Ujq9fNvD3hbaq380v0RE6QQIdYLxBOiiz-VwOER7hWj4QJZrNCDRAB_uVoqIoINS0gTyYAxekSiFFPgURhWGbN8YjICcA5f6nIxO0zVyLxU3CRNDoJj7up37nT5_UjnWJ23nsNah2Hvvj8bkFxMzE_PrvTIxV39QczXHWKivCxNz677_NiZmbsRkpJZSyWnSkwYlh83Gx55LyW3UDOFWfS4fv_-vytOKvwAAAP__4SVz3Q) (R-Galveston). Before the legislation can take effect, the state must receive a waiver from the U.S. Department of Agriculture, which Gov. Abbott [requested last month](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykULuSsyAUfhrtcA4X9aeg-Bu7bLulc5RDwgbBERKSt98xr7DdV3z378t8QR98vH7hRqa8XJoj1RyoFDpmDKHQC3Nda45UN9wzRquUnAWInsHAQM0WfXjPqrVmAat5S4aPSoNUGnh7MwPqnttBj0O_rP2irZaCg3JSAl-tHltvTjMYQHEOXI6d06P7t1iOsufaKdcoIOtLOrpyo08fF97pSUe3pq0N5lbKnhv5vxFTI6Zaa-fSS50zTkIjphM2YvpIGeZ7Zo9skVX0TzrYgpH9POKduZQsc0faWI64s4UiOV9yexi7Vx-Cz8PYKLhu6MMnuvztsKcRvwEAAP__pZSAgg). Nebraska recently became the [first state](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLGOnDAURb8GOo9sY4ahcJFmuk2bEj3s58GJwcjvsR7y9RGbT9jy6kpH595fH9MHxBS3109Y0fI75GnDSgmZsUyQEuMbqLpKG9YVdoLNG9NNWupeyLuQZvIQ0zmZ1ttZ-lG1aNVgRtmZUap2sXc1PIyeNXSPEZVzznsYPWLf91J18tFGe8HkXRqlpOqGWxiH8Ji9gq5XYzChMRJ95FxuvOCXT0hn_sRyc3ltk12Yd2q6H41-NvpZa72F_L5WXH2jn3tOkaOjRj8XSEH4_Bc3seaCghgYScywCcoexO9j-yNCzl7sR3ELENL_SAzrToLLse4CXiW6I_FxEdAVZCinIDipLdbvNaYU6T40Rr5WiOlLk7937qfV_wIAAP__xFuSvQ) to receive such a waiver.
## All-Texas Women's College World Series
This year’s Women’s College World Series final features an [all-Texas showdown](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL2u4yAQhZ_G7sYCjP8KF9uku9tuaREYwmjHYAEKN2-_Sop9gduc6uj8fH--ji9DTPHx25y412-fjoitMNaK-TDMFb9NabaViO00VzHRaT0eSqgJxAxCH84Qvw7du_0u3CZ73OWiNzHqTcg-7GrbZlycX1avptnOSuFq9bJu26rcau897e8wMQstpZDjMvht8evdSTNOcvPad1qgo5ryUAN-9nh-pSfmwaaz5z3UepVu_NWpW6du5Uq5luFlQkpvQ6duJleyjJ26tXRiLGATMz4QWsrsoGAmLOApGoYr45OwQUgNPl3_1QawJkKjCDUgZfCUS4WSfL0bZrDBnBelWAJdUAMVaIh_Qc6TnGY5zkOoJ_d5d1cjZirz0mnxOA3x50f9Gf3nrv4FAAD__7uGnQc) between No. 6 Texas and No. 12 Texas Tech. Both teams are vying for their first NCAA softball championship in a best-of-three series starting tonight. Both the Longhorns and the Red Raiders faced four-time defending champion Oklahoma on their way to the final. The Sooners were the favorite coming into the WCWS, having [played in every final since 2018](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkDGy3CAMhk-DO3YEZrFdUKShe2lTemQjdslg2AGyPN8-480R0kgqNJ_0f7--1i8MMaTHTzzItG-f10S9RmqNyooxNvrG2vdeE_UDXxWTU2pcJcg7B81BrQ5DPFc1OLOBW8RARkxqgVEtIIanme8Iy4iLQpKz0vsGykm3aT9vhKTnIZgLBhqUECDG6eaXyc-bEzjexeKVZwrIhZbLrT3p84-PZ35Tue35GKJ5tvaqbPzBpGXS-tZvfyq27PC8Fpi0teVyXv2VS6tM2rQjdibtdZhJC_oqI5P2Q-fvyv8NjfYn7_mgVPmeY6QH8Z5LdLxSCVT5R8Nlh6fwGwsR3zGhO3nNvm0YI5N2ViBASwCYmLRDMe7VQ4yh6okpeBwY4idI-z_9byP_BgAA__9BgZgl). Texas lost to Oklahoma in the 2022 and 2024 championships, and their 4-2 win over their rivals on Saturday was their first ever in the NCAA Tournament. The Red Raiders beat Oklahoma 3-2 on Monday to [advance to their first WCWS final](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkb3K4zAQRZ9G7iboz3FcuNjGXbbd0kyskS12LBlJRMnbLwnsE3zdwMA593L_3Jc7Bg5x-40HTfXl0xKpFaZaKS_IXOmFpa2tRGoHngWjs9YsWuoe5BWkXRwGfi-2c9NDulF1NKnBjtLYUapun-TV9F7dDA5kPDpHFukmkQZ6EBrluzB9YPIqrVJSmeHix8HfHk6h6dXorRdWkgs15Uvd6ZvH8zs9KV_WdHQ87bWeRZhfQs9Cz621C5Uzfp5Cz2tipo2gnCnXIvRckq8PZP6cNeW30PMi9Byc0LPtrRmN6YWevx6otO5A0RWw4MhTdCFusO54nJD-Mu7pQMgUtggn4xt8yKVCSwdF-G9uKbODQjlQAR8iMjw_5BeWLk_ubIE5lOsgrNwODPxtVX-2xXPS_wIAAP__qSmhGQ). The Longhorns have several strong pitchers on their roster, and their .357 batting average is the second-best in the NCAA. The Red Raiders, meanwhile, [will lean on NiJaree Canady](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkb2unDAQhZ_GdF7ZxsBSUGwUkSY3TYqUyOABrBgP8kyud98-YqU8QboZHenT-fn1MX24EEPafrgDBn6uOCUoFIEZ8uRiZHg6KkuhBOVwJ7nkra0no0wjVSuVnbwL8TXZyg-z8r2uYNCd7VVte6WrfVgaDW2ja3tvm97MFvo7zKvu_AIz9K2pwnDBVKus1krX3W3tu_U-e-3qRverXYVV4ANjvvEObz9rfOEn5NuCRxWHnfkkUT-EGYUZSym3g9KlCTNCkn9ImJFOzHwdyDtkYcY3R0ZM2445kczg_EuumGXBAxLJBWOEDWTBHL0kyAFIcuAI_x6XvAxMMiFLt7mQiCX-jm7HwwkzuiwfD_3NWPVd1CMuwYv665eraij0E_JZ5cGfJcQYqO2EVdvhQnxn4v9b4nMwfwMAAP__d3qgJA), who struck out more than 300 batters this year and whose 0.89 ERA is the best in the country.
| Flying together with our sponsor [](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkMFu6yAURL8G72JdLtc4XrDIe5UXVVMpq0rdIGwgQcV2ZEhI_r5yfqHrOZoZna-jPpoQw3z-NJNT-eEXPbuSosvZrdrEmN3DpDKWNLsymWsysyUSGgGbHcgdkLYmxKemyqoBbMcrp3hLHQjqgFcXZbnElgiFoD14OcKAo8cGO2GlBCOroLYykECcAxdt7bvW7wfLjWh458kzAmdDXtY6X9zrj4_P5e7WelymKqpLztfExIFhz7AvpdRmiAh5_dkAhj1-nP6LLev_nb73cuNEn5bbOjodLBNvPk63lK2zQO-3GZtDtSp7LSHGkGTLCM6TCfE1l_8m6a7wNwAA__-ApHf5) Andrew McKinley, a local chef from Indiana, had been suffering from tinnitus for over 13 years. The constant ear ringing disrupted his sleep, focus, and daily life—until something unexpected happened. One evening, Andrew prepared a new dish using a [**_unique blend of ingredients_**](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkMFu6yAURL8G72JdLtc4XrDIe5UXVVMpq0rdIGwgQcV2ZEhI_r5yfqHrOZoZna-jPpoQw3z-NJNT-eEXPbuSosvZrdrEmN3DpDKWNLsymWsysyUSGgGbHcgdkLYmxKemyqoBbMcrp3hLHQjqgFcXZbnElgiFoD14OcKAo8cGO2GlBCOroLYykECcAxdt7bvW7wfLjWh458kzAmdDXtY6X9zrj4_P5e7WelymKqpLztfExIFhz7AvpdRmiAh5_dkAhj1-nP6LLev_nb73cuNEn5bbOjodLBNvPk63lK2zQO-3GZtDtSp7LSHGkGTLCM6TCfE1l_8m6a7wNwAA__-ApHf5). The moment he took a bite, he noticed the ringing in his ears began to fade. Within minutes, the buzzing got quieter... and then completely vanished. Shocked and curious, Andrew consulted medical experts. Tests revealed that his dish contained [**_a rare spice_**](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkMFu6yAURL8G72JdLtc4XrDIe5UXVVMpq0rdIGwgQcV2ZEhI_r5yfqHrOZoZna-jPpoQw3z-NJNT-eEXPbuSosvZrdrEmN3DpDKWNLsymWsysyUSGgGbHcgdkLYmxKemyqoBbMcrp3hLHQjqgFcXZbnElgiFoD14OcKAo8cGO2GlBCOroLYykECcAxdt7bvW7wfLjWh458kzAmdDXtY6X9zrj4_P5e7WelymKqpLztfExIFhz7AvpdRmiAh5_dkAhj1-nP6LLev_nb73cuNEn5bbOjodLBNvPk63lK2zQO-3GZtDtSp7LSHGkGTLCM6TCfE1l_8m6a7wNwAA__-ApHf5) known for its powerful neurological benefits. Doctors were stunned—this single spice appeared to calm the nerves responsible for Tinnitus. New episodes drop daily—watch on YouTube or listen on Spotify and Apple Podcasts, all in under 15 minutes. | | Missing World War II Pilot Identified The U.S. Department of Defense announced Monday that the remains of First Lieutenant Charles W. McCook, a 23-year-old Georgetown native who was killed during World War II, had been . McCook, a pilot in the Army Air Forces, died when his B-25 crashed during a . Two of the six crew members were captured by Japanese forces, while the remaining four perished and were declared missing in action. Their remains were in a common grave near a Burmese village. They were unidentified at the time and were interred as "unknowns" in the National Memorial Cemetery of the Pacific in Honolulu. McCook’s remains were exhumed in 2022 and identified using dental, anthropological, isotope, and mitochondrial DNA analysis. He will be buried in Georgetown in August, and a on the Walls of the Missing at the Manila American Cemetery to indicate he has been accounted for. Legislature Restricts Use of Cellphones, Food Stamps Bills passed during this year’s legislative session would add Texas to the growing number of states that have banned student cellphones from public schools and prohibited the purchase of junk food using food stamps. prohibits students from using their phones during the school day and requires school districts to determine how to implement the restriction. Several Texas school districts have already banned student cellphones, and at least nine other states have similar policies. would prevent the purchase of sodas and candy using federal Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program benefits. “ Taxpayer-funded soda and junk food turn into taxpayer-funded healthcare ,” (R-Galveston). Before the legislation can take effect, the state must receive a waiver from the U.S. Department of Agriculture, which Gov. Abbott . Nebraska recently became the to receive such a waiver. All-Texas Women's College World Series This year’s Women’s College World Series final features an between No. 6 Texas and No. 12 Texas Tech. Both teams are vying for their first NCAA softball championship in a best-of-three series starting tonight. Both the Longhorns and the Red Raiders faced four-time defending champion Oklahoma on their way to the final. The Sooners were the favorite coming into the WCWS, having . Texas lost to Oklahoma in the 2022 and 2024 championships, and their 4-2 win over their rivals on Saturday was their first ever in the NCAA Tournament. The Red Raiders beat Oklahoma 3-2 on Monday to . The Longhorns have several strong pitchers on their roster, and their .357 batting average is the second-best in the NCAA. The Red Raiders, meanwhile, , who struck out more than 300 batters this year and whose 0.89 ERA is the best in the country. | Flying together with our sponsor Andrew McKinley, a local chef from Indiana, had been suffering from tinnitus for over 13 years. The constant ear ringing disrupted his sleep, focus, and daily life—until something unexpected happened. One evening, Andrew prepared a new dish using a . The moment he took a bite, he noticed the ringing in his ears began to fade. Within minutes, the buzzing got quieter... and then completely vanished. Shocked and curious, Andrew consulted medical experts. Tests revealed that his dish contained known for its powerful neurological benefits. Doctors were stunned—this single spice appeared to calm the nerves responsible for Tinnitus. Now, researchers say just one tablespoon of this spice may be enough to silence ear ringing for good, without pills, surgery, or expensive treatments. Over 45,000 people have already tried it with incredible success. Want to know what the spice is and how to use it? () ➤ Tarrant County: The Commissioners Court voted 3-2 yesterday to adopt a new district map that gives Republicans improved odds of flipping the two seats currently held by Democrats. Outgoing state Rep. Tony Tinderholt (R-Arlington) announced shortly after the vote that he would run for one of those seats. () ➤ Killeen: A train carrying 90 armored vehicles from Fort Cavazos departed for Washington, D.C., on Monday for the June 14 military parade to celebrate the Army’s 250th birthday. Six hundred soldiers from the post are also participating in the parade. () ➤ Ector County: Strong storms damaged mobile homes and RVs in the Pleasant Farms community, displacing several families. There were no reports of injuries. () ➤ Dallas: Police have been instructed not to enforce a city ordinance allowing officers to question individuals suspected of soliciting prostitution if they attempt to stop a passerby. Despite revisions to the ordinance, a municipal judge declared it unconstitutionally vague. () ➤ Harris County: A district judge tightened a gag order on former District Attorney Kim Ogg after she shared new allegations about one of the suspects in the rape and murder of 12-year-old Jocelyn Nungaray last June. It’s true, Peter.
Every time you might think they can’t find a new way to cheat the system, Republicans keep trying. They’ll gerrymander as many maps as they can. They’ll severely restrict state ballot measures, like they recently did in Florida. They’ll try to steal a state supreme court seat, like in North Carolina. And on and on.
Republicans are nothing if not consistent. And that’s exactly how _we_ need to be: consistent and _persistent_ in our fight to save our democracy.
[**In the face of anti-democracy forces who will do anything it takes to seize and hold power, contribute now to help make sure we are able to fight back in Florida, Ohio, Louisiana, and all across the country.**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/108940736/549507536/-1156862612?refcode=don_ndrc_tier1_email_AGQuoteAppeal_email_20250604-NDRC-Tier1-AGQuoteAppeal_var1&utm_source=email&utm_medium=email&utm_term=don&utm_content=20250604-NDRC-Tier1-AGQuoteAppeal_var1&utm_campaign=AGQuoteAppeal&amount=30&sourceid=&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjc3OThmMjdhLTNiNDEtZjAxMS1hNWYxLTYwNDViZGE5ZDk2YiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=K1LYly1AK7U1JmKl3HEx6fGCCn-1J9u-BS_19HQNeYI=)

The truth is, Republicans are terrified of voters. The MAGA GOP wants to avoid accountability at any cost. But we’re fighting back for a level playing field, where voters have the power to make their voices heard. And we know that Democrats don’t need to cheat to win — as Attorney General Holder says, “if the maps are fair, Democrats do just fine.”
Look: If someone tries to cheat at basketball or soccer or chess, you don’t just let them break or rewrite the rules. You stand up, defend fair play, and resolve to win the right way.
**We must never underestimate how far Republicans will go — so we must always make sure we have everything we need to fight back and _win_.**
[**Donate $30 or whatever you can spare right now to power our fight for democracy in states across the country.**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/108940746/549507558/-1156862612?refcode=don_ndrc_tier1_email_AGQuoteAppeal_email_20250604-NDRC-Tier1-AGQuoteAppeal_var1&utm_source=email&utm_medium=email&utm_term=don&utm_content=20250604-NDRC-Tier1-AGQuoteAppeal_var1&utm_campaign=AGQuoteAppeal&amount=30&sourceid=&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjc3OThmMjdhLTNiNDEtZjAxMS1hNWYxLTYwNDViZGE5ZDk2YiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=K1LYly1AK7U1JmKl3HEx6fGCCn-1J9u-BS_19HQNeYI=)

subject: New York has become a third-world hellscape!
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY MAUD FOR MANHATTAN
body:
The George Soros elected, Pro-Criminal, Trump-Deranged DEI hire that has turned my beloved New York City into a third-world Hellscape.

| | |
---
---
| | [](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zjmve54a2uvd135lr4jfumdj7mvdbpdcl4npmdto_rp22sh2s8i6eob4s1imapb2c5h2bs)
---
| | My Dear Friend, [**My name is Maud Maron, and I need your help to defeat Alvin Bragg and replace him as the District Attorney of Manhattan.**](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zmpfqo9q38mbs3kni2u10bhqbfdcsmncljrta81g_rp22sh2s8i6eob4s1imapb2c5h2bs) You know Alvin... The George Soros elected, Pro-Criminal, Trump-Deranged DEI hire that has turned my beloved New York City into a third-world Hellscape.If you think that sounds too dramatic, just look at a few examples of what Alvin Bragg’s “weak-on-crime,” Trump-obsessed tenure has brought to the law-abiding citizens of New York:
---
* An innocent woman set on fire by a Guatemalan Illegal Alien who had already been deported at least once before...
* A mob of Illegal criminals caught on video assaulting two New York City Law Enforcement officers outside a “Migrant Shelter,” all **released without bail by Alvin Bragg!**

---
Instead of working for the last 4 years to make the Citizens of New York safe and secure, Alvin has been on a singular, sick, twisted mission and one mission only -- TAKE DOWN DONALD TRUMP.That’s what he ran on...Those were his marching orders from George Soros and his Radical donors...And those are orders he dutifully followed -- at the expense of not only those of us who live in New York, but every single American that deserved to vote in a fair election not tainted by a ridiculous set of trumped-up charges made up out of thin air.New Yorkers deserve better...Americans deserve better.That’s why -- whether you live right here in Manhattan, a thousand miles away in Des Moines, Iowa, or anywhere else in our great country -- I hope you’ll help me defeat Alvin Bragg. **[Would you consider helping my campaign with a one-time $28 contribution today?](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2z4q15dmmlcadb185aeavk40s2skocqs01s388nr0_rp22sh2s8i6eob4s1imapb2c5h2bs)** I could really use your help.Alvin Bragg has millions of dollars pouring into his campaign by the same Radicals who put him there the first time around...
---
* George Soros
* Hollywood Celebrities
* Elites right here in New York and Hollywood
* The deep pockets of the Democrat election machine

---
I don’t have any of that on my side -- but I hope I’ve got YOU on my side.To have any chance of beating the Democrat machine funding Alvin Bragg’s run for re-election, I need a nationwide army of Americans to rally by my side...A nationwide army of Americans who understand how toxic it is having a Radical like Alvin Bragg in charge of the criminal justice system of one of our largest cities...Who know how close Alvin Bragg came to knocking Donald Trump out of the 2024 Presidential election and what that would have done to our country... Who watched in disgust as Alvin Bragg conjured up a 34-count felony conviction against a man whose only real crime was trying to Make America Great Again!This is our chance to send a clear message not only to the people of New York -- but the nation -- that these Radicals put into place by George Soros to do his bidding are not invincible...That they CAN be defeated! Alvin Bragg is the perfect example of why I left the Democrat party.I think it all started when my 11-year-old got COVID... We were told to mask in the house and give my son meals in his room for 10 days. Instead, we cuddled up & watched TV together -- unmasked in the living room and ate our meals as a family at the dinner table. That was a watershed moment for me.What were we doing?When did the United States of America turn into a place where the Government told us how to live?When I dared to suggest that it may not be the best idea for biological boys to participate in girls’ sports, I was all but run out of town on a rail... Then I dared suggest that the complete failure of our school system might be due to something other than racism, I -- after a lifetime of considering myself a liberal Democrat -- was labeled a “Right Wing Extremist” by the New York Times!The blinders came off -- suddenly, I realized that if simply acknowledging the truth, calling out corruption and challenging the long-held beliefs of the Democrat machine disqualified me from being a Democrat.The truth will set you free -- and it’s an honor to represent you on the “Freedom Side” of the wall.I’ve been in their camp.As a former Democrat and as a mom raising four kids in Manhattan. I know how much they are getting wrong. And how bad it is for our children. **[That’s why I’m asking good folks like you to help me.](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zimceuseou3bevgqpr6qck6404hbtuqh9tl9bkh0_rp22sh2s8i6eob4s1imapb2c5h2bs)** It’s going to take all of us to take down Alvin Bragg -- but this has been a season of political miracles!I humbly ask for your help today. I hope you’ll find it in your heart to send a one-time $28 contribution to help me pull off one more political miracle......and send Alvin Bragg back to obscurity where he belongs.He’s bad for New York...He’s bad for America...And he needs to go.One last favor...I know not everybody is going to be able to give that I write to today. **[So if you think you could spare a larger gift -- of $50, $100, $250, $500 or even $1,000 or more, a few of those would really help right now.](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zj4bcbfcae8pgdn6p384ns7fi84fgimql508r5fg_rp22sh2s8i6eob4s1imapb2c5h2bs)** We need a good, strong launch -- and without a few of those larger gifts, we’ll already be behind.Please just send what you can, My Friend.Just reading my email today is a tremendous favor, and I really appreciate it.Thank you, Wish Me Luck, and God Bless You!
---
---
**Maud Maron**

P.S. I guess Alvin Bragg has just been too darn busy to concentrate on the wave of crime and violence washing over New York...While he has spent the last 4 years prosecuting and persecuting President Trump, New York has become a third-world hellscape!New York deserves better...Our Country deserves better...

Will you help me take the place of Alvin Bragg by supporting my campaign to become the District Attorney of Manhattan?**[If so, please rush a one-time gift of $28, $50, $100, $250, $500 or even $1,000 or more -- I could really use your help!](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2z83c418cbbppcog9m3dssf5roov7v4kp4j81kuvo_rp22sh2s8i6eob4s1imapb2c5h2bs)**

FROM: A proud supporter
Subject: THANK YOU! Dear Senate Democrats,

**THANK YOU for leading the charge against Donald Trump and his MAGA minions as they attack our democracy!**

I know that Trump has put a huge target on your backs — so it means the world that you aren’t backing down even a little bit.

**_Our work is far from over:_ Republicans are plotting to END Social Security and Medicaid, DESTROY the federal government, and make Trump an untouchable KING.**

As a proud Democrat, you have my word that I will join your fight and do _everything_ I can to help take back the Senate and stop this dangerous administration.

name: Teichert in the Trenches
subject: Teichert in the Trenches - June 2025
body:
I appreciated the opportunity to join NewsNation...
---
| | | | | |
---
| |
#### Teichert in the Trenches - June 2025
Welcome to this month’s installment of Teichert in the Trenches, a monthly newsletter highlighting a snippet of my attempts to maximize my impact on people and our great nation and to develop the leaders our nation needs! ** _Teichert in the News_**
---
| | | | [ ](https://eufi5.r.a.d.sendibm1.com/mk/cl/f/sh/SMK1E8tHeFuBmP64SPACXkt7Xr7g/ts8BoqTfxAXu)
---
| | | I appreciated the opportunity to join NewsNation to chat about the new Army Transformation Initiative that includes lightning-fast scaling to 1,000 drones in each division (May 2nd) ** _Thoughts by Teichert_**
---
| | | | [ ](https://eufi5.r.a.d.sendibm1.com/mk/cl/f/sh/SMK1E8tHeG13EXv1dZJgdaBrW9hw/NAbln7sn2Fww)
---
| | | | [ "I have seen State’s
dysfunction up close..."](https://eufi5.r.a.d.sendibm1.com/mk/cl/f/sh/SMK1E8tHeG7uggjyojTAjPUbUSIC/C5T3eyHuGsqR)
---
Thanks to “The Hill” for publishing my thoughts about the State Department’s reorganization plans. “To pretend that streamlining a lumbering bureaucracy in D.C. will automatically harm U.S. interests is to neglect the bloat and dysfunction that currently suffocates U.S. diplomatic efforts.”
| | |
---
| |
---
| | | 😃 Communication (and leadership) that puts a smile on your face!

📣 These types of envelopes arrive every few weeks with a residual check for a short line I said in an episode of Monk in 2005: “Stop! They’re friendlies, they’re friendlies!” Every time I see one of these in my mailbox, it puts a smile on my face without even seeing the content. This one was bigger than most these days -- $31.83 – but it doesn’t matter.

🤔 We've all had leaders that are the opposite of that envelope. Every time you see an email in your inbox from them, it makes you cringe. It raises your blood pressure before you even read the content. You see them coming down the hall, and you avoid their path and dodge their gaze. Their style is characterized by negativity, judgment, hierarchy, toxicity, and endless expectations with harsh feedback. They don’t listen to the ideas of their team. They hold others to impossible standards and don’t ever reflect upon their own shortcomings or complicity in failure.

💥 Conversely, you’ve had leaders that make you feel just like this SAG envelope – putting a smile on your face when you even think about them. They are characterized by positivity, reassurance, kindness, collaboration, humanity, and reasonably high expectations with productive feedback. They listen, consider, and act on the ideas of their team. They hold everyone, especially themselves, to challenging standards while fostering a mutual supportive environment that enables success.

!!️ Create a leadership culture that elicits a response like that SAG Envelope!
---
| [ "New KC-46 Remote Vision System Slips Another 18 Months"](https://eufi5.r.a.d.sendibm1.com/mk/cl/f/sh/SMK1E8tHeGEm8pYvztcepEnLSksS/VnIMBmGAmlOr)
---
!!️ The KC-46: An Abject Acquisition Leadership Failure !!️

📣 The KC-46 case study is a gut-wrenching example of self-sabotage that diminishes the DoD’s ability to achieve lethality and readiness with acquisition leaders complicit in our shortcomings.

🎤 From my position as the commander of Edwards Air Force Base (2018-2020), I witnessed our operationally-minded flight testers raising concerns about the KC-46 RVS and submitting the appropriate Deficiency Reports (Cat 1 DRs). I fielded calls from Program Office leaders who tried to persuade me, and then begged me, and then threatened me to rescind those DRs. They wanted their program to stay on track without proper concern for the [warfighter](https://eufi5.r.a.d.sendibm1.com/mk/cl/f/sh/SMK1E8tHeGLdayNtB3m8v465R3Si/vdC0s4CBhxcA). We did not back down, even though they aligned with the contractor to pressure us and diminish our findings.

💥 A key reason for U.S. systemic acquisition failures is due to the perverse incentives that reward program offices for passing on problems to the operator to keep their programs “on-track”. This must be fixed!
| [ "What you need to know
about the REAL ID"](https://eufi5.r.a.d.sendibm1.com/mk/cl/f/sh/SMK1E8tHeGSV37CqMDvd0tOpPM2y/Jj7gnIl1KOqe)
---
| | | Government Dysfunction – the REAL ID Act

📣 The REAL ID Act went into effect last week. Before we pat ourselves on the back, let’s step back and consider a bit of context. Congress passed the Act in 2005 – two decades ago. It was supposed to go into effect in 2008. Now 17 years later we finally did it!

🧨 If the U.S. can’t accomplish something as simple as implementing a reliable personal identification product in a reasonable timeframe, how can we expect to mobilize to face a major foe, avoid a looming crisis, or overcome a significant national challenge?

!!️ Citizens must demand functionality from our government. We must insist that our elected officials succeed in accomplishing the responsibilities at their level of our federalized system. Anything less is unacceptable, and we have settled for far too long. ** _Teichert in the Trenches_**

| | | **_Behind the Scenes_**
---
| | | |
---
I’m striving for _The Dragon Who Earned His Wings_ to be out by the end of the month! As I’ve mentioned, not only is it a kid’s book, but it’s also a great book for adults to use as a metaphor for leadership, resilience, and innovation. It was a tough balance to speak to both audiences, but I think we got it right. One of the lines that was vetoed by my editors (i.e. my family and friends) was: “He never actually broke the rules, but found creative ways around them when they got in the way.” They rightfully pointed out that this line (as written) might not be exactly what parents want their kids taking away from the book. 😃
| | | |
---
🦀 A fitting devotional in the Maryland House of Delegates 🦀

🙏 Thanks to my good friend Delegate Stuart Schmidt for using Prayers for a Nation as his daily devotional during this year’s Maryland legislative session.

📣 Prayer is the least you should do and the most you can do to impact our great nation! ** _My Bookshelf_**
|
---
It is a raw and authentic book that ping-pongs back and forth between military struggles in war-torn Iraq and mental health and physical health challenges upon the author’s return. “The point of this book isn’t to whine; it’s to tell the story of the greatest success story of the Iraq war and to shine light on what I and many others like me have had to deal with coming home to a VA medical system that focuses on checking boxes rather than saving lives. Most important of all, this book aims to memorialize those who didn’t come back home and knowing that what they gave their lives for changed the course of the Iraq war for the better.”
|
---
“Use your imagination for a moment. Can you envision your brain as if it were an actual body? Based on the excessive amount of time we spend ingesting unhealthy ideas, social media, and worthless content, our brains (as bodies) would appear morbidly obese. How much do you think your brain would weigh? Two hundred, three hundred, five hundred pounds? ... Your mind is the most daunting, most powerful, and most influential force on earth. You must feed it – nourish it with ‘nutrients’ that drive productive habits, positive behaviors, and extreme focus.”
|
---
“In early 1998, I set out for Israel and the West Bank in search of a surprisingly elusive story. Despite the forests of newspaper stories and miles of videotape documenting the intractable conflict between Israelis and Palestinians, precious little light had fallen on the human side of the story, the common ground between enemies, and genuine hopes for coexistence ... I encountered many dead ends and broken leads. But then I came across something real. It was the true story of one house, two families, and a common history emanating from the walls of Jerusalem stone on the coastal plain east of Tel Aviv and Jaffa. From a single house, and the lemon tree in its garden, lay a path to the histories, both separate and intertwined, of the Khairi and Eshkenazi families, and to the large story of two peoples are one land.” ** _PLUS Posts for the Month_**
| | | |
---
| [ Rising to the Truth
(May 3rd)](https://eufi5.r.a.d.sendibm1.com/mk/cl/f/sh/SMK1E8tHeGZMVG1nXO576ihZNedE/BPJctpdgU3p- "Pronouns Matter")
---
| [ Regardless of Our Circumstances (May 10th)](https://eufi5.r.a.d.sendibm1.com/mk/cl/f/sh/SMK1E8tHeGgDxOqkiYEbCY0JLxDU/ioK_dgoTvpyv "Finding the Stars")
---
| [ The Proper Direction
(May 17th)](https://eufi5.r.a.d.sendibm1.com/mk/cl/f/sh/SMK1E8tHeGn5PXfhtiO5INJ3KFnk/nkZu82JT9X94 "We Must Listen")
---
| [ Looking Past the Burdens...
(May 24th)](https://eufi5.r.a.d.sendibm1.com/mk/cl/f/sh/SMK1E8tHeGtwrgUf4sXZOCbnIYO0/u5FgpYvXv4iJ "Transforming Common Days into Thanksgiving")
---
| | | **_Blasts from the Past_**
---
| | | | [ ](https://eufi5.r.a.d.sendibm1.com/mk/cl/f/sh/1t6Af4OiGsDhYHcZvsAP4JMNKzgWGO/0JIDyIWGNohi)
---
An Amazing Team on the Front Lines of American Power

📣 I recently came across this memorable picture of my core leadership team in Baghdad from a 14-month deployment as our nation’s Senior Defense Official and Defense Attache (2020-2021).

💥 Yes, we wore body armor over our suits on the way to frequent meetings with senior Iraqi leaders (escorted by a large security team anytime we left the embassy). And our time with our counterparts was vital to achieving U.S. national interests in the toughest of situations in the midst of extreme security challenges and COVID. The whole-of-government team we were a part of was incredible and demonstrated the potency of U.S. power that spans the globe to meet U.S. interests. ** _Dad Joke of the Month_** I had a great conversation with a dolphin last week.
We just clicked!
| | | |
---
| | | | **Teichert in the Trenches**
---
| Washington, DC

body:
Celebrate the Republican Brand by the Sea

| **Discover New Ways To Message the Republican Brand****Please Join Me Thursday**
**SECOND-ANNUAL**
**Frederick Douglass Celebration Event****Thursday, June 5, 2025 | Oceanside at The Owenego, 40 Linden Ave., Branford, CT 06405**
_**Join the Dominic Rapini in Celebrating a Great American and Great Republican**_
_**Special Guests**_ _**Gary Byron**_ _**Peter Lumaj**_ _**Matt Corey**_ _**Chairman Ben Proto**_ _**Vice Chair MaryAnne Turner**_
[**BUY TICKETS**](https://dominicforct.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=ebcefd906ec01db66e79c886c&id=fc6c996f2b&e=8763c56857)

## **About Frederick**
## **Douglass**
**A Force for Uniting the United States** Frederick Douglass, born into slavery in 1818, became a leading advocate for uniting our nation after the Civil War. His conviction in our founding values and principles helped guide the Republican party to win a second term under Abraham Lincoln’s leadership. Lincoln faced the challenge of rescuing a divided nation.
**Frederick Douglass played a crucial role in the birth of the Republican Party** The Republican Party was founded in 1854 by abolitionists who aimed to end slavery and advocate for the civil rights of Black people, with Frederick Douglass playing a significant role in initiating the political campaign.
**Believed in American Principles** Lincoln embraced Douglass’s message of preserving our nation’s values, and it became a roadmap for reunification. Today, Douglass’s contribution offers the most effective messaging for winning new hearts and minds.
**A Symbol of Republican Values** Republicans can use Frederick Douglass as a symbol to unite the voters and realize the greatness of our country, just like Lincoln did.

---

# A Long Overdue Update
---
[|
---](https://mansharamani.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=caf139d4582deef486da11685&id=0b1908001d&e=93b2c2b824)
|
## Announcing Goodwell Foods
---
| For years, I’ve suggested that the best way to navigate uncertainty is to integrate thinking by crossing silos and connecting dots. I’ve often described this method as the “generalist approach” because it prioritizes breadth of perspective over depth of expertise. It is impossible, I believe, to fully appreciate the complexities of our time without combining geopolitical, macroeconomic, regulatory, political, technological, demographic, and social perspectives. At the core of my thinking is a simple belief: _**context matters**_.
And so when I decided to return to the business world last fall, I started practicing what I preached. As I evaluated the many start-up, acquisition, and investment ideas that were swirling around my head, I constantly returned to my analytical framework of trying to understand the context. How, for instance, might a warmer planet impact the demand for HVAC services? Sure, demand for air conditioning might rise, but what about heating? Or how could demographics affect a sawmill’s potential revenue streams? Would aging populations help or hurt the market for building materials? And regardless of industry, I always returned to asking myself about technology. How might technology disrupt the industry, sector, or business I was analyzing?
I evaluated dozens of potential opportunities before homing in on one opportunity that caught my attention. During the summer of 2024, a New Hampshire-based pizza manufacturing operation had suddenly shut its doors, sending several dozen employees to the unemployment lines. Attempting to salvage value, banks and others who lent money to the company took control and began a process to find a buyer for the business. When I learned about the situation, there were several interested bidders looking to buy and restore the company. It wasn’t until those parties failed to make a deal that I began to really dig into the opportunity of entering the pizza manufacturing business.I began my diligence by focusing on the context. Regardless of the unique circumstances of the specific opportunity, I had to answer a simple question: _**Do I even want to be in the frozen pizza business?**_
To answer this question, I started my analysis with a focus on the big picture and the trends that were impacting the...
---
| [CLICK TO CONTINUE READING](https://mansharamani.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=caf139d4582deef486da11685&id=de034df2c6&e=93b2c2b824)
---
[|
---](https://mansharamani.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=caf139d4582deef486da11685&id=19bad7c676&e=93b2c2b824)
|
# Goodwell: What’s in a Name?
---
|
What’s my favorite book? That’s a question I am often asked, and it’s become harder to answer these past few years. The titles and authors I’ve named for decades come readily to mind, but I’ve started to wonder if those answers are still true. It’s been years since I’ve read some of them, and while they appealed to the teenage or undergraduate version of me, I wondered if they still would now that I am an adult. I have a friend – big Hemingway fan - who recently told me that he never cared for _The Old Man and the Sea_ when he read it in high school, but when he re-read it in middle-age, it landed a lot differently.
And so last summer, at the active encouragement of my good friend Worth Wray, I reread _Atlas Shrugged_. I rediscovered Objectivism and quickly became intellectually obsessed with Ayn Rand’s thinking. I reread the books of my youth, tearing through _Anthem_ and _The Virtues of Selfishness, We the Living,_ and _Capitalism_. Her insights into class warfare, tyranny, freedom, and the distinction between producers and looters now had an urgent relevance that I didn’t recall in my college reading of her work.
In much of Western culture, the capitalist is often portrayed as a villain, exploiting workers for his personal gain. For Rand, the businessman who created and built and hired was the hero of the story; production was the greatest virtue. And Rand saw business as a unique bulwark against tyranny. She [wrote](https://mansharamani.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=caf139d4582deef486da11685&id=1772339df0&e=93b2c2b824) that _**while other professions exist in totalitarian societies – politicians, farmers, teachers, scientists, soldiers – businessmen do not**_. Businessmen are what distinguish capitalist from statist societies, and Rand saw them as the ultimate symbol of freedom. One of her key messages was that there was a massive difference between businessmen who produce and bureaucrats who live off the production of others.
Rediscovering Rand was one of the reasons I decided to start a business last fall, something I wrote about in my last post. I had written and thought and taught about business. I had advised and served on the boards of some of the largest companies in the world. I had even started a few small businesses, usually focused on ideas and the abstract (I had often referred to myself as an “entrepreneur of ideas”). It was time to start and run a business that created something tangible and concrete, while also creating jobs.
And hence I formed [Goodwell Foods](https://mansharamani.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=caf139d4582deef486da11685&id=4f75f79221&e=93b2c2b824), a private label frozen pizza manufacturer located in Pittsfield, NH that now employs several dozen people. We went from standing still to sprinting...
---

# Other Mansharamani Musings...
---
| As many of you are aware, I’ve returned to writing my weekly newsletter and have been pleased by the rapidly growing interest in my thoughts. As I have in the past, I’m addressing a wide range of topics, ranging from geopolitics and economics to social trends to technology. The overall theme of my work is focused on “navigating uncertainty” and how a generalist approach can help us all make sense of what seems like chaos. If you have not already done so, please consider subscribing! (**It’s Free**!)

clean_body: I'm six months into a new project I want to tell you about... | | | | | | | | | | | A Long Overdue Update | Announcing Goodwell Foods | For years, I’ve suggested that the best way to navigate uncertainty is to integrate thinking by crossing silos and connecting dots. I’ve often described this method as the “generalist approach” because it prioritizes breadth of perspective over depth of expertise. It is impossible, I believe, to fully appreciate the complexities of our time without combining geopolitical, macroeconomic, regulatory, political, technological, demographic, and social perspectives. At the core of my thinking is a simple belief: context matters. And so when I decided to return to the business world last fall, I started practicing what I preached. As I evaluated the many start-up, acquisition, and investment ideas that were swirling around my head, I constantly returned to my analytical framework of trying to understand the context. How, for instance, might a warmer planet impact the demand for HVAC services? Sure, demand for air conditioning might rise, but what about heating? Or how could demographics affect a sawmill’s potential revenue streams? Would aging populations help or hurt the market for building materials? And regardless of industry, I always returned to asking myself about technology. How might technology disrupt the industry, sector, or business I was analyzing? I evaluated dozens of potential opportunities before homing in on one opportunity that caught my attention. During the summer of 2024, a New Hampshire-based pizza manufacturing operation had suddenly shut its doors, sending several dozen employees to the unemployment lines. Attempting to salvage value, banks and others who lent money to the company took control and began a process to find a buyer for the business. When I learned about the situation, there were several interested bidders looking to buy and restore the company. It wasn’t until those parties failed to make a deal that I began to really dig into the opportunity of entering the pizza manufacturing business.I began my diligence by focusing on the context. Regardless of the unique circumstances of the specific opportunity, I had to answer a simple question: Do I even want to be in the frozen pizza business? To answer this question, I started my analysis with a focus on the big picture and the trends that were impacting the... | | Goodwell: What’s in a Name? | What’s my favorite book? That’s a question I am often asked, and it’s become harder to answer these past few years. The titles and authors I’ve named for decades come readily to mind, but I’ve started to wonder if those answers are still true. It’s been years since I’ve read some of them, and while they appealed to the teenage or undergraduate version of me, I wondered if they still would now that I am an adult. I have a friend – big Hemingway fan - who recently told me that he never cared for The Old Man and the Sea when he read it in high school, but when he re-read it in middle-age, it landed a lot differently. And so last summer, at the active encouragement of my good friend Worth Wray, I reread Atlas Shrugged. I rediscovered Objectivism and quickly became intellectually obsessed with Ayn Rand’s thinking. I reread the books of my youth, tearing through Anthem and The Virtues of Selfishness, We the Living, and Capitalism. Her insights into class warfare, tyranny, freedom, and the distinction between producers and looters now had an urgent relevance that I didn’t recall in my college reading of her work. In much of Western culture, the capitalist is often portrayed as a villain, exploiting workers for his personal gain. For Rand, the businessman who created and built and hired was the hero of the story; production was the greatest virtue. And Rand saw business as a unique bulwark against tyranny. She that while other professions exist in totalitarian societies – politicians, farmers, teachers, scientists, soldiers – businessmen do not. Businessmen are what distinguish capitalist from statist societies, and Rand saw them as the ultimate symbol of freedom. One of her key messages was that there was a massive difference between businessmen who produce and bureaucrats who live off the production of others. Rediscovering Rand was one of the reasons I decided to start a business last fall, something I wrote about in my last post. I had written and thought and taught about business. I had advised and served on the boards of some of the largest companies in the world. I had even started a few small businesses, usually focused on ideas and the abstract (I had often referred to myself as an “entrepreneur of ideas”). It was time to start and run a business that created something tangible and concrete, while also creating jobs. And hence I formed , a private label frozen pizza manufacturer located in Pittsfield, NH that now employs several dozen people. We went from standing still to sprinting... | | | | | | | | | | | | | Other Mansharamani Musings... | As many of you are aware, I’ve returned to writing my weekly newsletter and have been pleased by the rapidly growing interest in my thoughts. As I have in the past, I’m addressing a wide range of topics, ranging from geopolitics and economics to social trends to technology. The overall theme of my work is focused on “navigating uncertainty” and how a generalist approach can help us all make sense of what seems like chaos. Peter, China just dropped **$500 MILLION** into the World Health Organization, a move designed to buy control over an organization that's already proven it can't be trusted. _**Remember when the WHO parroted CCP lies during COVID, downplaying the virus, shielding its origins, and blocking investigations into the Wuhan lab leak?**_ Now it's even worse: The WHO has ZERO incentive to tell the truth about COVID's origins--or warn us about future pandemics. With China's cash, they're fully owned by the CCP. Col. Pam ran for Kentucky Attorney General in 2023. Although she did not win, her performance on election night showed serious VIABILITY and an electorate that is open to voting for a Democrat – Specifically, Col. Pam Stevenson.

It’s worth mentioning that Democrats have been elected STATEWIDE in Kentucky consecutively since 1983. In 2023, Colonel Pam Stevenson outperformed every Democrat in EVERY race below her on the ballot. She also outperformed incredibly well-funded previous statewide candidates, Amy McGrath and Charles Booker, by nearly 4 points each. **We have a __real__ chance to win this seat and take back the U.S. Senate!**

Voters in Kentucky are saying that they’ve had ENOUGH – polling shows they feel as though they do not identify with either political party. The Kentucky GOP is battling heavily, leaving room for Col. Stevenson to make a direct cut to advance to victory.

**As you can see, we have plenty of reason to believe this election is winnable for a Democratic candidate like Col. Pam Stevenson, and we MUST take advantage of this moment. We _can_ replace a racist, sexist, bigot with a phenomenal Black woman. But we will need all the help we can get to make it happen.**

[**Col. Stevenson has served our country with dignity and honor. She is one of the highest ranking Democrats in the entire state of Kentucky – and certainly the most qualified leader. Help us get Col. Stevenson’s campaign successfully launched by pitching in $20.25 or more today!**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/108955956/549541596/-527673297?refcode=EM_F_250604fwd_N_HBM_NO_5_NG_N_L3&amount=20.25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAwNy8xLzg4NDk1IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImEwN2FjNTFhLTRhNDEtZjAxMS1hNWYxLTYwNDViZGE5ZDk2YiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=Ea7LkIwGkfO8neF2skQQsv_LFTZv3NxH5tC8qw4xvMQ=)

name: Team Scalise
subject: Trump’s bill passed. Now what?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by TEAM SCALISE, a joint fundraising committee authorized by and to benefit SCALISE FOR CONGRESS and EYE OF THE TIGER PAC
body:
The House just passed one of the most important pieces of legislation in the history of our nation

Friend, we know you are busy but:
The House just passed one of the most important pieces of legislation in the history of our nation: Trump's Big Beautiful Bill
We wanted to provide you with an open forum to share your thoughts on the Big Beautiful Bill and what you want us in the House to work on next.So if you have the time we would really appreciate your thoughts on this important matter.[SHARE YOUR THOUGHTS
](http://e.nationalrepublicanpolling.com/rd/9z2z3jlu6ni7olkn5ikf3t3ud8j4dqotjkqshncl17g_rp22sh2s8i6eob4s1im9p34c3h2bs)Feedback from loyal, informed Patriots like you is essential to the MAGA movement.
Would you please do us the honor of responding?
\- Team Scalise
* * *
\- Democrat Leadership formed an _anti-Trump task force_ \- More than a dozen Attorneys General are _suing_ the Trump Administration\- A House Democrat just introduced _7 Articles of Impeachment_[](http://e.nationalrepublicanpolling.com/rd/9z2zh239h8ec6eg2ph08qus43bi0acl1782fos7pqt8_rp22sh2s8i6eob4s1im9p34c3h2bs) If they manage to strip us of our House Majority, they'll have the power to impeach, investigate, and bring his entire legislative agenda to a SCREECHING HALT!
**We're PLEADING WITH YOU to do your part to help SAVE our Pro-Trump House Majority:**[ DO YOUR PART: PITCH IN NOW

name: AMAC Action
subject: President Trump only has 18 months.
body:
My friend, President Trump only has 18 months left until the midterm elections.

| |
---
| |
---
| |
---
[](http://e.1600daily.com/rd/9z2zgm2d14uau7rkg4r8asen3dlb6l5h4jif7lt8n2o_rp22sh2s8i6eob4s1hm0o34c9h2bs)
---
| | Dear Fellow Conservative, **[I’m writing to you today because I need your help to secure our elections once and for all and keep America on the path to greatness.](http://e.1600daily.com/rd/9z2zc7hv1513kvkkto75q290isig5i7o5omqq4fg5vg_rp22sh2s8i6eob4s1hm0o34c9h2bs)** My friend, President Trump only has 18 months left until the midterm elections. **That’s 18 months to push through as much of his America First agenda as he can while we have a majority in the House and Senate.** I don’t mean to be pessimistic about our chances in the midterms. I’m just being a realist.Historically, the party not in the White House wins control of the House of Representatives in the midterms. That means, in just 18 months we could be right back where we started if young radicals like Hakeem Jeffries and Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez regain power and start blocking every one of our efforts. **[That’s why our election integrity efforts must be a priority right now._election integrity efforts_ must be a priority right now.](http://e.1600daily.com/rd/9z2zm23seufq36litgalhluiiorem0etqvclr5ru0l8_rp22sh2s8i6eob4s1hm0o34c9h2bs)**_If we can’t get the SAVE Act through the Senate...__ _ _If we can’t cement President Trump’s “Preserving and Protecting the Integrity of American Elections” executive order through legislation...__ __If we can’t stop non-citizens from being counted towards the apportionment of congressional seats and electoral vote counts..._ ...the Left will be free to use every dirty trick in the book for the midterms in 2026, the presidential election in 2028... and beyond.[**AMAC Action, will never give up. But my friend, as a strong conservative senior, we NEED you right there beside us.**](http://e.1600daily.com/rd/9z2zcddhfq4c5d38ddal4o6hl161bsm31rqr5dhkq8g_rp22sh2s8i6eob4s1hm0o34c9h2bs) My name is Rebecca Weber. I’m the chairwoman of AMAC Action. **AMAC Action****is****the most influential advocacy group in the country when it comes to standing for the values of conservative Seniors.** **We’ve got 72 chapters and 300 volunteer delegates, who are the “feet on the street” advocates who contact Members of Congress and state legislators in their district offices.****Moreover, our chapters and delegates help organize Town Hall meetings with elected officials and candidates to enable those locally of 2,000,000 AMAC members to meet, hear, and question them.** **And you should also know that we now have more than 337,000 AMAC members who respond to our “calls-to-action” and weigh in with members of the U.S. House and Senate, as well as their state legislators on the****is****sues.** Friend, with the support of patriotic seniors like you, in just the past few months AMAC Action has been able to:
---
** Hold 100 meetings with Congress -- this year alone! And send 40 Letters of Support on a variety of issues that are important to you including opposing benefits for non-citizens. ** Support President Trump’s executive order “Preserving and Protecting the Integrity of American Elections.” This order requires that all mail-in and absentee ballots be received by Election Day, directs the DOJ to enforce laws prohibiting noncitizen voting, and instructs federal agencies to maintain accurate voter rolls. ** Answer the call from Speaker Johnson to mobilize our supporters and get 7,000 messages sent to Congress to help get the SAVE Act passed in the House of Representatives. The SAVE Act ensures only American citizens vote in American elections.** Launch calls-to-action in individual states to stop illegal immigrants from voting. ** And unite thousands of Americans around our “BAN NONCITIZEN VOTING” petition drive that calls for an amendment to the United States Constitution that expressly forbids illegal aliens from voting and from being counted in the census that determines congressional seats and electoral votes.
---
All our work is made possible by brave AMAC Action patriots. But with the stakes higher than ever, we need every conservative freedom fighter standing with us.That means YOU, friend.**[That’s why I hope you’ll click this link to support AMAC Action during this critical time for your sacred vote.](http://e.1600daily.com/rd/9z2z619iukqgjuchqm27065v2fqneo8uatfmu1dhdm0_rp22sh2s8i6eob4s1hm0o34c9h2bs)** We’ve got our work cut out for us as we fight to stop the 11.5 million illegals Joe Biden and Kamala Harris let into our country from:
---
* voting in our elections
* taking benefits reserved for you
* being counted the same as citizens when it comes to apportioning seats in the House of Representatives
* and being counted as citizens when it comes to giving states electoral votes.

---
Letting illegals change the balance of power in Congress and the electoral college means your vote may be fully **canceled out in all future elections**.The Left thinks they can pull a fast one on the American people.You, me and AMAC Action... we can’t let up for one minute. **[And that’s why I hope you’ll pledge your support for AMAC Action with your very best gift today.](http://e.1600daily.com/rd/9z2ze1sfrq4jt1b7887nil1fpunbhm9afpquuitdu4g_rp22sh2s8i6eob4s1hm0o34c9h2bs)** AMAC Action is taking every step possible to secure our elections because no matter what issue you care about the most – inflation, crime, the 2nd Amendment, Social Security and Medicare solvency, even the right to life......if you and I don’t have a vote... none of it matters. **[That’s what makes your best gift to AMAC Action so important today.](http://e.1600daily.com/rd/9z2zjv82ek1avlkok6nt0vo17tmd09g41cloj8vfbi0_rp22sh2s8i6eob4s1hm0o34c9h2bs)** So whatever critical amount you decide to return to help us secure our elections and your vote, please respond as soon as possible. We have an ambitious battle plan for the rest of the year—but first I must know that we have the resources to make it happen.I look forward to your response.
---
| | For Freedom,
---
**Rebecca Weber,_Chairwoman_****AMAC Action**
---
| [](http://e.1600daily.com/rd/9z2zte1rfck3rhcm3h8lophetg9n0uc7h5o47sp0fa0_rp22sh2s8i6eob4s1hm0o34c9h2bs)
---
| | P.S. [**To secure our elections and your vote once and for all please click this link or the button below to make your critical gift today.**](http://e.1600daily.com/rd/9z2z5rpushbpaekfdlr9rt6rjffcraif56rag9du5k0_rp22sh2s8i6eob4s1hm0o34c9h2bs) My friend, YOU are the difference between success and failure.
---
