name: Sharice Davids
subject: I’m proud to be the first openly LGBTQ+ Native American woman ever elected to Congress
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY SHARICE FOR CONGRESS
body:
If you’re a member of the LGBTQ+ community, please know that there are so many of us in Congress and across the country who support you

[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/108972545/549590681/-240735101?refcode=email-header&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NTc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlMGRiNTRkOC02MTQxLWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=OUQAkNZbLDwF0_hTP3THxY01VZRqe0HEjWSbTd2H5is=) Happy Pride Month! I couldn’t be more proud to serve as one of just a handful of openly LGBTQ+ voices in Congress. Together, despite the obstacles we face, we’re working towards a world where everyone can live openly, where our differences are celebrated, and where every American is afforded equal protection under the law, no matter who you are or who you love. I’m proud to be the first openly LGBTQ+ Native American woman ever elected to Congress. I’m proud that I’ve broken the mold of what a typical background is for a member of Congress. And I’m proud to work my heart out every single day to fight back against discrimination and injustice. If you’re a member of the LGBTQ+ community, please know that there are so many of us in Congress and across the country who support you. I understand that these times are difficult, but when we all stand together, I know we can keep pushing our nation forward toward equality. Thank you so much for your support, and happy Pride! \- Sharice P.S. If you believe that people like me belong in Congress, [_**I’d be grateful to have your support as we take on the big challenges that lie ahead.**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/108972546/549590682/277100410?refcode=email_060425_pride&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500,1000&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NTc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlMGRiNTRkOC02MTQxLWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=OUQAkNZbLDwF0_hTP3THxY01VZRqe0HEjWSbTd2H5is=) | | [Donate →](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/108972547/549590683/277100410?refcode=email_060425_pride&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500,1000&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NTc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlMGRiNTRkOC02MTQxLWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=OUQAkNZbLDwF0_hTP3THxY01VZRqe0HEjWSbTd2H5is=)

name: Wes Moore
subject: “No” and “Slow” vs. “Yes” and “Now”
body:
[ https://act.wesmoore.com/go/9?t=1001&akid=133%2E1086043%2Egdenck ]Wes Moore for Governor[ https://act.wesmoore.com/go/9?t=1002&akid=133%2E1086043%2Egdenck ]Wes Moore for Governor

Peter, with such
high stakes for the future, we need to act with urgency.

Gone are the days when Democrats are the party of “no” and “slow.” We must
be the party of “yes” and “now.”

Gone are the days when we are the party of bureaucracy, multi-year
studies, panels, and college debate club rules. We must be the party of
action.

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wes-em-20250604-1?refcode=em-20250604-1&refcode2=133_1086043_gdenck&akid=133%2E1086043%2Egdenck ]If you agree, will you rush a donation of $5 right now?

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wes-em-20250604-1?amount=5&refcode=em-20250604-1&refcode2=133_1086043_gdenck&akid=133%2E1086043%2Egdenck ]Donate »

Right now, the people of this country are calling on us to act.

The people want a growing middle class. They want access to work, wages,
and wealth. They want safe communities, accessible healthcare, and good
schools.

And the people cannot wait.

Peter, I don’t
come from a political background. My North Star isn’t the party line. It’s
the people.

The people guide my values, and it has helped us shape Maryland to be the great place that
it is. With the people as our North Star, we will be miles ahead of our
opponents.

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wes-em-20250604-1?refcode=em-20250604-1&refcode2=133_1086043_gdenck&akid=133%2E1086043%2Egdenck ]Together, we can help Maryland and the entire country thrive. But I
need you with me to accomplish this. Please chip in $5 or
whatever you can today.

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wes-em-20250604-1?amount=5&refcode=em-20250604-1&refcode2=133_1086043_gdenck&akid=133%2E1086043%2Egdenck ]Donate $5 ››

Elevate,

Wes

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wes-em-20250604-1?refcode=em-20250604-1&refcode2=133_1086043_gdenck&akid=133%2E1086043%2Egdenck ]Wes

You can unsubscribe from this mailing list at any time:
http://act.wesmoore.com/cms/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?akid=133%2E1086043%2Egdenck

* **TOP NEWS**

"**Texas Stock Exchange stands by 2026 debut,** " via Bloomberg wire**** - "[Texas Stock Exchange](https://substack.com/redirect/7c6ab9e5-7083-4dc5-a859-f4f4ba9e0182?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) is on track to make its debut as a trading venue early next year as its powerful rivals intensify their push into the state, said **Jeb Hensarling** , an adviser to the proposed marketplace and former U.S. congressman.

After the start of trading, the upstart exchange aims eventually to add dual listings, initial public offerings and exchange-traded products, Hensarling said Tuesday in an interview with Bloomberg Television. The TXSE is seeking federal regulatory approval as a national securities exchange [after applying earlier this year](https://substack.com/redirect/95e1ce0c-36d9-4fb5-8245-0919ea0962d0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).

TXSE is trying to carve out a niche as the New York Stock Exchange and Nasdaq fortify their footprints in the state. The NYSE, which recently moved its Chicago equities exchange to Texas, has announced a series of dual listings in recent weeks, while Nasdaq set up a regional headquarters in Dallas earlier this year.

Their sharpened focus underscores the state's efforts to attract more business and capital, said Hensarling, a Republican who led the House Financial Services Committee as a congressman from Texas. State lawmakers recently passed measures to strengthen a new system of business courts and raised the threshold for shareholders to start certain proxy fights.

In November, voters will weigh a ballot measure that would add a ban on financial transaction taxes to the state constitution.

"Texas is the place in America where people want to go to do business," Hensarling said." [DMN](https://substack.com/redirect/8fda4428-047b-4390-bd88-872d4d3e34b1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) ($)

TOP NEWS "Texas Stock Exchange stands by 2026 debut, " via Bloomberg wire** - " is on track to make its debut as a trading venue early next year as its powerful rivals intensify their push into the state, said Jeb Hensarling , an adviser to the proposed marketplace and former U.S. congressman. After the start of trading, the upstart exchange aims eventually to add dual listings, initial public offerings and exchange-traded products, Hensarling said Tuesday in an interview with Bloomberg Television. The TXSE is seeking federal regulatory approval as a national securities exchange . TXSE is trying to carve out a niche as the New York Stock Exchange and Nasdaq fortify their footprints in the state. The NYSE, which recently moved its Chicago equities exchange to Texas, has announced a series of dual listings in recent weeks, while Nasdaq set up a regional headquarters in Dallas earlier this year. Their sharpened focus underscores the state's efforts to attract more business and capital, said Hensarling, a Republican who led the House Financial Services Committee as a congressman from Texas. State lawmakers recently passed measures to strengthen a new system of business courts and raised the threshold for shareholders to start certain proxy fights. In November, voters will weigh a ballot measure that would add a ban on financial transaction taxes to the state constitution. "Texas is the place in America where people want to go to do business," Hensarling said." ($) FREE SUBSCRIPTIONS HAVE ENDED : As of July 11, 2022, we have moved to a paywall for free subscribers. Peter, here’s some good news:

Thanks to conservatives like you, we ended May by reaching our fundraising goal. Last-minute support on the 30th and 31st got us over the finish line.

_But here’s the catch:_

_**Jon Ossoff has at least _$11 MILLION_ in the bank.**_ We have a lot of ground to (still) make up if we’re going to have the resources to compete and win.

I don’t have to tell you what’s at stake. This is the #1 Senate race in the country, and Democrats, elites, and liberal megadonors are going to do everything they can to keep Georgia’s Senate seat blue.

_**The attacks are already coming, with more on the way.**_

[**That’s why we must stay on offense, and that’s why my team is launching a 48-hour fundraising drive to raise another $25,000.**](http://links.freedomtrainalert.com/u/click?_t=2d4f229f156142619b4e4f956d2d65c9&_m=9d0d2f63cb4a4ec19ff13259e7caf619&_e=8Vd5qC8hZVwInVBQS0r7DFt-Xo11mQ_UcleSsfCyobVTMiy2LxIAO8d2k38CGjKkpT_R9yNvr1JmskNk8LqYKWyaSjYIt7D2zA6ihFIi0uHII3Wx1a2w5zFlaLApafcV6kcBaEszXXLpc0bWtf5PK6CIMq_UmskmYSI54f56nd55wCn7d1n52o2Mv4WOUs9_bT6zTckx-iGDwBpsddt_u7a4AeHUHTDA9Ni2PsZ89AEGPOVHSHbPdSJl4TDsOXEd3rvZ4uMhtHSnkOaR6XKX35eipgWiPDLdyT_qxJF2WRXqkVXjT0cZ1ulIGIpAd82RHxLH11JOoPRr9eec7TUFXPiAEZPteTU6bkDojJ0MbDfLdMVHQmIhsb_5TC4mpE4U89k-c0e1rQNU7T-18zIACw%3D%3D)

Democrats know their policies have failed, so they’re going to raise and spend millions to distract voters and attack me. _**The only way they’ll win is if we don’t have the means to share my story and message.**_

We must keep up our foot on the gas, and especially for the next 48 hours. clean_body: Thanks to conservatives like you, we ended May by reaching our fundraising goal. Last-minute support on the 30th and 31st got us over the finish line. | Peter, here’s some good news: Thanks to conservatives like you, we ended May by reaching our fundraising goal. Last-minute support on the 30th and 31st got us over the finish line. But here’s the catch: Jon Ossoff has at least $11 MILLION in the bank. We have a lot of ground to (still) make up if we’re going to have the resources to compete and win. I don’t have to tell you what’s at stake. This is the #1 Senate race in the country, and Democrats, elites, and liberal megadonors are going to do everything they can to keep Georgia’s Senate seat blue. The attacks are already coming, with more on the way. Democrats know their policies have failed, so they’re going to raise and spend millions to distract voters and attack me. The only way they’ll win is if we don’t have the means to share my story and message. We must keep up our foot on the gas, and especially for the next 48 hours. Thank you, Maj. Gen. John King (retd.) | General John King is a soldier, lawman, and the first Hispanic elected statewide in Georgia history. He’s running for the Senate now because Georgians need a leader who will fight for them, fight for President Trump’s agenda, and put America First. | | | Paid For By John King For Senate Inc. This past weekend, Yassamin hosted a town hall with the folks from Opportunity Arizona in Republican Rep. David Schweikert’s district to hear directly from Arizonans about their concerns.
In case you missed it, Rep. Schweikert fell asleep during last week’s budget vote in Congress. But Yassamin was _wide awake_ in his district on Saturday — because even if Republicans and this administration won’t listen to voters, **Yassamin will.**
It was powerful to hear the voices and stories of the everyday people we serve — worries over childcare, housing, and healthcare, concerns about the attacks on trans kids and immigrants, and frustrations over Donald Trump’s complacency with foreign governments.
**[So we wanted to know, Peter: Will you tell us how you really feel and take our priorities survey today? Yassamin needs stories from people like you to carry with her and fuel the fight ahead.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/108972985/549592114/727184693?refcode=AU_YA_EG_GEN_SUR_AC_20250604_PM1_V1_S2&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83OTA5NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2NGFiNTdiMC02MjQxLWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=S2Y8zsna9i8eODqFFHNZ9H59-6P_g7hwFvAJlFKYTag=&emci=53ab57b0-6241-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&emdi=64ab57b0-6241-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&ceid=45157234)**

* **TOP NEWS**

"**Texas Stock Exchange stands by 2026 debut,** " via Bloomberg wire**** - "[Texas Stock Exchange](https://substack.com/redirect/d054cddb-40ed-4866-99fe-24cd67e958bd?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) is on track to make its debut as a trading venue early next year as its powerful rivals intensify their push into the state, said **Jeb Hensarling** , an adviser to the proposed marketplace and former U.S. congressman.

After the start of trading, the upstart exchange aims eventually to add dual listings, initial public offerings and exchange-traded products, Hensarling said Tuesday in an interview with Bloomberg Television. The TXSE is seeking federal regulatory approval as a national securities exchange [after applying earlier this year](https://substack.com/redirect/52b8868b-c499-474b-93f4-f59245de680d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).

TXSE is trying to carve out a niche as the New York Stock Exchange and Nasdaq fortify their footprints in the state. The NYSE, which recently moved its Chicago equities exchange to Texas, has announced a series of dual listings in recent weeks, while Nasdaq set up a regional headquarters in Dallas earlier this year.

Their sharpened focus underscores the state's efforts to attract more business and capital, said Hensarling, a Republican who led the House Financial Services Committee as a congressman from Texas. State lawmakers recently passed measures to strengthen a new system of business courts and raised the threshold for shareholders to start certain proxy fights.

In November, voters will weigh a ballot measure that would add a ban on financial transaction taxes to the state constitution.

"Texas is the place in America where people want to go to do business," Hensarling said." [DMN](https://substack.com/redirect/3d8834b5-8ed0-40eb-8b4a-32117c3f707a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) ($)

TOP NEWS "Texas Stock Exchange stands by 2026 debut, " via Bloomberg wire** - " is on track to make its debut as a trading venue early next year as its powerful rivals intensify their push into the state, said Jeb Hensarling , an adviser to the proposed marketplace and former U.S. congressman. After the start of trading, the upstart exchange aims eventually to add dual listings, initial public offerings and exchange-traded products, Hensarling said Tuesday in an interview with Bloomberg Television. The TXSE is seeking federal regulatory approval as a national securities exchange . TXSE is trying to carve out a niche as the New York Stock Exchange and Nasdaq fortify their footprints in the state. The NYSE, which recently moved its Chicago equities exchange to Texas, has announced a series of dual listings in recent weeks, while Nasdaq set up a regional headquarters in Dallas earlier this year. Their sharpened focus underscores the state's efforts to attract more business and capital, said Hensarling, a Republican who led the House Financial Services Committee as a congressman from Texas. State lawmakers recently passed measures to strengthen a new system of business courts and raised the threshold for shareholders to start certain proxy fights. In November, voters will weigh a ballot measure that would add a ban on financial transaction taxes to the state constitution. "Texas is the place in America where people want to go to do business," Hensarling said." ($) FREE SUBSCRIPTIONS HAVE ENDED : As of July 11, 2022, we have moved to a paywall for free subscribers. Free subscribers will only receive the top story in their morning email. Free subscribers will need to become paid subscribers to receive full story summaries. Sign up now and invite your friends and family to be part of the informed. | TOP NEWS “Texas Stock Exchange stands by 2026 debut, ” via Bloomberg wire** – “ is on track to make its debut as a trading venue early next year as its powerful rivals intensify their push into the state, said Jeb Hensarling , an adviser to the proposed marketplace and former U.S. congressman. After the start of trading, the upstart exchange aims eventually to add dual listings, initial public offerings and exchange-traded products, Hensarling said Tuesday in an interview with Bloomberg Television. The TXSE is seeking federal regulatory approval as a national securities exchange . TXSE is trying to carve out a niche as the New York Stock Exchange and Nasdaq fortify their footprints in the state. The NYSE, which recently moved its Chicago equities exchange to Texas, has announced a series of dual listings in recent weeks, while Nasdaq set up a regional headquarters in Dallas earlier this year. » » Their sharpened focus underscores the state’s efforts to attract more business and capital, said Hensarling, a Republican who led the House Financial Services Committee as a congressman from Texas. State lawmakers recently passed measures to strengthen a new system of business courts and raised the threshold for shareholders to start certain proxy fights. In November, voters will weigh a ballot measure that would add a ban on financial transaction taxes to the state constitution. “Texas is the place in America where people want to go to do business,” Hensarling said." name: Rashida Tlaib
subject: Re: Soon we’ll launch our Youth Fellowship program for 2025
body:
Together, we are empowering the next generation to fight for our shared future. | | |
---
Friend, In less than two weeks, when we convene our 2025 Youth Civic Engagement Fellowship, we’ll bring together 15 or more young people, ages 14 to 24, to learn from movement leaders and get hands-on-experience educating and mobilizing voters in their communities. As one of our fellows said during the last youth fellowship training:
> _“In the short time I’ve been in this fellowship I have witnessed immense love, strength, struggles, and power. Surrounded by my likeminded allies, I can clearly say this fellowship is one step in an ongoing marathon to success and prosperity.”_
Right now we’re interviewing fellowship candidates from a record pool of applicants (89!). Each fellow who completes the six-week training and 30 hours of field work will receive a $1,000 stipend. **How much we raise this week toward the 2025 Youth Fellowship will dictate whether we can accept more than 15 applicants.** [**Will you rush a donation now to power our work training the leaders of tomorrow, today?**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqtiu64DAa6R6mEIlUl6sWbtm4mR3rR5-gdsy2SqfTCd2E0qoB4Bqg7RkUJYOGNGAH_QNlsB_v451ZhoAFX1yloa7cvgDykNlgC1IYhh_PDpQxXRVRrG9ogqlKtHki7CWUJmWWDVdhAiazF9l0BQA4gkAdMhfdR7mnuSavVvSZ5qOMIHp6xBv72mjs24rnSjPganDKUqY4gniytWjZxpu2VOtjV637c7HDPwxhJQ8NUlkXnhEEeFB3wVi4KfLYX_Z48r5hGn0AiR_IV33Aymb1hBTTiR7OuvJ_BYDsGrdNMI3LEnp9Vp52NRVgBSaIcpFRtJ_nIe6AODnYbGmxMr3hwzHW7uoGXhrtIVovsRwBxDnbNCeUChCG9Mm_umx9u0SwZvMLLGD9PNrsIlFPHUxb8N/4h2/uItYRCGnTIGhS0TRHdOFJw/h0/h001.z-px9mGG-20TWk1rR1jskhWpVp53yzX5ibplvD38dXI) _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your secure donation will go through immediately:_ | | | [Chip in $15 now](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqtiu64DAa6R6mEIlUl6sWbtm4mR3rR5-gdsy2SqfTCd2I6SOeZ73Hj6aJ_BPqYbiuQ40TIjM97i7zDoBzL4cvWn89dKkISTwsxZYJ7mMTb2uYeaa3_SpiRQ6JDiEvD9PehBcBgNnIovIbpR4HjXbjlPTAVP_YMr_9tQ2BwdK0m-lEEU2eiUY5f8GEs8aeFDuyg9HjnDIHDYO3ObwBhUu960HbPShmrgI4vZc6dKTJTfljdYE1LF2buCY_1TpKDuBHW9yA2xjfN3LU7F-Vq9lGbpthfSkyBhvaG5ot8pbqyM9H0Zpc1hApqiVZ1p53kHytab-546i_xUXRe8CJ1Bco2r7R7hFjo_N_o5G143xBj9U_lxR4JPlqqmFGiOPQowxWYKKAOJYG-hkDwQ2VxvDeX8XFIunH2DYsoUa72GZjNoVXpkNlELtLa1eyB5Kwpn3Wtbkqhck6LSBDexkO6M_jcs/4h2/uItYRCGnTIGhS0TRHdOFJw/h1/h001.I5qysnvhamwrvN_KKC7hqzww6pnFLiXphXutszNUTmg)

\---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: **Rashida Tlaib******
Date: Tue, Jun 3, 2025
Subject: Soon we’ll launch our Youth Fellowship program for 2025
To: dpwillis67@gmail.com
Friend,
Young people are critical to the future of our democracy. **That’s why we’re training the leaders of tomorrow, today.**
On June 14th, we’ll launch our 2025 Youth Civic Engagement Fellowship where we’ll train young people over 6 weekends throughout the summer to learn from movement leaders and from each other. Fellows from 14 to 24 years old will build organizing and leadership skills through trainings and 30 hours of on-the-ground experience, after which they’ll receive a $1,000 stipend.
**We normally select 15 fellows, but this year we received a record number of applicants (89!).** That means we need to raise closer to $20,000 to cover the costs of this important program, which is free to participants, so we can train more fellows than ever before.
[**Will you help us expand the number of fellows we can accept into this year’s Youth Civic Engagement Fellowship with a donation today?**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqtiu64DAa6R6mEIlUl6sWbtm4mR3rR5-gdsy2SqfTCd2E0qoB4Bqg7RkUJYOGNGAH8M9WpfumceUByBxy7Gsiyck02hqMbMCun2CmpWqUeuzlAZABuAEhDI3nQEw87ZyO3z7aLMO1-F6Q8NLLVdhqUfZi64VGrT_WqKBIFBM_AfW3Vk1RyhKpfwnVuntzynGKT_ir8uc0dZ7aEXwQn6MMDrAxVdlhKGbxwXg1QACdOvCN5Jbdnl1FcWIbQ879QqZyFBdGe2tIPC7i-tKpur4yxdIEwwiB3BEPr1tYCw2l6fsg81XOGHvrk-pnx_N4vQCbzo1e9q4pdx2WtCG_w-zWUB6a4pAnb-XBIQ8U85cnxt7QYwmIs2vQu2qX7YPTbNdHIjW2QAaoF0BYLmXs3hleMA/4h2/uItYRCGnTIGhS0TRHdOFJw/h7/h001.p0Sm6D1foAkjxfHVqFX50Gpi1iM4WLMOXurkN3gB_B8)

We’re also partnering with other Democratic members of Congress to train more young leaders about how to mobilize their communities for change. The Democracy Summer program educates high school and college-aged young people in the struggle for democratic freedom, including supporting them with hands-on experience registering and connecting with voters.
**Following a summer of youth civic engagement, we won’t stop there.** From August 2025 through May 2026, we’re giving young leaders a real seat at the table, offering a chance for high school students to work alongside me and other policymakers to create meaningful change through the Youth Advisory Council. They’ll develop initiatives to address key concerns in their communities and advise me about what they want from me as their Congressmember.
I’ve developed legislation in partnership with young people before, including my _Youth Homelessness Guaranteed Income Pilot Program Act_ to address youth homelessness by providing monthly cash assistance. Young people with lived experience helped craft the bill to ensure that it meets the real needs of our unhoused neighbors.
**Every time I get to meet with my district’s youth, their curiosity, thoughtfulness, and determination fuel my soul and remind me why we are fighting for an equitable society where everyone can thrive.** I just spoke at a local high school’s graduation, which filled me with pride and hope. Young people are our next generation of leaders, and we can help them build political power today.

|

Peter, When voters at an Iowa town hall raised concerns that Medicaid and SNAP cuts could cause people to die, Iowa Senator Joni Ernst had the nerve to say: _**“Well, we all are going to die.”**_ This wasn’t a gaffe. Senator Ernst is saying the quiet part out loud. When Republicans like Derrick Van Orden talk about slashing Medicaid, they’re not trying to fix it -- they’re trying to gut it. Just like they want to slash Medicare by hundreds of billions, chip away at Social Security, and rip the rug out from under working families and seniors who’ve paid into it their whole lives. And they’re doing it so billionaires like Elon Musk and Peter Thiel who fund their campaigns can get another tax break. Meanwhile, regular folks are left rationing insulin or skipping doctor visits to keep the lights on. It’s truly sickening, Peter. **We’ve had multiple hospitals close in WI-03 in the last year. Thousands are uninsured. And still, Van Orden wants to take more away.** I believe every Wisconsin life is valuable regardless of your income -- and I won’t stop fighting until working people in Wisconsin can get the care they need to live, work, and age with dignity. name: Catherine Engelbrecht
subject: Standing Strong: The Trial Ahead, and the Future We Must Choose.
body:
| | | | June 04, 2025
---
| Hi Friend —
---
What follows is a broad update on recent lawsuits, developments out of D.C., and a powerful new program we’re launching later this week. Let’s jump in!
### **On Lawsuits— and Where to Draw the Line**
You may recall that a few weeks ago, True the Vote presented oral arguments before the 11th Circuit Court of Appeals in _Fair Fight v. True the Vote_. We previously won this case in federal district court, but Fair Fight appealed. Their lawsuit, filed in December 2020, was a clear attempt to block citizens from helping counties lawfully maintain clean and accurate voter rolls. The case is now in the hands of appellate judges. A decision may take months, but we remain confident in both our position and our right to engage in this essential work.
During that same season, three additional lawsuits were filed against us— all of which have since been won, withdrawn, or dismissed— but not without incurring significant collateral damage in the form of lost time and resources. That, of course, is the intended outcome of lawfare: not justice, but exhaustion.
This Friday, we return to court in _Mark Andrews v. True the Vote, Catherine Engelbrecht, Gregg Phillips, et al_ , a case filed in 2022 over a 1.8-second video clip used by the filmmaker of ‘ _2000 Mules’_.
Also in 2022, we were sued by Konnech, a CCP-aligned provider of U.S. election administration software. That case resulted in Gregg and me being imprisoned for protecting a confidential source, then released by emergency order of the Fifth Circuit. Konnech eventually dropped the case entirely. It’s making headlines again, following a newly published article by one of the researchers who helped us on the project.
| [| | | True the Vote @TrueTheVote |
---|---|---
Replying to@KanekoaTheGreat
Thank you for your unwavering support of this investigation and for using your platform to spread awareness. We brought Konnech to the FBI in 2021, ended up in prison for it in 2022. Many powerful players tried to destroy us in order to keep the cover-up alive.
| KanekoaTheGreat @KanekoaTheGreat 🚨EXCLUSIVE: @FBIDirectorKash & @FBIDDBongino: Expose the FBI and Gascón’s Konnech–CCP Election Software Cover-UpStrong evidence shows that corrupt FBI officials and former LADA George Gascón deliberately concealed CCP infiltration of U.S. election systems—because exposing it
---

### **But Wait... There’s More**
Only recently did we learn that, during that same period in 2022, a Grand Jury was convened against us in the Northern District of New York. The Biden Administration was seeking criminal indictments against Gregg and me. We were labeled “flight risks” by the U.S. Attorney, which allowed the case to be sealed— kept entirely hidden from us.
Without our knowledge, Google, Apple, and potentially others were subpoenaed and compelled to turn over every digital record we had, going back to 2017. We only learned of this after both companies sent us notifications, years later. Had they not, we likely never would have known.
I rarely speak about these experiences, because dwelling on them does not move us forward. Still, they must be shared— not for sympathy, but as a warning. They show how quickly a government can veer off course when left unchecked. And while lawfare is real— weaponized litigation intended not to win, but to wear you down— it’s just one front in a much broader battle.
### **Technology Moves Faster Than Truth**
Today, technology multiplies the speed and reach of abuse. Surveillance, censorship, data profiling, AI-generated narratives— these tools can be deployed instantly and invisibly. What once took years now takes seconds. What once required warrants now happens behind closed doors. If it can happen to us, it can happen to anyone.
We are entering a time for choosing. An era of both peril and promise. The technical reality of Voter Singularity— the resolution of identity, citizenship, and residency in real time— offers a path to transparent, accurate elections that remain locally controlled and citizen-led. We’re excited! But the very tools that can preserve liberty can also be used to destroy it. The line between empowering free people and surveilling them is razor thin— and far too easy to cross.
### **Two Stories, One Warning**
Last week, Louisiana became one of the first states to access the Department of Homeland Security’s enhanced[ SAVE database](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.UJcOIEsxQ3qr5lEWJDDC4j0zB6Fn0a9bbc4RdX_e19-08CNP8hBfS48G4oDrwJeXRfa6S7-GaIQ1CuIuWRHNdZ7y11bzJ_9nIlPaub7PIgW5js_7c6LbuUDZjsGMuf-C7KxJVCfxkJwdI85WKc33lOUy7r2L2CLTOCSI4n1sPtApkr69JEGz0zXeUAO185tf9D041mnH4-u7OSVi7MWFy6zp0fpNSe8MJ9rdxoHVOBJB-EJVT69HNbIAbHq6x96MkiJ1krg8N1sKx6a1Jao-St70dc4s7ebnOYoRRR9CnTo4gYYE0PqaYK-4dJX7qlex72eMoroyIr88pSfU7MT8_rIlclFHEMuLdT26yuT92ntDrq69bgg5MCouzwZtpRmJ/4h2/ygVtMscqQDmuxebfrg512w/h1/h001.NFsgRxh9DiS67udcgIy_ReNd0AJn_Qf5J9JaVv1_v1g)— a major step forward in enabling states to verify citizenship based on federal records. This is the kind of decentralized access states should have had long ago. Great news!
But in that same week, it was also reported that the Department of Government Enterprises (DOGE), which developed the enhanced SAVE system, has turned over vast amounts of data to [Palantir— a company with a clear agenda to build a centralized digital identity ](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.0WrCDK40KJSxvZRd_3SAdHq6uSiNySKKMiBeU7LLBnTRTzvzwDVAUNsIGWivhqXuQgJNJwLYMtStgPhHCk20dV41CWHEAHN-3-ip4n--e8kl8RsQQBtWea609Tk0jxhUu1-HjijqPfB-N3fpza5HWjEZQ_UGllchHL48ur_tSX7eh6_mvGzk6XvEh5uuekOzMKja1cXdNiVHdjgNzHytH_MW4II7qIDTokc_QThMTiMRbfYeqoZ_bLnpk767xgobYcnDJXydRMPb4sGYN5jX6USFAmFWi9pekOpNV5YoINhHkbrj3qJKH5cXKi63KxYqiotQcJl4koYLH4W4UNSCPoylKdWjUo_Gvy84LevxujpLSNTHwDoksFqR83L0t9dSZ3iXv0-TBEyrkW8qrfVTNSWoTZxPsTWGrbNSQTp253YiYHFDyxDCrNSX7MCv0TX_1gnb9dt0Sgtvz3NA1h_Epur6xBxPTxe1gOWZhDOYOALzvK0bgnx_s3hEH_yKcAltjkRt-r5vBrs6EMxHT7qEbQ/4h2/ygVtMscqQDmuxebfrg512w/h2/h001.eKcYatZyJ-AKEH2zUP6_tMQfk2geGKBziEnF1nlns6k)infrastructure, consolidating IRS, Social Security, immigration records, and much more, all in a single platform. Such centralization of data poses a threat to individual freedoms and privacy. Surrendering our data to unchecked power isn’t just a technical risk— it’s a moral failure. God didn’t grant us free will so we could trade it for convenience or fear.
### **We Are Many**
Technology is not neutral. It will either preserve our freedoms—or monitor our every move. But it can never replace what we have when we stand together. It can never replicate the strength of a free people, united in purpose, grounded in truth.
We are not powerless. We are not alone. **We are many.** And when we rise— together— we become a force no government, no algorithm, no agenda can silence.
So get involved. In your local elections. In your church. Your school. Your community. Choose one place to start. Shake a hand. Ask a question. Offer help. Build trust.
This is how we reclaim what matters. This is how freedom wins—not in sweeping headlines, but in thousands of small, everyday acts of courage by people who refuse to give up. Let them see that we are awake. That we are alert. That we are together—and by God’s grace, we are unafraid.
### **Introducing: LegiTrack**
Later this week, we’re launching **LegiTrack** , a new citizen tool designed to help you follow election integrity legislation in your state and in Washington, D.C.. With LegiTrack, you’ll be able to track bills, receive alerts, and take action when it matters most. We’ll host regular conference calls to break down legislative trends, explain how to make your voice heard, and equip you to spread the word when we need others to stand alongside.

name: Qasim Rashid, Esq.
subject: Did UnitedHealth Exploitation Result in Yet Another Preventable Death?
body:
22-year-old Cole Schmidtknecht's asthma medicine abruptly jumped from $55 to $700--unable to get the medicine he needed, he died 5 days later

# [Did UnitedHealth Exploitation Result in Yet Another Preventable Death?](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2550223&post_id=165166516&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY1MTY2NTE2LCJpYXQiOjE3NDkwNTY5NzcsImV4cCI6MTc1MTY0ODk3NywiaXNzIjoicHViLTI1NTAyMjMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.IHlLWjWwnL5dGeDkEvwAR-AfTarXSE8nEPoqISlak0U)
### 22-year-old Cole Schmidtknecht's asthma medicine abruptly jumped from $55 to $700--unable to get the medicine he needed, he died 5 days later
| | [Qasim Rashid, Esq.](https://substack.com/@qasimrashid)
---
| Jun 4
---
| [](https://substack.com/@qasimrashid)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2550223&post_id=165166516&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY1MTY2NTE2LCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc0OTA1Njk3NywiZXhwIjoxNzUxNjQ4OTc3LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMjU1MDIyMyIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.yuCpDV8nNf2D4VG6gLrJN7Rwho2-9NFAkY0bHeA8Q_E&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2550223&post_id=165166516&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY1MTY2NTE2LCJpYXQiOjE3NDkwNTY5NzcsImV4cCI6MTc1MTY0ODk3NywiaXNzIjoicHViLTI1NTAyMjMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.IHlLWjWwnL5dGeDkEvwAR-AfTarXSE8nEPoqISlak0U&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)

| | [](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9vcGVuLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9wdWIvcWFzaW1yYXNoaWQvcC9kaWQtdW5pdGVkaGVhbHRoLWV4cGxvaXRhdGlvbi1yZXN1bHQ_.N4k0IR4TpdXTo1ibfucD_YjNbS1vlepdmE0V1oRhJNw?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)

| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/qasimrashid/p/did-unitedhealth-exploitation-result?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

If you've followed my writing, you've seen my extensive coverage of how the health insurance industry exploits working people in this country--[including the preventable harm and suffering such corporations imposed on my own 9-year-old daughter](https://substack.com/redirect/cc482805-b0c1-4282-8ec2-450c8b77e3aa?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). Today, I write about another horrifying story--that of 22-year-old Cole Schmidtknecht. When Cole walked into his pharmacy to refill his asthma medication, he faced a shocking price tag. The inhaler that once cost under $70 had suddenly jumped to over $500. Cole, like millions of working-class Americans, didn't have an extra $500 lying around to spend on the same medicine he had always used to stay alive. So he left the pharmacy with a cheaper emergency inhaler and nothing else.
Five days later, Cole collapsed from a severe asthma attack. He never regained consciousness. He died. At 22 years old. From asthma. In the richest country in the world. Unsurprisingly, the corporation behind his denied care was once again UnitedHealth. **Let 's Address This.**
**Let's Address This with Qasim Rashid is a reader-supported publication. To receive new posts and support my work, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber.**
[ Upgrade to paid](https://substack..3Jp77kWyQjcVZgTlMi1G7pkXgCTjrwKx_WFA8OI2EtA?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=165166516)
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/9208dd65-369d-4564-af48-f004cda06da8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Cole Schmidtknecht, 22 years old
Let me be crystal clear: this was not an unavoidable tragedy. This was a profit-driven execution. Cole's death was preventable. But he was killed--killed--by a system designed to exploit, deny, and dehumanize working-class people for the benefit of billionaires.
I've said it before and I'll say it again: We don't have healthcare corporations in this country--we have HELL corporations--Health Exploitation Limited Liability. Their business model is built on a simple formula: deny care, cash in, and call it "cost savings." And once again, that is what happened here.
Cole's insurance coverage was managed by Optum Rx, a Pharmacy Benefit Manager (PBM) under UnitedHealth Group--yes, the same UnitedHealth whose CEO was allegedly killed by Luigi Mangione. Per the Schmidtknecht family, that price jump was beyond Cole's means, and he left the pharmacy with inadequate medication.
A HELL Corporation apparently knowingly denied a person medicine they knew he needed to live. Once again
We need to stop pretending this is normal. It is not. Meanwhile, [UnitedHealth has responded to the lawsuit filed by Cole's family by filing a motion to dismiss](https://substack.com/redirect/008d339c-f758-4720-8cae-38d399adf9fb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). That's right: in response to Cole's death because he couldn't afford the medicine he was prescribed, the multi-billion-dollar corporation involved is more concerned with escaping accountability than saving lives.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..3Jp77kWyQjcVZgTlMi1G7pkXgCTjrwKx_WFA8OI2EtA?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=165166516)
Remember, a Yale-Lancet study has already found that [68,000 Americans die every year](https://substack.com/redirect/902aa362-ab5f-45b7-a84c-f75976c45b9d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) simply because they lack access to basic healthcare. Let me say that again. Sixty-eight. Thousand. Every year. And yet our politicians, left and right, still submit themselves to the healthcare lobby and let these HELL corporations run our country.
How many more young people have to die? How many more parents have to bury their children before we finally recognize that healthcare is a human right--not a line item to be auctioned off for stock buybacks?
I say this with the full weight of personal grief. I lost my own cousin to a preventable asthma attack--under almost identical circumstances. In the U.S., asthma and allergies kill about 4,000 people every year. Most of those deaths are preventable. Yet we tolerate this violence as if it were an act of God, not an act of greed.
Meanwhile, the WSJ reports that that DOJ has opened a criminal fraud investigation into UnitedHealth's Medicare practices. This isn't an isolated incident. Last year, a five-year investigation into UnitedHealth's practices by the FTC found that, "_OptumRx, the group's pharmacy benefit manager, along with its two main peers, Express Scripts and CVS Caremark Rx, have pocketed an extra $7.3 billion over cost thanks to price gouging_ " of overcharging cancer patients by more than 1000%.
And let's not forget Walgreens, the pharmacy where Cole tried to get his medicine. The lawsuit filed by Cole's family alleges that Walgreens failed to notify him of affordable alternatives and didn't reach out to his doctor--two things the company was required to do. Instead, Cole was handed an emergency inhaler and sent home. That inhaler was found next to his bed. It was empty.
Now, UnitedHealth and Walgreens are offering their "deepest sympathies"--but only as they ask the court to dismiss the case.
No. That's not justice. That's a cover-up.
To every politician--Republican or Democrat--who refuses to declare healthcare a human right, I ask: What will you tell the Schmidtknecht family? What would you tell my family? That Cole should have picked a cheaper disease? That my cousin should have chosen a more affordable time to die?
Asthma doesn't care what political party you belong to. It will kill you all the same.
And now, as we brace for more corporate-sponsored carnage, I ask you to remember Cole's story. Remember his parents, who now bear tattoos of his memory, etched into their skin as permanent grief. Remember that this fight is not about theory--it's about survival. And if you're still not sure what side you're on, let me help you out: you're either with the profiteers or you're with the people.
Choose wisely. Because one day, it could be your child walking into that pharmacy. And walking out with nothing.
We need class solidarity to fight for a country where healthcare is not a privilege for the wealthy, but a human right for all.

name: Tina Kotek
subject: Reckless and mean
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Tina Kotek
body:
|
#
---
Reckless and mean. There’s really no other way to describe the cuts that Republicans in Congress want to make to Medicaid health insurance and SNAP food benefits. Their cuts threaten the health care of over a million Oregonians. Their cuts would rip away care for our loved ones with disabilities, older adults, and the youngest Oregonians. They would take food off the tables of our most vulnerable neighbors and families who need help paying for groceries. **[And why? All to hand that money over to the richest Americans. Will you stand with me and send a message to Congress to demand they protect Medicaid and SNAP?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/108975372/549600591/1071112159?refcode=AU_TK_EG_GEN_PET_AC_20250604_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiMDcwZjc3MC02NzQxLWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=8hv0bJirWeGSYW9Ke5l5vcmuVNrICZZCGP5H4rtj6SM=&emci=2340caf5-4541-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&emdi=b070f770-6741-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&ceid=15353729)** | [SIGN ON](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/108975373/549600592/1071112159?refcode=AU_TK_EG_GEN_PET_AC_20250604_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiMDcwZjc3MC02NzQxLWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=8hv0bJirWeGSYW9Ke5l5vcmuVNrICZZCGP5H4rtj6SM=&emci=2340caf5-4541-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&emdi=b070f770-6741-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&ceid=15353729)

name: Adam Schwarze, former Navy SEAL
subject: I hope Tampon Tim keeps talking
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Schwarze for Senate
body:
I’m a former Navy SEAL running for the U.S. Senate, and I believe in immigration enforcement and the deportation of criminal illegal aliens. That does not make me a Nazi. | [](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=ddd6ce3deb7a4254ade9026da8220368&_e=HWXgESYfttnv5I_l_-AAjlB1UhGh2_YpGi8bW_X1On3y-I7bItfQaDoo7A8YHt8qTsbzgQeDZqH4XsDQYbh2UTBqJwLPJN42EJQCxWdRcd3_-EXKrMOFJA5cfbY-at7qbZbkNBvK-sZJ2ufMyI9zctnB_w9lFUuExFHjqWKeeQ9zuaKwTvQTZDvTKjcveBX4fHTU3qjBVfvwkEqQGXaUGLcevI36mFVA-SNrEhEHB4Gg_SsnMfRtm5eK9dwafQsgtJBn4BP2RRPBaEqXo6d67gKtRtlPX2rbje0vT2gBZEnDv-dr5I1h2fcQSSWKFYUbxDlt_QmsMyOVgf81cg8ZnJXmLVDtXnwIQngzV4A3eXvl47kzVYa__d6IPtDD0Jt6DRGk3tvJroEdRvwqS5FGaA%3D%3D)

---
[](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=ddd6ce3deb7a4254ade9026da8220368&_e=HWXgESYfttnv5I_l_-AAjlB1UhGh2_YpGi8bW_X1On3y-I7bItfQaDoo7A8YHt8qTsbzgQeDZqH4XsDQYbh2UTBqJwLPJN42EJQCxWdRcd3_-EXKrMOFJA5cfbY-at7qbZbkNBvK-sZJ2ufMyI9zctnB_w9lFUuExFHjqWKeeQ9zuaKwTvQTZDvTKjcveBX4fHTU3qjBVfvwkEqQGXaUGLcevI36mFVA-SNrEhEHB4Gg_SsnMfRtm5eK9dwafQsgAZ_hSK3Z_w2vMSRGfHdqLOrMelF8wHmwwVVmOTaJCwEObm69YY14P0NeRATR0NEuY5XOHDeWPNzVrkEscIf_c6lpa_Aofbe5tDNJXgb8pjshPEaQui99Ouj_WpuGGhWg_8WDLpHV-q3skoiiSkpBHQ%3D%3D)

Peter,

My name is Adam Schwarze, and if this is your first time hearing from me, let me introduce myself.

I was born in St. Paul, Minnesota, in 1983 and adopted by Joel and Deb Schwarze when I was six months old. I grew up in Bloomington, where hockey was more than a pastime—it was a foundation for leadership and teamwork.

After the terrorist attacks on 9/11, I felt a deep calling to serve. **On my 18th birthday in October 2001, I enlisted as an infantryman in the United States Marine Corps. I deployed with the 1st Marine Division during Operation Iraqi Freedom, earning multiple valor and meritorious promotions awards.**

In 2013, I achieved my lifelong ambition by becoming a **Navy SEAL Officer, serving with SEAL Team Three and later joining the elite undersea-focused enterprise of U.S. Special Operations Command.** Over my 21-year military career, I deployed nine times to over 70 countries, conducting combat, intelligence, and humanitarian missions.

Now, I'm stepping up to serve Minnesota in a new capacity—as a Republican candidate for U.S. Senate.

**The Stakes in This Race Are Enormous.**

I'm running against **Tim Walz’s lieutenant governor** , who is even more radical than he is. While Minneapolis was burning during the 2020 riots, she wasn't defending the people or supporting the police—she was preparing a lawsuit _**against**_ the Minneapolis Police Department.

Governor Walz wants America to believe **Immigration and Customs Enforcement agents are the Gestapo.** His words, not mine. And this is who they call a “moderate.”

So ask yourself: _**If this is what their moderates sound like, what is their real agenda?**_

**Every time the left doesn't get their way, they call Republicans Nazis.**

Every time we demand border security or want criminal illegal aliens deported, they call us bigots.

But you know better—and so do I. Enforcing immigration law is not hateful.**It's common sense**. It's about **protecting Americans**.

Peter, if you're sick of being called a Nazi because you want a secure border, then **[I’m asking you to pitch in $1, $5, or even $15 today.](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=ddd6ce3deb7a4254ade9026da8220368&_e=HWXgESYfttnv5I_l_-AAjlB1UhGh2_YpGi8bW_X1On3y-I7bItfQaDoo7A8YHt8qTsbzgQeDZqH4XsDQYbh2UTBqJwLPJN42EJQCxWdRcd3_-EXKrMOFJA5cfbY-at7qbZbkNBvK-sZJ2ufMyI9zctnB_w9lFUuExFHjqWKeeQ9zuaKwTvQTZDvTKjcveBX4fHTU3qjBVfvwkEqQGXaUGLcevI36mFVA-SNrEhEHB4Gg_SsnMfRtm5eK9dwafQsgWEqb-zJH28x0yJHj_-ZsIM2UWnmzAXxLoomRAE0xmOLHyCQ6ZfWKlG6NuFKqu_9hPrRnUHMNL8KBaDDPdmmQtkwDzODNoOF6JTmu4H4ECDNv8mJhuZgjvUpTqxK3uDzu93bpwSCSWZPmhuSmQzb4zQ%3D%3D)**

[**SEND ADAM TO THE SENATE**](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=ddd6ce3deb7a4254ade9026da8220368&_e=HWXgESYfttnv5I_l_-AAjlB1UhGh2_YpGi8bW_X1On3y-I7bItfQaDoo7A8YHt8qTsbzgQeDZqH4XsDQYbh2UTBqJwLPJN42EJQCxWdRcd3_-EXKrMOFJA5cfbY-at7qbZbkNBvK-sZJ2ufMyI9zctnB_w9lFUuExFHjqWKeeQ9zuaKwTvQTZDvTKjcveBX4fHTU3qjBVfvwkEqQGXaUGLcevI36mFVA-SNrEhEHB4Gg_SsnMfRtm5eK9dwafQsgFYYg0YEeTrovuz0TXkH8ympW7ZwY62SP0z750ysGifRke-BoO0hTBjfTs31jDCDGOCZSVGwx6TMCmfIgMowEi13iU_2oWun7io4qBzz2FPWnwi27z3FY9yAGAt-3ERCT-YNgEjWbAScflK_SLx-ZpQ%3D%3D) **_
_**
[](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=ddd6ce3deb7a4254ade9026da8220368&_e=HWXgESYfttnv5I_l_-AAjlB1UhGh2_YpGi8bW_X1On3y-I7bItfQaDoo7A8YHt8qTsbzgQeDZqH4XsDQYbh2UTBqJwLPJN42EJQCxWdRcd3_-EXKrMOFJA5cfbY-at7qbZbkNBvK-sZJ2ufMyI9zctnB_w9lFUuExFHjqWKeeQ9zuaKwTvQTZDvTKjcveBX4fHTU3qjBVfvwkEqQGXaUGLcevI36mFVA-SNrEhEHB4Gg_SsnMfRtm5eK9dwafQsgG9eDQN2CMrxP-fCmFiDwKpm1daEKhCU_XwIuaUFIxY625mGAAddSHsrpuJwm26hfV2ZaJSOFn5P3Ndlj398zQaLmhu28BnJFOe9vJ2JzhE3PldPkPNt7GDJNAxHpM3CY_GsjrdnBJyA9lCWzbF0QAQ%3D%3D)

**Here's What I Believe:** I believe that no one is above the law—especially criminal illegal aliens who commit rape, murder, and violence against American citizens.

I believe in a **strong border, deporting those who break our laws, and respecting the rule of law** —no exceptions, no apologies.

I believe that veterans, police officers, working families, and law-abiding Americans deserve a voice in the Senate—not professional politicians who mock us and ignore our communities.

**I've Never Run for Office Before. That's the Point.**

Unlike my opponent, I wasn't groomed for politics. I joined the military on my 18th birthday—October 2001—just weeks after the attacks on our homeland. I didn't run from duty. I ran toward it.

While Tim Walz was dodging deployments, I was deployed nine times. I fought to protect conservative American values—the ones that raised me, the ones that made this country great, and the ones that are now under daily assault.

Tim Walz hand-picked my opponent in the U.S. Senate race to be his lieutenant governor. We can’t trust him due to his past actions in the military and the governor's mansion, so it should go without saying that we can’t trust her, either.

Minnesota hasn't gone Republican in a presidential race since 1976. But we're not a deep-blue state. Not yet.

With Senator Tina Smith retiring, this is our moment. The seat is open. The field is fluid. And I believe—with everything in me—that if we build now, we can flip this seat, and flip the narrative.

clean_body: I’m a former Navy SEAL running for the U.S. Senate, and I believe in immigration enforcement and the deportation of criminal illegal aliens. That does not make me a Nazi. | Peter, My name is Adam Schwarze, and if this is your first time hearing from me, let me introduce myself. I was born in St. Paul, Minnesota, in 1983 and adopted by Joel and Deb Schwarze when I was six months old. I grew up in Bloomington, where hockey was more than a pastime—it was a foundation for leadership and teamwork. After the terrorist attacks on 9/11, I felt a deep calling to serve. On my 18th birthday in October 2001, I enlisted as an infantryman in the United States Marine Corps. I deployed with the 1st Marine Division during Operation Iraqi Freedom, earning multiple valor and meritorious promotions awards. In 2013, I achieved my lifelong ambition by becoming a Navy SEAL Officer, serving with SEAL Team Three and later joining the elite undersea-focused enterprise of U.S. Special Operations Command. Over my 21-year military career, I deployed nine times to over 70 countries, conducting combat, intelligence, and humanitarian missions. Now, I'm stepping up to serve Minnesota in a new capacity—as a Republican candidate for U.S. Senate. The Stakes in This Race Are Enormous. I'm running against Tim Walz’s lieutenant governor , who is even more radical than he is. While Minneapolis was burning during the 2020 riots, she wasn't defending the people or supporting the police—she was preparing a lawsuit against the Minneapolis Police Department. Governor Walz wants America to believe Immigration and Customs Enforcement agents are the Gestapo. His words, not mine. And this is who they call a “moderate.” So ask yourself: If this is what their moderates sound like, what is their real agenda? Every time the left doesn't get their way, they call Republicans Nazis. Every time we demand border security or want criminal illegal aliens deported, they call us bigots. But you know better—and so do I. Enforcing immigration law is not hateful.It's common sense. It's about protecting Americans. Peter, if you're sick of being called a Nazi because you want a secure border, then Here's What I Believe: I believe that no one is above the law—especially criminal illegal aliens who commit rape, murder, and violence against American citizens. I believe in a strong border, deporting those who break our laws, and respecting the rule of law —no exceptions, no apologies. I believe that veterans, police officers, working families, and law-abiding Americans deserve a voice in the Senate—not professional politicians who mock us and ignore our communities. I've Never Run for Office Before. That's the Point. Unlike my opponent, I wasn't groomed for politics. I joined the military on my 18th birthday—October 2001—just weeks after the attacks on our homeland. I didn't run from duty. I ran toward it. While Tim Walz was dodging deployments, I was deployed nine times. I fought to protect conservative American values—the ones that raised me, the ones that made this country great, and the ones that are now under daily assault. Tim Walz hand-picked my opponent in the U.S. Senate race to be his lieutenant governor. We can’t trust him due to his past actions in the military and the governor's mansion, so it should go without saying that we can’t trust her, either. Peter, I'm asking you for something personal today—something I never thought I'd do: Minnesota hasn't gone Republican in a presidential race since 1976. But we're not a deep-blue state. Not yet. With Senator Tina Smith retiring, this is our moment. The seat is open. The field is fluid. And I believe—with everything in me—that if we build now, we can flip this seat, and flip the narrative. I Don't Have the Media. I Don't Have Hollywood. I Don't Have the Swamp. What I have is grit, a record of service , and hopefully, the support of patriots like you who still believe America is worth fighting for. If you're tired of watching cities like Minneapolis fall apart while liberal elites cheer it on... And if you believe we deserve leaders who've fought for this country— Then I'm humbly asking you to join my team today. Let's do this. Let's turn the tide. Let's win. With gratitude, Adam Schwarze Republican Candidate for U.S. Senate - Minnesota FOLLOW ADAM ON SOCIAL MEDIA | | Paid for by Schwarze for Senate Adam Schwarze is a former Navy SEAL and Marine. Use of his military rank, job titles, and photographs in uniform does not imply endorsement by the Department of the Navy or the Department of Defense.

body:
I'm running for re-election to the U.S. Senate in Colorado. | | [ ](https://url1005.email.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqsFYFs7_M8OcuIzmnVG3psyqefN3fD75otQjpjZg2BIP2KcBJuZZEsXXU0dBuZ-t5HGgOUkgM5eJV5IP63De-K-YxROt5Z4eEZMdPz5HZQaST6y8dbstmPpkO_oAzu500NIzaOAJoaYQO8-E01LxqJXByZnp149ff7BXZeU__06sYMLB8Y82KFWTxUKfTjvf3ZbP4CHdK4ficoLvSu7uV4Z7puQtVibRwMshEo4o56A3K5VZ4NdGgv0BMPjoLYajlfo_r-7EceGwbdBHnYu0nJeU8cb79vzPm8yl0mYzrrjkA/4h2/-9URSnWQQ129j62sJd2zmA/h0/h001.qS0-C02ozIs0nF5seq4kxNw3d0n0EUVKpHOikFwTEgs)

Hey Friend, I'm running for re-election to the U.S. Senate, and I hope you'll join me in the fight to give Colorado's priorities and values a voice in Washington.
[**Let me tell you more about this decision, then will you split a donation between my campaign and No Dem Left Behind****to help build this campaign from the ground up?**](https://url1005.email.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqsFYFs7_M8OcuIzmnVG3psyqefN3fD75otQjpjZg2BIP8LmBnyVHJxjnot42cKun2JF_6kqoLS08iC3Rt945Ag5W9MO0tuncPHQko8EHoDWoz7YCcvcMMGgDbTXSTTewKdor15RdFnFGp1ItfT0lHaaUmrGoedmSKw03kX_MdtEj9WGMmByUhxlWtsg3q4eO6VbpWRCelvNfVMfPoNVZy6potH8EC5UxOTzjITpdJzxWGBZ6p-_j_5PkpC46C87B53YX-IUuPSsRqVgL3hjYa06AB0R7iscm9mgNucHew432A/4h2/-9URSnWQQ129j62sJd2zmA/h1/h001.2xmZM3u530YL2VXHjvyqMFSQ-PsdRYX9pp3hcVzlKRk)
---
[ Donate now! ](https://url1005.email.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqsFYFs7_M8OcuIzmnVG3psyqefN3fD75otQjpjZg2BIPxlVXKiYFjorl7K--QubppgijHPvEJ4WnI88W8d5unkXWAAXnX5iG1Koroy9F6TTCkZBzFu3zUxYBEnbNI-E7zKsZA0RAAA0tHkcYSXGGLu0tBFua3atFrOQFfwTOjvsJExZwSmCepuB14OeQObSta8H62zUtjdC0Tnhx3CK1Owiy_cWLXVkqq7DmzXh38KjGZqYa-34AO6ZfZFxqCD2lYU6GEfNJvXSqEiRKImRWR7ZirC8_eZrbDf2N3AOWBFX1w/4h2/-9URSnWQQ129j62sJd2zmA/h2/h001.s9OJZRHCudfNZ74r7xviB7UafNCD7OT7n4MspsXk_pI)
---
Before diving into the world of politics, I took a chance, like so many small business owners do, and opened Denver's very first brewpub: Wynkoop Brewing Company. At Wynkoop, we were known for our unique beers like the Timberland Pale and Sagebrush Stout, which we offered at just $2.25 per pint - _a great deal compared to mainstream options!_ **Wynkoop was more than just a brewpub for me. It was a place where people with diverse viewpoints could come together, united by a shared love of good beer. It was a community where we proved time and again that there 's hardly anything that can't be sorted out over a refreshing pint.** Below are some nostalgic photos from the Wynkoop's early days!
---
| [ ](https://url1005.email.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqsFYFs7_M8OcuIzmnVG3psyqefN3fD75otQjpjZg2BIPzl5y2sf0slJta293-qSmcFtRMu8zJQfkFubhhXeTEOxJ38E5v9lSQv7nse0c11DwNhY6rsrk5cygcaVINw5Vt1ES1YUoS1MyP179WPK1O0zYD5VITXSQdzTvbZ9WDzNYAhopyEeEGTdNzBWTX_kRgh-r3cxfyzhWb-6Qp1-rxPNVQPWNaDlwsfSJmsrg3tNM3lSZEv9TRQTviQc630xhJQgF0uk0Ft1dCz0Q5qTJQGDYHj_T4mGU-D1DUf98XRamQ/4h2/-9URSnWQQ129j62sJd2zmA/h3/h001.qE-3bmMCNAIT_1jMOxSE1FHQiRTozklPP5U9rHYFvn0)
---
| [ ](https://url1005.email.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqsFYFs7_M8OcuIzmnVG3psyqefN3fD75otQjpjZg2BIP0Cy-09ykpHiPrdVfAA4-2PyZZCthz6qVbhpmq_Su8dYqro6fe19xCTimhhT8Y1Bb50hpEkrCPp1kbma28eCZPuWnMjp-w1jftIi6YDIjd486bbieNBaB7Y2gVRo1AT9i6TC7T2NVLfdUMmuGFRcG4PpjXKBA7bUamoVm2kCcOZVeUmcKkK2ZZ3O5JbSs2LTikiVOyHagOWNfA3RYO_IQsVCsebFt6ELjdsM-UYFo7xKTGxf7hfPdNEDNVSC5scOGA/4h2/-9URSnWQQ129j62sJd2zmA/h4/h001.m6kmpDUOOVssmSg1EiyLdBzSitIklriQyOj9ieg89Qc)
---
I brought that same commitment to collaboration and problem-solving to public service--first as Mayor of Denver, then as Governor, and now as your U.S. Senator. **And together, we 've gotten things done:**
* Lowering prescription drug costs and capping insulin prices
* Investing in clean energy and fighting climate change
* Defending reproductive rights against far-right attacks
* Boosting jobs and small businesses to keep Colorado's economy strong

**But there 's still so much more to do--and the stakes couldn't be higher. Trump and National Republicans are tearing apart our progress, and corporate special interests will spend millions to try and take this seat back.** [**That 's why I need you in this fight. Will you split a donation now of $5--about the cost of two Wynkoop pints--to help us build a winning campaign?**](https://url1005.email.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqsFYFs7_M8OcuIzmnVG3psyjio3OiBknLHFDeUw31RsS87Qfod58uHO8G_UFcvIlm8LiBwcp3RnJDbOtuivS_iYTe0cOWMU0bvyP7RLqZhHS9EJqepVa55FX2ai-kP_iZylVHf_mdHFgNCnk0kVxKfKtz8XrYrK_Tp9DnULYpnyZFaNNqVk8O_b3grM5QXDCUL9yqVUWPg9OBsr5KgpPySDLcmddHNTSmWqwIoAJNBsq6IWJTzHcYlpnnj1z3pKXt_dNXXc9iboKUPiz5Yi7NHERVM2vewLsYjJZRmyZVrJEw/4h2/-9URSnWQQ129j62sJd2zmA/h5/h001.NUKLD_rXfj2Bw8AOtEU8miYNeXBY-ws97BjnE8wZIxU)

body:

body:
Sixth wolf death of 2025 confirmed—CPW says ‘wolf population will continue to grow’
| | | | [](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
---
**PRESENTED BY[ROCKYMOUNTAINVOICE.COM](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
| | Hi folks,
Only a couple seats left! Join us for a powerful night in the Rockies with Eric Trump at the Mountain Majesty Gala—**[grab your seat](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** now before they’re gone.
Listen to today's Mountain Minute podcast [here](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.), or watch on [YouTube](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) or [Rumble](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)! Here is a summary of today's top stories:
🏔️ **Here in Colorado...** A [sixth wolf death](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) was confirmed by CPW, but officials insist the population is still growing. Meanwhile, a Fremont County rancher remains in limbo after [15 cattle mysteriously died in one day](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)—testing has yielded inconclusive results, and the quarantine remains in place. Tensions also flared after [Colorado Democrats hosted Rep. Ilhan Omar](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) at a fundraiser in Aurora just hours after the antisemitic attack in Boulder, drawing criticism from Jewish groups and Colorado Republicans alike.
🇺🇸 **On the National Stage...** A major 19-state sting uncovered Chinese-run sex-trafficking rings operating in places like Colorado. Many victims—mostly Chinese women—were lured under false pretenses, funneled through asylum loopholes, and trapped in a [$5 billion industry](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.).
🗣️ **Commentary Spotlight...** Ahnaf Kalam warns that Colorado’s permissive policies have [invited extremist violence](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.), pointing to the Boulder attack as a result of woke ideology run amok. Michael Hancock urges Americans to resist rising authoritarianism—not with chaos, but by [restoring trust and civic courage](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.). And Montrose Commissioner Sean Pond draws a hard line [against federal land grabs](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.), calling on local leaders to defend Colorado’s autonomy before it's too late.
This is the Mountain Minute for Wednesday, June 4, 2025.
– Team RMV
---
|
---
**TODAY'S TOP STORIES**
**[Sixth wolf death of 2025 confirmed—CPW says ‘wolf population will continue to grow’](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [DENVER7](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
Another gray wolf that was brought to Colorado as part of the state’s reintroduction program has died, Colorado and federal officials said on Monday afternoon.
In a press release on Monday, Colorado Parks and Wildlife (CPW) said they received a mortality alert for a male wolf in northwest Colorado on May 31. The wolf had been brought to Colorado from Canada as part of the January 2025 reintroduction, CPW confirmed to Denver7. It is the fifth wolf from the original 15 released that month that has died.
As with any wolf death in Colorado, the U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service is investigating because gray wolves are a federally listed species under the Endangered Species Act. The USFWS will determine its cause of death. That investigation is ongoing.
No other details were available from either agency, however CPW added that it is continuing to monitor four potential den sites.

**[Montrose Commissioner Pond: Why I’m Drawing the Line and Standing Against Federal Land Grabs](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
* Montrose County Commissioner Sean Pond is sounding the alarm over two major federal land proposals—the GORP Act and the Dolores River NCA—that would restrict access, harm rural economies, and limit land use in Western Colorado.
* Pond argues the 730,000-acre GORP Act, backed by Senators Bennet and Hickenlooper, will hurt ranchers, energy workers, and outdoor recreation by banning motorized access and curbing wildfire mitigation efforts.
* He’s urging Coloradans to speak out, get involved locally, and push back against what he calls long-term activist pressure and federal overreach threatening Colorado’s freedom and economic stability.

**[Kalam: Colorado’s Woke Zealotry Ushers Islamist Terror into Boulder](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
* Commentary by Ahnaf Kalam argues that Colorado’s progressive sanctuary policies and restrictive gun laws created the conditions that allowed the Boulder terrorist attack to occur.
* He links the incident to a broader “red-green alliance” between far-left ideologues and Islamist extremists, warning that political rhetoric and lax immigration enforcement have consequences.
* Kalam criticizes state leaders like Governor Polis and State Senator Iman Jodeh for enabling extremism and failing to properly condemn anti-Israel violence.

**[Hancock: Saving Liberty in an Age of Manufactured Tyranny](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)****BY MICHAEL HANCOCK****| COMMENTARY | JUNE 3** _There is a form of political ventriloquism occurring in America. The left warns, with breathless urgency, that Donald Trump is a dictator in waiting, a Mussolini in a red tie, poised to crush democracy with a golf swing and a sneer._
__
**[Ganahl: Congress must back DOGE to cut waste and energize GOP voters for 2026](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)****BY HEIDI GANAHL | COMMENTARY | JUNE 2** _Under President Donald Trump, the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE)—led by Elon Musk—is lighting a fire under Republican voters ready to rip out Washington’s waste at the roots._
__
**[O’Donnell: One in 20 workers is a state employee—who’s footing the bill?](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)****BY MIKE O'DONNELL | COMMENTARY | JUNE 2** _Communism, socialism, Marxism, Maoism, post-Mao Chinese-ism, and fascism may wear different uniforms, but they all march to the same beat—state control. One-party rule, diminished freedoms, political prosecutions, judicial overreach, hostility to markets, and the slow suffocation of private enterprise under the weight of public bureaucracy._
__

Join us for a powerful night in the Rockies with Eric Trump at the Mountain Majesty Gala— now before they’re gone. Listen to today's Mountain Minute podcast , or watch on or ! Here is a summary of today's top stories: 🏔️ Here in Colorado... A was confirmed by CPW, but officials insist the population is still growing. Meanwhile, a Fremont County rancher remains in limbo after —testing has yielded inconclusive results, and the quarantine remains in place. Tensions also flared after at a fundraiser in Aurora just hours after the antisemitic attack in Boulder, drawing criticism from Jewish groups and Colorado Republicans alike. 🇺🇸 On the National Stage... A major 19-state sting uncovered Chinese-run sex-trafficking rings operating in places like Colorado. Many victims—mostly Chinese women—were lured under false pretenses, funneled through asylum loopholes, and trapped in a . 🗣️ Commentary Spotlight... Ahnaf Kalam warns that Colorado’s permissive policies have , pointing to the Boulder attack as a result of woke ideology run amok. Michael Hancock urges Americans to resist rising authoritarianism—not with chaos, but by . And Montrose Commissioner Sean Pond draws a hard line , calling on local leaders to defend Colorado’s autonomy before it's too late. This is the Mountain Minute for Wednesday, June 4, 2025. – Team RMV | TODAY'S TOP STORIES Another gray wolf that was brought to Colorado as part of the state’s reintroduction program has died, Colorado and federal officials said on Monday afternoon. In a press release on Monday, Colorado Parks and Wildlife (CPW) said they received a mortality alert for a male wolf in northwest Colorado on May 31. The wolf had been brought to Colorado from Canada as part of the January 2025 reintroduction, CPW confirmed to Denver7. It is the fifth wolf from the original 15 released that month that has died. As with any wolf death in Colorado, the U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service is investigating because gray wolves are a federally listed species under the Endangered Species Act. The USFWS will determine its cause of death. That investigation is ongoing. No other details were available from either agency, however CPW added that it is continuing to monitor four potential den sites. | DON'T MISS OUT ON THE MOUNTAIN MAJESTY GALA —LIMITED TICKETS AVAILABLE 🎟️ | Montrose County Commissioner Sean Pond is sounding the alarm over two major federal land proposals—the GORP Act and the Dolores River NCA—that would restrict access, harm rural economies, and limit land use in Western Colorado. Pond argues the 730,000-acre GORP Act, backed by Senators Bennet and Hickenlooper, will hurt ranchers, energy workers, and outdoor recreation by banning motorized access and curbing wildfire mitigation efforts. He’s urging Coloradans to speak out, get involved locally, and push back against what he calls long-term activist pressure and federal overreach threatening Colorado’s freedom and economic stability. | The Rocky Mountain Voice is a 501(c)(3) that exists to promote informed civic engagement and the principles of self-governance through enterprise reporting on public policy, politics, government, and culture throughout the state of Colorado. The only way we can continue to spread truth is with your support! | Commentary by Ahnaf Kalam argues that Colorado’s progressive sanctuary policies and restrictive gun laws created the conditions that allowed the Boulder terrorist attack to occur. He links the incident to a broader “red-green alliance” between far-left ideologues and Islamist extremists, warning that political rhetoric and lax immigration enforcement have consequences. Kalam criticizes state leaders like Governor Polis and State Senator Iman Jodeh for enabling extremism and failing to properly condemn anti-Israel violence. | ONLY ON ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE ⤵️ MICHAEL HANCOCK| COMMENTARY | JUNE 3 There is a form of political ventriloquism occurring in America. The left warns, with breathless urgency, that Donald Trump is a dictator in waiting, a Mussolini in a red tie, poised to crush democracy with a golf swing and a sneer. HEIDI GANAHL | COMMENTARY | JUNE 2 Under President Donald Trump, the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE)—led by Elon Musk—is lighting a fire under Republican voters ready to rip out Washington’s waste at the roots. MIKE O'DONNELL | COMMENTARY | JUNE 2 Communism, socialism, Marxism, Maoism, post-Mao Chinese-ism, and fascism may wear different uniforms, but they all march to the same beat—state control. One-party rule, diminished freedoms, political prosecutions, judicial overreach, hostility to markets, and the slow suffocation of private enterprise under the weight of public bureaucracy. __ Feel like you have a unique story or commentary to share? We'd love to talk with you about becoming an RMV Contributor. Reply to this email (or reach out to mailto:tori@rockymountainvoice.com?subject=Contributor%20Interest%20\(via%20Mountain%20Minute\)) or send us a message on social media to chat! | | 🥘 DID YOU KNOW? Unlike many countries, the United States has no official national dish —not even apple pie, burgers, or hot dogs. While these foods are iconic, no single one carries the title. However, plenty of states have claimed their own official foods for locals and visitors to enjoy. | CARTOON OF THE DAY | QUOTE OF THE DAY "Good intentions will always be pleaded for every assumption of authority. It is hardly too strong to say that the Constitution was made to guard the people against the dangers of good intentions. There are men in all ages who mean to govern well, but they mean to govern. name: Eric Sorensen
subject: Be yourself 🏳️‍🌈
body:
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/108976382/549604407/-1812448514?refcode=em-6.4.25-all-a&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTMvMS85MzkwNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmOGU2OTY4MS02YTQxLWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=JOzcxSHfxoXntlBYq0apOhHT8vcKx_IPTE9JgVQ_6wU=)
Happy Pride Month, Peter.
Not a day goes by where I don’t feel humbled, and honored to serve openly and honestly as Illinois’ first out LGBTQ+ member of Congress.
Because the truth is, no matter what title I hold today, it wasn't long that I was just a gay kid named Eric who never really believed I'd be accepted as a TV meteorologist -- let alone as a congressional “trail blazer.”
I still vividly remember the words from management at my first job as a meteorologist in East Texas:
**“We can’t have a gay meteorologist at channel 7, Eric.”**
Being pushed out of my job was horrible, and something too many members of our community can relate to. But I was lucky – I persevered, and after job searching and soul searching, I got the opportunity of a lifetime -- to serve my hometown of Rockford, Illinois -- where I could be my truest, proudest self.
The rest, as they say, is history.
**So, yeah, their hate may get louder this time of year. But our diversity is strength and our unity of purpose is power. And they will never have that!**
And to those struggling this Pride Month, know that there’s hope. Whether it's your 10th year openly celebrating, your first, or maybe you're not ready to just yet -- know that you are loved and that I'm proud of you. Together, we will come through everything they throw at us stronger than before.
Thanks for your time and support, and Happy Pride Month! 🌈
Eric
[Donate](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/108976383/549604408/-1812448514?refcode=em-6.4.25-all-a&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTMvMS85MzkwNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmOGU2OTY4MS02YTQxLWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=JOzcxSHfxoXntlBYq0apOhHT8vcKx_IPTE9JgVQ_6wU=)
---

Eric Sorensen for Illinois
P.O. Box 1172
Moline, IL 61265
United States

I’m running for the U.S. Senate in Georgia because President Trump needs backup and Jon Ossoff is AWOL The Federal Election Commission's filing deadline for the runoff is tomorrow ... we have LESS THAN 24 HOURS TO HIT OUR FUNDRAISING GOAL |
[](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/u/click?_t=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_m=69ab20f1372147cc9b06e93895ebae65&_e=PxdknqsjI9kS04kPuo_IvzqIdhrA-laz-QiHcIsmVKDdN0ZzAJQdFl-W7CQiU43Ic7tXKdT4XFMy5tfOj9LyMBDYYcRw5GN1YsT5LsnrpCpvSn2YAl8Ojre0DgNLzRw8Mjf6fIsTfFXUdrGJtv3XgG9RgWxuVmzJawTGeIhD_RgoHjGdIYu7t4veUQaU1QVBGuhp-GU17vzuszrcU5fEVVxm-n4oV6M4Xw2W6L1wQTScr163JjgrP6mWZUdiVb__DwHk3ytmo__z_bcftC_KmbfvEyFIzJEiJFrxCDzA1EA7WxIlNWko1lfv8Z6laekgDYDnV2Ee16FYpYz3nCTJBISw3ICo6LCERD8Fn0oXSrJKct93iK4TrOisqJoLk7-Nke5iCGlEYm2ycwkCfEfcyb84B0O8ikZEVlNGvEjUhMQ%3D)
