The George Soros elected, Pro-Criminal, Trump-Deranged DEI hire that has turned my beloved New York City into a third-world Hellscape.If you think that sounds too dramatic, just look at a few examples of what Alvin Bragg’s “weak-on-crime,” Trump-obsessed tenure has brought to the law-abiding citizens of New York:
---
* An innocent woman set on fire by a Guatemalan Illegal Alien who had already been deported at least once before...
* A mob of Illegal criminals caught on video assaulting two New York City Law Enforcement officers outside a “Migrant Shelter,” all **released without bail by Alvin Bragg!**

---
Instead of working for the last 4 years to make the Citizens of New York safe and secure, Alvin has been on a singular, sick, twisted mission and one mission only -- TAKE DOWN DONALD TRUMP.That’s what he ran on...Those were his marching orders from George Soros and his Radical donors...And those are orders he dutifully followed -- at the expense of not only those of us who live in New York, but every single American that deserved to vote in a fair election not tainted by a ridiculous set of trumped-up charges made up out of thin air.New Yorkers deserve better...Americans deserve better.That’s why -- whether you live right here in Manhattan, a thousand miles away in Des Moines, Iowa, or anywhere else in our great country -- I hope you’ll help me defeat Alvin Bragg. **[Would you consider helping my campaign with a one-time $28 contribution today?](http://links.freedomtrainalert.com/u/click?_t=2d4f229f156142619b4e4f956d2d65c9&_m=28df36e931354f4a836fe5e103170fa2&_e=7eSE7H34lh94ZlOADYnG0gv-dvIBXQ8MsIBQwZNS8FL08rO8tj3P4pPld9X7K3XAVZNaesagEzUVExJpUfwOV-nv7ZLYbirUFoQqe0XmzWk4hj8CGSWk5XOnyYfoseXjjmyfALw4HdwZcDqBoT3uIC2h1woruTQnmTcSMMN9Ll7_OItBN_4tbu1X-skjdTjnLB1WRiRjkdGUnf9QK2_4ESJ6t0uKakG-KTghoHwhD4qgqM6iwRRhLHJvmzqj6VdDk97cl7aQPutSrD9KfnV9dCqWQ6of0B7uywF6o8q13ulV7zN9905tLoG8RyzHxDfPLXTQMTVZMs0daInO5dE3LiCMQDtGXNrHh1rPRltMPG_oXBwItQ_HBcbasr5PeXGnpr9HQTs6v96328csZAh2M-E4d-HgQGZyUmiJvzCqSNM%3D)** I could really use your help.Alvin Bragg has millions of dollars pouring into his campaign by the same Radicals who put him there the first time around...
---
* George Soros
* Hollywood Celebrities
* Elites right here in New York and Hollywood
* The deep pockets of the Democrat election machine

---
I don’t have any of that on my side -- but I hope I’ve got YOU on my side.To have any chance of beating the Democrat machine funding Alvin Bragg’s run for re-election, I need a nationwide army of Americans to rally by my side...A nationwide army of Americans who understand how toxic it is having a Radical like Alvin Bragg in charge of the criminal justice system of one of our largest cities...Who know how close Alvin Bragg came to knocking Donald Trump out of the 2024 Presidential election and what that would have done to our country... Who watched in disgust as Alvin Bragg conjured up a 34-count felony conviction against a man whose only real crime was trying to Make America Great Again!This is our chance to send a clear message not only to the people of New York -- but the nation -- that these Radicals put into place by George Soros to do his bidding are not invincible...That they CAN be defeated! Alvin Bragg is the perfect example of why I left the Democrat party.I think it all started when my 11-year-old got COVID... We were told to mask in the house and give my son meals in his room for 10 days. Instead, we cuddled up & watched TV together -- unmasked in the living room and ate our meals as a family at the dinner table. That was a watershed moment for me.What were we doing?When did the United States of America turn into a place where the Government told us how to live?When I dared to suggest that it may not be the best idea for biological boys to participate in girls’ sports, I was all but run out of town on a rail... I know how much they are getting wrong. And how bad it is for our children. **[That’s why I’m asking good folks like you to help me.](http://links.freedomtrainalert.com/u/click?_t=2d4f229f156142619b4e4f956d2d65c9&_m=28df36e931354f4a836fe5e103170fa2&_e=7eSE7H34lh94ZlOADYnG0gv-dvIBXQ8MsIBQwZNS8FL08rO8tj3P4pPld9X7K3XAVZNaesagEzUVExJpUfwOV-nv7ZLYbirUFoQqe0XmzWk4hj8CGSWk5XOnyYfoseXjjmyfALw4HdwZcDqBoT3uIC2h1woruTQnmTcSMMN9Ll7_OItBN_4tbu1X-skjdTjnLB1WRiRjkdGUnf9QK2_4ESJ6t0uKakG-KTghoHwhD4qgqM6iwRRhLHJvmzqj6VdD8qaydHKV0tn7VUSw6d-6D5woP-71dQYGOyFMrth4l6yNGKUnctSd_TertTYQsUbvramc-exfB_2gZh7vInUX2RybajhWlbJSRSQWD8AImzPf8zURzJRH_4bLvX2Xe1Iwcv-UHajcdWbXkvtQjYmLV0Hl2t6pjqUM7EhpXOKIO3o%3D)** It’s going to take all of us to take down Alvin Bragg -- but this has been a season of political miracles!I humbly ask for your help today. I hope you’ll find it in your heart to send a one-time $28 contribution to help me pull off one more political miracle......and send Alvin Bragg back to obscurity where he belongs.He’s bad for New York...He’s bad for America...And he needs to go.One last favor...I know not everybody is going to be able to give that I write to today. **[So if you think you could spare a larger gift -- of $50, $100, $250, $500 or even $1,000 or more, a few of those would really help right now.](http://links.freedomtrainalert.com/u/click?_t=2d4f229f156142619b4e4f956d2d65c9&_m=28df36e931354f4a836fe5e103170fa2&_e=7eSE7H34lh94ZlOADYnG0gv-dvIBXQ8MsIBQwZNS8FL08rO8tj3P4pPld9X7K3XAVZNaesagEzUVExJpUfwOV-nv7ZLYbirUFoQqe0XmzWk4hj8CGSWk5XOnyYfoseXjjmyfALw4HdwZcDqBoT3uIC2h1woruTQnmTcSMMN9Ll7_OItBN_4tbu1X-skjdTjnLB1WRiRjkdGUnf9QK2_4ESJ6t0uKakG-KTghoHwhD4qgqM6iwRRhLHJvmzqj6VdD_0GvFclBVIi7dNF2GSsm1hgD-XVqXzddEoApK4DGpTzUXwp3tk4TpgAeXXOuN9t7uZBVkk1i-dCKpCSDP4du8GK7LJsckFKbX51RmLoYqDOvbxvXO-N2gSYHIBTWAlyCAGp82EZ_r6aoFYchZfFPkVvo4sV9TLMO4kTP2Zsb-fQ%3D)** We need a good, strong launch -- and without a few of those larger gifts, we’ll already be behind.Please just send what you can, My Friend.Just reading my email today is a tremendous favor, and I really appreciate it.Thank you, Wish Me Luck, and God Bless You!

Donald Trump wants access to your Social Security records.

**Yes, you read that right.** While a federal judge has blocked this overreach, Trump is now urging the Supreme Court to reverse that ruling and grant DOGE – still active despite Elon Musk's departure – full access to _your_ data.

This isn’t about “government efficiency.” It’s another case of Trump wielding his unchecked political power. This time, it’s putting your privacy, your future, and your Social Security at risk.

Over 70 million Americans – including seniors, veterans, and people with disabilities – depend on Social Security. They’ve earned these benefits, and they _deserve_ to know their information is protected.

Instead of strengthening this vital resource, Trump is trying to seize power over our Social Security data – despite court findings that DOGE doesn’t even need this data to do its “job.”

**This fight is happening right now.** The Supreme Court could decide at any moment whether to hand over your personal information and the very resources so many have worked a lifetime to earn. We must stand together and send a clear message: our data must be protected, not exposed.

clean_body: Donald Trump wants access to your Social Security records. Yes, you read that right. While a federal judge has blocked this overreach, Trump is now urging the Supreme Court to reverse that ruling and grant DOGE – still active despite Elon Musk's departure – full access to your data. This isn’t about “government efficiency.” It’s another case of Trump wielding his unchecked political power. This time, it’s putting your privacy, your future, and your Social Security at risk. We’re gathering urgent feedback from voters, and we’re counting on people like you to speak up for OH. | | Over 70 million Americans – including seniors, veterans, and people with disabilities – depend on Social Security. They’ve earned these benefits, and they deserve to know their information is protected. Instead of strengthening this vital resource, Trump is trying to seize power over our Social Security data – despite court findings that DOGE doesn’t even need this data to do its “job.” This fight is happening right now. The Supreme Court could decide at any moment whether to hand over your personal information and the very resources so many have worked a lifetime to earn. We must stand together and send a clear message: our data must be protected, not exposed. | Thank you for making your voice heard, Team Gillibrand * = A note from Kirsten: Friend, I am truly honored to have won reelection, and I promise to continue the critical work toward bipartisan solutions for New Yorkers as we enter this new term. While we had many victories in New York this election season, our fight to retake control of the Senate is far from over. = Thank you, Kirsten Gillibrand

name: Elizabeth Warren
subject: It seems that every Congressional Republican has had their spine removed.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Warren for Senate
body:
We’ve done this before.

| | | [ ](https://act.elizabethwarren.com/go/216?t=3&akid=2561%2E4032634%2EiDb8QT)
---
It feels like ancient history now, but when he was running for president last year, Donald Trump promised over and over that he would lower costs “on Day 1.” After he was elected and an interviewer pointed out that his policies would drive up costs, Trump said he “couldn’t care less.” And now, it’s Day 135 and costs are up. People are paying more for gasoline. More for housing. More for groceries. More for eggs. More for baby strollers -- or as Donald Trump calls it, “the thing you carry the babies around in.” So what are Trump and the Republicans doing about that? Working on ways to make costs even higher. His rather Big and Ugly Bill will rip health care away from 14 million people. It will slash programs that help people pay for their groceries. It will cancel clean energy programs. And instead of standing up and saying what Trump is doing is wrong, Republicans in Congress are suffering a major medical emergency. It seems that every one of them has had their spine removed. Evidently, the only movement that Congressional Republicans can now perform is to bow low, to kiss the Earth beneath the feet of Donald Trump. [**Our fight to make our government work for everyone — not billionaires — is going to be tested. This is a moment to throw everything we’ve got into this fight. And that’s what I’m going to do. If, and only if, you’re able, please pitch in $28 or anything you can to support our campaign that fights for working families.**](https://act.elizabethwarren.com/go/3019?amount=28&t=8&akid=2561%2E4032634%2EiDb8QT) This is a critical moment in our country. This is the moment that Democrats have the chance to show the American people who we are and what we will fight for — and to throw everything we’ve got into stopping this plan from passing. And I’m going to fight hard for this. And every single Republican who will vote to raise costs for families to finance tax giveaways for billionaires must be held accountable at the ballot box — and that work starts now. We’ve done this before. Back in 2017, Donald Trump and the Republicans tried to rip health care away from Americans. John McCain famously voted no at the last second, and we saved health care for millions of Americans. That was a huge victory, but there was more. The energy from that fight helped build a massive blue wave in the midterms that put Democrats back in control of the House and set us up to beat Trump and the Republicans in 2020. We have that same opportunity today. **Trump’s Big Ugly Bill is not law. Not yet. It still has to go through the Senate, and it may have to go back through the House again. So now is the time to raise hell.** [**If, and only if, you’re able, please pitch in $28 or anything you can to power our fight for working people in the Senate.**](https://act.elizabethwarren.com/go/3019?amount=28&t=9&akid=2561%2E4032634%2EiDb8QT)

name: Dexter HQ
subject: They are coming for Medicaid.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Maxine for Congress
body:
https://maxinefororegon.com/ [https://maxinefororegon.com/]
Peter,
As a doctor, Maxine doesn’t just fight for her patients—she fights on behalf of every American, whether she’s in the hospital or Washington, D.C.
Now, the GOP wants to bankroll billionaire tax cuts by ripping health insurance away from millions of Americans.
They passed a bill to cut billions from programs that working families rely on—all to fund trillions in tax breaks for the ultra-rich.
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_med_email-25q2?refcode=X_250604_MedicaidCut_actrs [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_med_email-25q2?refcode=X_250604_MedicaidCut_actrs]
Their plan? Fewer jobs, higher premiums, and more crowded ERs because people can’t get care when they need it.
And for what? To line the pockets of wealthy donors.
We won’t let that happen. That’s why Maxine and the Democratic Doctors Caucus are fighting back.
While Republicans sell out your healthcare, Maxine is working to strengthen it. She is one of six licensed medical doctors defending our healthcare in the House.
As a doctor, she sees firsthand how barriers to access harm patients and communities. Her vision is simple: that every American can afford the high-quality care they deserve..
But to get this done and make the changes stick, we need to show the Republicans the door.
Your care should be in the hands of doctors, not GOP donors. Let’s elect leaders who believe in science, stand with families, and fight for your health. Will you support Maxine in this fight by chipping in now? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_med_email-25q2?refcode=X_250604_MedicaidCut_actrs]
DONATE NOW [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_med_email-25q2?refcode=X_250604_MedicaidCut_actrs]
Healthcare should be a right,
Dexter HQ

clean_body: [] Peter, As a doctor, Maxine doesn’t just fight for her patients—she fights on behalf of every American, whether she’s in the hospital or Washington, D.C. Now, the GOP wants to bankroll billionaire tax cuts by ripping health insurance away from millions of Americans. They passed a bill to cut billions from programs that working families rely on—all to fund trillions in tax breaks for the ultra-rich. [] Their plan? Fewer jobs, higher premiums, and more crowded ERs because people can’t get care when they need it. And for what? To line the pockets of wealthy donors. We won’t let that happen. That’s why Maxine and the Democratic Doctors Caucus are fighting back. While Republicans sell out your healthcare, Maxine is working to strengthen it. She is one of six licensed medical doctors defending our healthcare in the House. As a doctor, she sees firsthand how barriers to access harm patients and communities. Her vision is simple: that every American can afford the high-quality care they deserve.. But to get this done and make the changes stick, we need to show the Republicans the door. Your care should be in the hands of doctors, not GOP donors. Let’s elect leaders who believe in science, stand with families, and fight for your health. A simple question to clarify the aims of higher education in the United States

| |
---|---|---
| | | Forwarded this email? [Subscribe here](https://substack..dcgTHbgeGThWQ11RTrqV8OxKugQ-GEBnAYj-Q41-tBg?) for more

# [Should We Educate Our Enemies?](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1661999&post_id=165219070&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY1MjE5MDcwLCJpYXQiOjE3NDkwNzgxMDAsImV4cCI6MTc1MTY3MDEwMCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTE2NjE5OTkiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.zDHNZFQp73kEvL0ruCgVd4oIXDrsskv0gWT2rhvb_-E)
### A simple question to clarify the aims of higher education in the United States
| | [Vikram Mansharamani](https://substack.com/@mansharamani)
---
| Jun 4
---
| [](https://substack.com/@mansharamani)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1661999&post_id=165219070&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY1MjE5MDcwLCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc0OTA3ODEwMCwiZXhwIjoxNzUxNjcwMTAwLCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMTY2MTk5OSIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.yEVtVqGE-hRQfyGapIFbHGaAofUh7yUisuL4updOWPM&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1661999&post_id=165219070&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY1MjE5MDcwLCJpYXQiOjE3NDkwNzgxMDAsImV4cCI6MTc1MTY3MDEwMCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTE2NjE5OTkiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.zDHNZFQp73kEvL0ruCgVd4oIXDrsskv0gWT2rhvb_-E&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1661999&post_id=165219070&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY1MjE5MDcwLCJpYXQiOjE3NDkwNzgxMDAsImV4cCI6MTc1MTY3MDEwMCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTE2NjE5OTkiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.zDHNZFQp73kEvL0ruCgVd4oIXDrsskv0gWT2rhvb_-E)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9vcGVuLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9wdWIvbWFuc2hhcmFtYW5pL3Avc2hvdWxkLXdlLWVkdWNhdGUtb3VyLWVuZW1pZXM_.rKZVMPF07tW8L0EldG5oFBEAowsEyTe-SdDH8FicqMw?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/mansharamani/p/should-we-educate-our-enemies?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

| [](https://substack.com/redirect/756f217b-06d8-4a11-947e-7b3088d36e3e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
It was a surreal moment...while Harvard's graduation [ceremony](https://substack.com/redirect/58bef4a5-df8a-496a-beac-c3484957e417?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) was underway last week, the Trump Administration's effort to ban the school from enrolling international students was making its way through the courts. Alan Garber, the University's President, took aim at this policy during his opening address when he welcomed graduating students from "[around the world, ](https://substack.com/redirect/2fdab70f-d8b7-40b4-a633-e6ab081baa05?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**[just as it should be](https://substack.com/redirect/2fdab70f-d8b7-40b4-a633-e6ab081baa05?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).**" The assembled crowd cheered at the reference, and [speaker](https://substack.com/redirect/a2895d2b-a94a-43e3-b1c5-688f95316501?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) after [speaker](https://substack.com/redirect/a282039b-89c6-4a66-ada1-ead1ceff54b7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) reiterated the sentiment that Harvard should be free to educate the world.
And when news [broke](https://substack.com/redirect/5bdc47cd-710b-4ebb-8b9f-20ec6ae0c0db?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that a judge had issued a preliminary injunction against the Trump attempt to ban foreign students, the _New York Times_ [reported](https://substack.com/redirect/07c2bc2b-cc7b-43b2-82d3-66ec4c8d1773?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that celebratory reactions rippled through the crowd.
[](https://substack.com/redirect/e14dd117-8426-4393-8c25-8e55990315f4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
The effort to ban international students isn't an isolated incident; the Trump Administration is fighting on several [fronts](https://substack.com/redirect/e2766175-d51f-4fbd-94a0-96280b3b0f27?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). There are also the investigations into antisemitism on campus, Harvard's ties to foreign governments, and whether it discriminates based on sex or race. There are also attempts to freeze billions in research funds and government contracts and threats to its tax-exempt status.
But it's the international student issue that I find the most interesting. Ignoring for a moment the wider culture war that is being waged and looking at this as a standalone issue, it's worth noting there are powerful arguments to be made both for and against the attempted ban on foreign student enrollment at Harvard.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..1vb0sH9aQs5mVOIwNqWIuNgoA6iZH7hz7W1Z6_EEADE?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=165219070)
The obvious argument in favor of international students is based on the global competition for talent. As a business owner who is constantly seeking capable talent, I'm all for growing the pool of available professionals for American businesses. Do we want the world's best minds and most motivated students going elsewhere? That's an easy question to answer. Of course we want them coming to the United States, conducting research here, contributing to our knowledge-based economy, even starting businesses. This is especially important when it comes to the STEM workforce in America, where [43 percent](https://substack.com/redirect/d95fa945-399a-4512-bff0-ebc65b795193?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) of PhDs are foreign-born. Do we really want them studying in and contributing to the development of other countries and economies? As one European friend recently told me, energized by the opportunity, "Fine. You don't want them? We'll take them!"
But what about the students who don't stay and take their learnings and newfound research skills home? There's certainly nothing wrong with a student wanting to go back home and improve one's country. That is a worthy and noble undertaking, and over the course of my business and academic careers I have certainly known many who educated themselves with the sole aim of lifting their countrymen up. And there's something our universities gain by having different perspectives on campus.
But not all of these students are returning home to friendly countries; some are from regions that are not only hostile to the U.S. but antithetical to our entire way of life. Some come from countries that are actively seeking to displace the Western values such as liberty, freedom, and the protection of individual rights. **Should we educate our enemies?**
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/522d15dc-7fa3-40a7-8ff9-71c69015d5e2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
To this point, a recent _Wall Street Journal_ [article](https://substack.com/redirect/c2a9c824-7086-4d40-b201-9ec85034ff33?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) made a stunning revelation: over the course of decades, China has sent thousands of its most promising bureaucrats to U.S. colleges and universities to pursue postgraduate studies. Harvard is a preferred destination; so much so in fact, that the Chinese Communist Party refers to it as their top "party school." (_I remember people thought of Tulane as a top party school!_) Graduates often attain senior bureaucratic roles; some have made it to the party's Politburo. Should America really be educating the leaders of a government that **36 years ago today** attacked their own people and today may be conducting a genocide against Muslims within their borders?
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..1vb0sH9aQs5mVOIwNqWIuNgoA6iZH7hz7W1Z6_EEADE?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=165219070)
Years ago, I had a friendly debate with the chief investment officer of one of America's largest foundations, a debate that seems eerily relevant today. Knowing that I had studied geopolitics and taught business ethics, he what he believed was a tough question: "What's the best way to ethically invest in China?"
My answer was simple: "You can't."
"That's not true," he claimed. "Surely healthcare is not a political issue, shouldn't we be willing to improve access to drugs or medical care for the ordinary Chinese people? Or what about providing education to the Chinese people?" We went on to have a fruitful conversation about the Chinese people vs. the CCP and the differences between the two groups.
I asked him one question which seemed to crystalize our differences. "Would you have been willing to invest in access to drugs and medical care in Nazi Germany in the mid to late 1930s? Would you have educated Nazi leadership in economics or chemistry?" He didn't think the analogy between the CCP and Nazis was valid (despite my noting the treatment of Uyghurs), and we ultimately agreed to disagree, conceding that "time will tell."
So, I'll ask the question again, this time to a boarder group: **Should we be educating our enemies?** Please vote in the poll below to see the results.
| | POLL
---
Should we be educating our enemies?
| [Yes, education is not political](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9tYW5zaGFyYW1hbmkuc3Vic3RhY2suY29tL2FwaS92MS9wb2xsL3ZvdGUvZW1haWw_.2F0XLMj5jMiZWH0H4o8FZZvyEDOW6R7moZwojLdX3EM?)
---
[No, we're strengthening adversaries](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9tYW5zaGFyYW1hbmkuc3Vic3RhY2suY29tL2FwaS92MS9wb2xsL3ZvdGUvZW1haWw_.nKpjFxNTF6AvtvBTwv8n8ePpXRvb1d7Zf7A3He4icXc?)

Vikram lives in Lincoln, New Hampshire with his wife and two children, where they can usually be found hiking or skiing.

clean_body: A simple question to clarify the aims of higher education in the United States | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more ? A simple question to clarify the aims of higher education in the United States | | | Jun 4 | | | | | | | | | | | | | | ---|---|--- It was a surreal moment...while Harvard's graduation was underway last week, the Trump Administration's effort to ban the school from enrolling international students was making its way through the courts. Alan Garber, the University's President, took aim at this policy during his opening address when he welcomed graduating students from "." The assembled crowd cheered at the reference, and after reiterated the sentiment that Harvard should be free to educate the world. And when news that a judge had issued a preliminary injunction against the Trump attempt to ban foreign students, the New York Times that celebratory reactions rippled through the crowd. The effort to ban international students isn't an isolated incident; the Trump Administration is fighting on several . There are also the investigations into antisemitism on campus, Harvard's ties to foreign governments, and whether it discriminates based on sex or race. There are also attempts to freeze billions in research funds and government contracts and threats to its tax-exempt status. But it's the international student issue that I find the most interesting. Ignoring for a moment the wider culture war that is being waged and looking at this as a standalone issue, it's worth noting there are powerful arguments to be made both for and against the attempted ban on foreign student enrollment at Harvard. The obvious argument in favor of international students is based on the global competition for talent. As a business owner who is constantly seeking capable talent, I'm all for growing the pool of available professionals for American businesses. Do we want the world's best minds and most motivated students going elsewhere? That's an easy question to answer. Of course we want them coming to the United States, conducting research here, contributing to our knowledge-based economy, even starting businesses. This is especially important when it comes to the STEM workforce in America, where of PhDs are foreign-born. Do we really want them studying in and contributing to the development of other countries and economies? As one European friend recently told me, energized by the opportunity, "Fine. You don't want them? We'll take them!" But what about the students who don't stay and take their learnings and newfound research skills home? There's certainly nothing wrong with a student wanting to go back home and improve one's country. That is a worthy and noble undertaking, and over the course of my business and academic careers I have certainly known many who educated themselves with the sole aim of lifting their countrymen up. And there's something our universities gain by having different perspectives on campus. But not all of these students are returning home to friendly countries; some are from regions that are not only hostile to the U.S. but antithetical to our entire way of life. Some come from countries that are actively seeking to displace the Western values such as liberty, freedom, and the protection of individual rights. Should we educate our enemies? | | ---|---|--- To this point, a recent Wall Street Journal made a stunning revelation: over the course of decades, China has sent thousands of its most promising bureaucrats to U.S. colleges and universities to pursue postgraduate studies. Harvard is a preferred destination; so much so in fact, that the Chinese Communist Party refers to it as their top "party school." (I remember people thought of Tulane as a top party school!) Graduates often attain senior bureaucratic roles; some have made it to the party's Politburo. Should America really be educating the leaders of a government that 36 years ago today attacked their own people and today may be conducting a genocide against Muslims within their borders? Years ago, I had a friendly debate with the chief investment officer of one of America's largest foundations, a debate that seems eerily relevant today. Knowing that I had studied geopolitics and taught business ethics, he what he believed was a tough question: "What's the best way to ethically invest in China?" My answer was simple: "You can't." "That's not true," he claimed. "Surely healthcare is not a political issue, shouldn't we be willing to improve access to drugs or medical care for the ordinary Chinese people? Or what about providing education to the Chinese people?" We went on to have a fruitful conversation about the Chinese people vs. the CCP and the differences between the two groups. I asked him one question which seemed to crystalize our differences. "Would you have been willing to invest in access to drugs and medical care in Nazi Germany in the mid to late 1930s? Would you have educated Nazi leadership in economics or chemistry?" He didn't think the analogy between the CCP and Nazis was valid (despite my noting the treatment of Uyghurs), and we ultimately agreed to disagree, conceding that "time will tell." So, I'll ask the question again, this time to a boarder group: Should we be educating our enemies? Please vote in the poll below to see the results. | | POLL Should we be educating our enemies? | ? ? | | ---|---|--- VIKRAM MANSHARAMANI is an entrepreneur, consultant, scholar, neighbor, husband, father, volunteer, and professional generalist who thinks in multiple-dimensions and looks beyond the short-term. Self-taught to think around corners and connect original dots, he spends his time speaking with global leaders in business, government, academia, and journalism. He 's currently the Chairman and CEO of Goodwell Foods, a manufacturer of private label frozen pizza. LinkedIn has twice listed him as its #1 Top Voice in Money & Finance, and Worth profiled him as one of the 100 Most Powerful People in Global Finance. Vikram earned a PhD From MIT, has taught at Yale and Harvard, and is the author of three books, , and . Vikram lives in Lincoln, New Hampshire with his wife and two children, where they can usually be found hiking or skiing. Last month, House Republicans jammed through Trump’s dangerous budget – **passing one of the largest Medicaid cuts in history that could cause millions to lose their coverage!**

Then, at a town hall last Friday, Republican Joni Ernst responded to terrified Iowans with a chilling, mocking line: “we’re all going to die.”

In Ernst’s own state, **40% of the people who are enrolled in Medicaid are children, 42% live in rural areas, and 50% are nursing home residents.**

These aren’t just numbers, _they’re lives on the line._

New Dems are fighting the GOP’s callous and cruel agenda, but it’s going to take a lot of people power to hold MAGA extremists like Ernst accountable.

name: Powered By People
subject: Republicans are putting religion in our schools
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Powered by People, [poweredxpeople
body:
Instead of prioritizing real solutions like better resources or higher teacher pay, Republican leaders are focused on pushing an agenda that imposes their own worldview onto students.
#
The Texas Legislature just passed a bill requiring the Ten Commandments to be displayed in public school classrooms.
Senate Bill 10 now heads to Greg Abbott’s desk, where he is expected to sign it into law. At the start of the 2025-26 school year, our students will be in classrooms that ignore the constitutional principle of separation of church and state.
Texas public schools are already facing serious challenges — underfunded and understaffed. But instead of addressing these issues, like securing better resources or raising teacher pay, Republican leaders are prioritizing their own political agendas, leaving our kids behind.
They’ve attacked LGBTQ+ students, banned books, and now they’re mandating religious texts in our classrooms. **It’s unconstitutional and un-American.**
As State Rep. James Talarico pointed out, Republicans claim it’s the parents' role to teach things like empathy, kindness, and basic sex education to their children.
_So why, then, is it the state's role to impose a specific religious doctrine in our classrooms?_
This is exactly why Powered by People is organizing year-round in Texas. While Republican officials are using schools as a platform for their self-serving agendas, we’re focused on the real issues — fixing our schools and ensuring our kids get the education they deserve. But we cannot do it without your help.
[**Will you donate $3 today to help us keep fighting for our public schools and a government that works for all Texans?**](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20250604.OyNLiS&refcode=ema20250604-240135&t=1&akid=240135%2E444211%2ErQ84cQ)

clean_body: Instead of prioritizing real solutions like better resources or higher teacher pay, Republican leaders are focused on pushing an agenda that imposes their own worldview onto students. The Texas Legislature just passed a bill requiring the Ten Commandments to be displayed in public school classrooms. Senate Bill 10 now heads to Greg Abbott’s desk, where he is expected to sign it into law. At the start of the 2025-26 school year, our students will be in classrooms that ignore the constitutional principle of separation of church and state. Texas public schools are already facing serious challenges — underfunded and understaffed. But instead of addressing these issues, like securing better resources or raising teacher pay, Republican leaders are prioritizing their own political agendas, leaving our kids behind. They’ve attacked LGBTQ+ students, banned books, and now they’re mandating religious texts in our classrooms. It’s unconstitutional and un-American. As State Rep. James Talarico pointed out, Republicans claim it’s the parents' role to teach things like empathy, kindness, and basic sex education to their children. So why, then, is it the state's role to impose a specific religious doctrine in our classrooms? This is exactly why Powered by People is organizing year-round in Texas. While Republican officials are using schools as a platform for their self-serving agendas, we’re focused on the real issues — fixing our schools and ensuring our kids get the education they deserve. But we cannot do it without your help. Thank you, Powered by People Paid for by Powered by People, , not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee.

name: Gretchen Whitmer
subject: We can do a hell of a lot better, Peter.
body:
It's time to take care of business |
---
[](https://click.everyaction.com/k/108992112/549658225/-1686146655?sourceid=1056329&refcode=WUEFE608_20250604_WhitmerEOMRS_ALL&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzYjdjMWVhNC05NzQxLWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=lQhVgaHlUNMmPkGct0FwLTZvm5pG29t-McRbdKrg6zA=) Peter,

It's Gretchen Whitmer.

When I reached out to you last year, the 2024 election had just shown us how deeply divided Americans are about our vision for our country. Today, we're seeing health care and basic necessities being ripped away from the people who need it most.

Democrats have a clear to-do list. We need to get organized, we need to get energized, and we need to show up for the people who rely on us to make a real difference in their lives.

It's time to take care of business, Peter, which, in Democratic politics, means one thing — it's time to look to EMILYs List. **My friends at EMILYs List have been helping Democratic pro-choice women win elections for 40 years and counting. Their role in changing the face of power in American politics can't be overstated, and I'm proud to be an EMILYs List woman myself.[Their work is entirely donor-supported. Will you help power EMILYs List with a donation of any amount today?](https://click.everyaction.com/k/108992113/549658226/-1686146655?sourceid=1056329&refcode=WUEFE608_20250604_WhitmerEOMRS_ALL&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzYjdjMWVhNC05NzQxLWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=lQhVgaHlUNMmPkGct0FwLTZvm5pG29t-McRbdKrg6zA=)** | **[ELECT WOMEN](https://click.everyaction.com/k/108992114/549658227/-1686146655?sourceid=1056329&refcode=WUEFE608_20250604_WhitmerEOMRS_ALL&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzYjdjMWVhNC05NzQxLWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=lQhVgaHlUNMmPkGct0FwLTZvm5pG29t-McRbdKrg6zA=)**
---
In times of upheaval, we need to look to what has always been true. And here's what I know for sure: More freedom is better than less. Some rights, like the right to make our own medical decisions, are nonnegotiable. And when more women are at the decision-making table, we all win.

I was thrilled to see that the very first 2025 endorsements EMILYs List made were for women running for governor. I'm here to tell you firsthand, Peter: **We need more women in charge of our states**.

Republicans haven't taken a day off from dismantling our democracy. So neither can we, in our work to get _every last one of them out_. **There are EMILYs List women on the ballot as soon as next month** , and the momentum from these elections will set the tone for midterms in 2026. Taking back power starts right here.

**[Will you donate to EMILYs List, Peter? I've seen firsthand what a difference this community can make for women running for office everywhere. Together, we can get this done.](https://click.everyaction.com/k/108992115/549658228/-1686146655?sourceid=1056329&refcode=WUEFE608_20250604_WhitmerEOMRS_ALL&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzYjdjMWVhNC05NzQxLWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=lQhVgaHlUNMmPkGct0FwLTZvm5pG29t-McRbdKrg6zA=)**

clean_body: It's time to take care of business | = Peter, It's Gretchen Whitmer. When I reached out to you last year, the 2024 election had just shown us how deeply divided Americans are about our vision for our country. Today, we're seeing health care and basic necessities being ripped away from the people who need it most. Democrats have a clear to-do list. We need to get organized, we need to get energized, and we need to show up for the people who rely on us to make a real difference in their lives. It's time to take care of business, Peter, which, in Democratic politics, means one thing — it's time to look to EMILYs List. My friends at EMILYs List have been helping Democratic pro-choice women win elections for 40 years and counting. Their role in changing the face of power in American politics can't be overstated, and I'm proud to be an EMILYs List woman myself.= | = In times of upheaval, we need to look to what has always been true. And here's what I know for sure: More freedom is better than less. Some rights, like the right to make our own medical decisions, are nonnegotiable. And when more women are at the decision-making table, we all win. I was thrilled to see that the very first 2025 endorsements EMILYs List made were for women running for governor. I'm here to tell you firsthand, Peter: We need more women in charge of our states. Republicans haven't taken a day off from dismantling our democracy. So neither can we, in our work to get every last one of them out. There are EMILYs List women on the ballot as soon as next month , and the momentum from these elections will set the tone for midterms in 2026. Taking back power starts right here. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue, your donation will go through immediately: = | = ---|--- = | = = | = Thanks for stepping up, Gretchen = Gov. Gretchen Whitmer = | | | | ---|---|---|---|--- Thank you for being a supporter of EMILYs List. This email was sent to: dpwillis67@gmail.com. Inbox overcrowded? Sign up to get less email . name: Justice Democrats
subject: Summer torches Trump’s Education Secretary
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS
body:
[1]Justice Democrats

Peter,

Summer Lee just got done grilling Trump’s Education Secretary, Linda
McMahon, on the administration’s assault on our public education and
attempt to erase Black history from our textbooks... humiliating is an
understatement.

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-em-20250604-sl?refcode=em20250604-36734&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&refcode2=36734_1203867_9aN5kc&akid=36734%2E1203867%2E9aN5kc ]Screenshot of a post on X by Acyn (@Acyn) showing a transcript of a
congressional exchange. The text reads: 'Lee: Would say it would be
“illegal DEI” for a lesson plan on the Tulsa race massacre? McMahon: I’d
have to get back to you. Lee: Do you know what the Tulsa race massacre is?
McMahon: I’d like to look into it more and get back to you on it. Lee: How
about the book from Ruby...' Below the post is a video still showing Rep.
Summer Lee seated at a congressional hearing.

Today, in the Education & Workforce Committee that Summer sits on,
bumbling Secretary of Education Linda McMahon, best known for her work in
the World Wrestling Federation, came to explain the Education Department’s
“priorities” under Donald Trump.

But we already know what those priorities are: defunding and shutting down
the Department of Education.

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-em-20250604-sl?refcode=em20250604-36734&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&refcode2=36734_1203867_9aN5kc&akid=36734%2E1203867%2E9aN5kc ]If you stand with Summer to defend our public education, split a
donation right now — $5, $15, whatever you can — to help us keep her in
office and keep fighting back against this administration!

Summer wasn’t going to let Secretary McMahon come to Congress without
exposing her lies and holding this administration accountable for their
war on public education.

While this right-wing, extremist Dept. of Education tries to ban anything
they consider “illegal DEI,” Summer asked McMahon what exactly that would
include. Here are some of the things that Linda McMahon couldn’t confirm
or deny are “illegal DEI:”

* Joe Biden winning the 2020 election
* The Tulsa race massacre
* Ruby Bridges, the first Black child to integrate public schools in the
South

In fact, Secretary McMahon didn’t even know who Ruby Bridges is or what
the Tulsa race massacre was! Seems like we should be funding public
education after all, and Secretary McMahon should be the first person in
the classroom — and the last person near our Dept. of Education.

While too many corporate, do-nothing Democrats have been capitulating to
Republicans to appease their right-wing donors, Justice Democrats like
Summer are only fighting for their constituents and community. Now more
than ever, we need strong progressives like Summer to lead the Democratic
Party against the Trump administration and towards a better future for us
all.

name: James Carville
subject: This race will be a bellwether for the entire country
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Heartland Patriots (LucasKunce
body:
The Virginia Governor’s race isn’t just about the future of Virginia
[](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/89464?refcode=em20250604-8984&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=8984%2E614464%2E555ABa)
Peter,
I’ve seen a lot of tough races in my time, but let me tell you: **this one is as important as it gets.**
The Virginia Governor’s race isn’t just about the future of Virginia – it's a bellwether for the entire country. This will be the first major election after Trump’s inauguration, and it could set the stage for Democrats in 2026. If we don’t win big here, we’ll be facing a rough road ahead.
**[So, before I continue, can I count on you to split a contribution between Abigail Spanberger’s campaign for Governor of Virginia and Heartland Patriots?](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/89464?refcode=em20250604-8984&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=2&akid=8984%2E614464%2E555ABa)**
**[CONTRIBUTE](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/89464?refcode=em20250604-8984&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=3&akid=8984%2E614464%2E555ABa)**
Here’s what’s got me terrified:
The _Cook Political Report_ has called this race a TOSS-UP, and I couldn’t agree more. Polls are going to stay tight, and this race is going to be one of the hardest-fought in the nation. Republicans are going to flood the state with millions of dollars through their super PACs, trying to keep the Governor’s seat red. They know this race is everything to them – and we need to match their energy, their money, and their commitment. Remember, this race is the only opportunity to flip a Governor's office from red to blue in 2025.
If we don’t win this one, we’ll be handing Republicans a major victory ahead of 2026, and trust me – we can’t let that happen.
Right now, Democratic Governors are our last line of defense against the reckless policies coming out of the Trump administration, and Abigail Spanberger is the leader Virginia needs at this moment.
That’s why I’m asking you to step up today. We can’t win this without your help. The money that’s coming from the other side is going to be massive, and we need to be able to push back. Every dollar you contribute will make a difference in keeping Virginia blue and in keeping the Democrats on track for victory in 2026.
**[Will you contribute right now to Heartland Patriots and Abigail Spanberger to help Abigail Spanberger win this race and deliver a major victory for Democrats?](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/89464?refcode=em20250604-8984&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=4&akid=8984%2E614464%2E555ABa)**
If you've stored your info with ActBlue Express, we'll process your contribution instantly:
[Contribute $10](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/89465?refcode=em20250604-8984&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=10&t=5&akid=8984%2E614464%2E555ABa) [Contribute $25](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/89465?refcode=em20250604-8984&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=25&t=6&akid=8984%2E614464%2E555ABa) [Contribute $30](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/89465?refcode=em20250604-8984&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=30&t=7&akid=8984%2E614464%2E555ABa) [Contribute $50](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/89465?refcode=em20250604-8984&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=50&t=8&akid=8984%2E614464%2E555ABa) [Contribute $100](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/89465?refcode=em20250604-8984&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=100&t=9&akid=8984%2E614464%2E555ABa) [Contribute $250](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/89465?refcode=em20250604-8984&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=250&t=10&akid=8984%2E614464%2E555ABa) [Give another amount](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/89464?refcode=em20250604-8984&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=11&akid=8984%2E614464%2E555ABa)
---
Thank you,
James Carville

name: Jena for CO
subject: The 19th Amendment was just the beginning
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jena for Colorado
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2506-q2-jg?refcode=250604-email [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2506-q2-jg?refcode=250604-email]
Friend,
105 years ago today, Congress passed the 19th Amendment, which after being ratified over a year later, granted some women the constitutional right to vote. The passing of the 19th amendment was a historic step forward, and it took decades of activism and struggle, but it wasn’t the end of the fight for equal access to the ballot—and it still isn’t.
As Secretary of State, Jena has worked tirelessly to protect the right to vote and expand access to democracy for every voter—Republican, Democrat, and Unaffiliated alike.
Now, she’s running for Attorney General to keep fighting back against the attacks on our rights and freedoms—from voter suppression to MAGA extremism.
Will you chip in $19—or whatever you can—to help us honor this anniversary and continue the fight for justice and equality? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2506-q2-jg?refcode=250604-email&amount=19]
Jena has:
→ Implemented automatic voter registration
→ Guaranteed voting access on tribal lands and public college campuses
→ Protected election workers from threats and intimidation
→ Stood up to Donald Trump and his efforts to undermine our democracy
But the same forces that once tried to silence women’s voices are still working today to chip away at our rights.
Jena is running for Attorney General to continue being a force against the Trump Administration’s attack on our rights, and we need your support. Rush a donation of $19 today to show you stand with Jena. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2506-q2-jg?refcode=250604-email&amount=19]
Thanks for being part of this fight. We can’t do it without you.
Jena for CO
Follow Jena Online:
https://www.facebook.com/Jenaforcolorado/ [https://www.facebook.com/Jenaforcolorado/] https://twitter.com/JenaGriswold [https://twitter.com/JenaGriswold] https://www.instagram.com/jenagriswold/ [https://www.instagram.com/jenagriswold/] https://www.threads.net/@jenagriswold [https://www.threads.net/@jenagriswold] https://bsky.app/profile/jenagriswold.bsky.social [https://bsky.app/profile/jenagriswold.bsky.social]
Paid for by Jena for Colorado. Rachel Gordon, Registered Agent.

Louisville, CO 80027

name: John Fetterman
subject: Medicaid is on the chopping block
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Fetterman for PA
body:
[ https://act.johnfetterman.com/go/50678?t=1001&akid=10120%2E434096%2EBd7lxt ]John Fetterman

Derek, it’s John. I know you're busy, so I'll keep this quick:

🛑 Republicans want to give tax cuts to billionaires, so they’re trying to pass a budget that would gut Medicaid. 🛑

We’re talking about a health insurance program that covers 3 million people in PA alone – that’s well over a third of kids and more than half of nursing home residents. No bullsh*t – this is life-or-death stuff here.

So I need to know what you think, Derek. Will you please take my Medicaid survey now? It’ll only take like 60 seconds:

https://act.johnfetterman.com/go/51216?email=&zip=&t=1002&akid=10120%2E434096%2EBd7lxt

I’ve always been a hard NO on this GOP budget. They wanna cut $1.5 trillion in federal spending to give their billionaire cronies a tax cut, which basically *guarantees* cuts to Medicaid.

I'm not playing political games; that’s just the math.

And it'll hurt working folks in PA and across the country – millions of people, especially in rural areas, use Medicaid to see an urgent care doctor or just fill their kid’s asthma medication!

So we gotta put a real fight against these cuts *now* – before it gets to a vote – and I need to know yinz are with me. Please click here to take the Medicaid survey now.

https://act.johnfetterman.com/go/51216?email=&zip=&t=1003&akid=10120%2E434096%2EBd7lxt

Thanks for your help,

John ❤️

In 2022, he made history to become the first member of Gen Z elected to Congress. Maxwell isn't taking any corporate PAC, big sugar, or fossil fuel contributions. He needs your help to protect this seat for poor and working-class people. Will you join our historic movement today? | ~~ ~~ ~~ ~~ ~~ | PAID FOR BY MAXWELL FROST FOR CONGRESS Maxwell Frost for Congress PO Box 772671 Orlando, FL 32877-2671 United States | Email is an important way for us to communicate with our supporters. body:
|
# [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/108992998/549661318/-664592562?refcode=em-header&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS84OTkxNCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhZWU0YWIzOS05ZDQxLWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=L1rg5Hl4EjrY56UwWrmo2gqia9-GskYaVssZRrjFMqg=)
**➤➤****BREAKING: Senator Ernst dismisses Medicaid concerns with
“We're all going to die”, then DOUBLES DOWN** [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/108992999/549661319/-209663064?refcode=em-6.04.25-all-a&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS84OTkxNCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhZWU0YWIzOS05ZDQxLWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=L1rg5Hl4EjrY56UwWrmo2gqia9-GskYaVssZRrjFMqg=)
* * *
This is beyond shameful, friend. At a recent town hall in Iowa, Senator Joni Ernst faced constituents worried about proposed cuts to Medicaid under the "One Big Beautiful Bill." When a concerned citizen warned that people could die due to these cuts, all Ernst could say was: **_"Well, we're all going to die."_** And when she got backlash, she didn’t walk it back... Instead, she doubled down in an Instagram story filmed _in a graveyard_ , smirking and sarcastically “apologizing” for her remarks. **This is what the Republican Party has become: “leaders” who treat people’s lives — their access to Medicaid, cancer screenings, prescriptions – as punchlines for their base.** Ernst isn’t alone. Trump and MAGA Republicans in Congress are pushing brutal healthcare cuts, then mocking the people those cuts will hurt. They’re doing it shamelessly, proudly, and publicly. **That’s why we need leaders who will fight back** – and why Democratic Secretaries of State are more important than ever. In red and purple states, they are some of the last statewide officials standing up for basic dignity, civil rights, and accountability. At DASS, we’re not just electing Democrats to count votes – we’re building a firewall against MAGA cruelty at every level of government. But we can’t do it without you. [**Your support is crucial. Will you chip in $3 right now to help us stop the cruelty and fight for a government that respects the people it serves?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/108993000/549661320/-209663064?refcode=em-6.04.25-all-a&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS84OTkxNCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhZWU0YWIzOS05ZDQxLWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=L1rg5Hl4EjrY56UwWrmo2gqia9-GskYaVssZRrjFMqg=) __If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: __ | | | **[Chip in $10 Now](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/108993001/549661321/-209663064?refcode=em-6.04.25-all-a&express_lane=true&amount=10&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS84OTkxNCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhZWU0YWIzOS05ZDQxLWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=L1rg5Hl4EjrY56UwWrmo2gqia9-GskYaVssZRrjFMqg=)**

Derek, Earlier this week, we announced that one grassroots supporter and a guest will get to meet President Obama later this summer. And the response was incredible! Take a look: [](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/meet-obama-sweepstakes-2025?refcode=em_20250604_3640_fr&amount=3&refcode2=3660_22930333_xHSRaY&akid=3660%2E22930333%2ExHSRaY) Since we announced this contest, donations have _skyrocketed!_ Each dot on the map represents someone pitching in not only for their chance to meet President Obama, but to support the DNC’s work organizing around the country. Now’s your chance to join them. **[Make a contribution of $3, or any amount that feels right today, and you'll be entered into a chance to meet President Obama (travel and hotel expenses paid!) while supporting the DNC’s work to elect Democrats nationwide.](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/meet-obama-sweepstakes-2025?refcode=em_20250604_3640_fr&amount=3&refcode2=3660_22930333_xHSRaY&akid=3660%2E22930333%2ExHSRaY)**

name: The Real Texas PAC
subject: Trump just attacked ActBlue. Fight back now!!!!
body:
Peter, what Trump just did should terrify every single person who believes in democracy.
He issued an Executive Order weaponizing the Department of Justice to go after ActBlue — the small-dollar fundraising platform that powers thousands of Democratic campaigns across the country.
Why? Because he’s terrified of us.
He knows grassroots Democrats are his biggest threat — and he’s trying to meddle with our ability to fight back before the election even begins.
This is how authoritarians seize power:
1️⃣ They discredit elections.
2️⃣ They silence the media.
3️⃣ And then — they come for the money.
Trump is officially coming for the money. This is an all hands on deck moment.
This is a targeted, strategic assault on the tools we use to organize, fundraise, and win. We MUST overwhelm him with IMMEDIATE resistance. Rush a donation right now to defend ActBlue, defend grassroots power, and defend our democracy: [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-abeo?refcode=052125_1]

Married couples may together give a total of $10,000 per calendar year. Federal political action committees (PACs) can contribute up to $5,000 per calendar year. The first $5,000 contributed in a calendar year from federally permissible sources will be deposited in The Real Texas PAC's contribution account. Once a federally permissible source has contributed $5,000 in a calendar year, The Real Texas PAC will transfer any amounts in excess of $5,000 to its non-contribution account. Contributions from corporations, labor organizations, treasury funds, and federal government contractors are prohibited. Only U.S. citizens or legal permanent residents are eligible to contribute. An Individual can contribute up to $5,000 to The Real Texas PAC per calendar year. Married couples may together give a total of $10,000 per calendar year. Federal political action committees (PACs) can contribute up to $5,000 per calendar year. The first $5,000 contributed in a calendar year from federally permissible sources will be deposited in The Real Texas PAC's contribution account. Once a federally permissible source has contributed $5,000 in a calendar year, The Real Texas PAC will transfer any amounts in excess of $5,000 to its non-contribution account. name: VoteVets
subject: That’s Billion, with a B
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY VOTEVETS, VOTEVETS
body:
#
Peter, at this point it just sounds like we’re making things up. But what we’re about to tell you is true — and insane.
You know that plane? The one Trump shook down Qatar for? The $400 million luxury jet?
The Department of Defense is going to spend **$1 BILLION** to retrofit that thing and make it meet the standards required of Air Force One.
Yeah, that’s **_Billion.**_ Capital B.
Now, guess what? That’s not the worst part.
**$1 BILLION** was just redirected away from Army barracks maintenance and refurbishment. Yeah, the same amount we’re going to waste on Trump’s free flights to Mar a Lago.
That’s not all. Recent estimates are that nearly 800,000 Veterans are either living unhoused or paying more than half their income for housing. And in their Big Beautiful Bill, they’re cutting $26 billion in rental assistance. Who knows how many Veterans are going to be forced out of their housing to pay for ridiculous tax cuts for billionaires?
Trump isn’t just screwing working families and the middle class. He’s hitting our Active Duty, too. He’s pulling the rug out from underneath Veterans. And the entire Republican Party is helping him do it.
Whether it’s this parade nobody wants, attacks on health care, or cuts to housing — this is an all out assault. We have to pull out all the stops to combat Trump and this extreme MAGA Congress if we’re going to stop the madness.
[**VoteVets, as always, is the tip of the spear in this fight. And if you can help, please: Make a donation and keep us going.**](https://act.votevets.org/go/2927?c0=vv-pac-em-june25.e5pwgQ&refcode=em250604-1-120&t=1&akid=34435%2E3013755%2EYz_0lk)

I’m ready to get to work to protect reproductive freedom, fight crime, expand family leave and Pre-K, improve K-12 education, help parents take care of their aging parents, address the mental health crisis affecting our kids — and so much more. Thank you, Josh | Josh is running to be the next Governor of New Jersey in 2025 – the “Lower Taxes, Lower Costs Governor.” Since 2016, his campaign lawn signs have always said the same thing: “Lower Taxes. Jersey Values” – and that’s what he’s done, from expanding the Child Tax Credit to fighting to restore SALT to clawing dollars back to Jersey for first responders. If you'd like to support his campaign , or to unsubscribe Thank you for being on Team Gottheimer. | | | Prefer to donate by mail?** Make checks payable to Josh Gottheimer for Governor PO Box 201 Hackensack, NJ 07602 PAID FOR BY JOSH GOTTHEIMER FOR GOVERNOR Josh Gottheimer for Governor PO Box 201 Hackensack, NJ 07602 United States
